It looks like you need to setup your Audio in Wine.  Run winecfg from the command line and take a look at the Audio tab.

As for updating, yes, install the game in Windows, let it update, and then copy all of the files from there to the Linux install.  You'd really just have to copy the updated files, but it can be tricky to determine which ones those are.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-05
> **dahli.llama said:**
> Is there a consolidated list of settings for both the game and Wine for this game?  I know I've seen a lot of settings being tossed around, but it is getting quite difficult to track them down in all these pages.  I looked at WineHQ, but it doesn't look like this game has been added to the DB yet.
Look through some of the earlier posts on this thread (especially mine), there are a couple of regedit settings (VideoMemorySize & UseGLSL or something like that) and three definate settings in UserPrefs (StencilShadows=Disabled, VideoPostEffects=False & LandscapeStaticObjectShadows=Disabled).

You'll have to double-check the ones set via regedit because I'm not 100% sure I've given them the right name.

The AppDB for LotRO is a mess at the moment (and in the wrong section), it's on my to-do list for getting corrected.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-05
> **carajean said:**
> err:wave:DSDB_MapBuffer Please run winecfg, open "Audio" page and set
"Hardware Acceleration" to "Emulation".
err:dscapture:widDsCreate DirectSoundCapture flag not set
This sound card's driver does not support direct access
The (slower) DirectSound HEL mode will be used instead.
I'd check your audio settings, try OSS or Alsa and the hardware emulation settings for both (ie try Alsa on emulation, alsa on non-emulation, OSS/emulation then OSS/non-emulation) until you find one that works, note that at present the movies play but with no sound so you need to wait till you hit the character selection screen for noise.

> But other than that I have to install the game to a windows machine and let it update. NOW when I do this do I just overwrite my existing files or what do I have to do to get this up to date and playable??? Im lost here but this thread has been goin well so im gonna stick around a long time and see how it goes.
I just re-copy the entire LotRO directory (from Program Files) after an update, all your config settings are stored elsewhere so you don't have to worry about accidently overwriting them.

### Post by dahli.llama on 2007-06-05
> **mike r said:**
> I did a writeup based on info found in this posting. Got it working on my MEPIS system. Should be pretty much the same for Ubuntu.

It is currently for EU servers, though this thread points how to get it working for US servers.

[http://www.mepislovers.org/forums/showthread.php?t=7958](http://www.mepislovers.org/forums/showthread.php?t=7958)

Just a couple typos:

VideoPostEffects=Disabled  (should be False.  It's a True/False value.)

**TO  EVERYONE ON THE US SERVERS - There has been an update overnight, you will need to run the update from the windows copy or virtual machine and copy the files across**

The UserPreferences.ini should be in the 'My Documents/The Lord of the Rings Online' folder. 
I have Wine setup so that My Documents points to my home folder.  This can be accomplished in winecfg under the desktop integration tab.

In the Userpreferences.ini file you need to change the value *AllowFakeFullScreen=True to False*.  I ran in to trouble also when trying to run it at a resolution below 1024x768.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-06
> **fernando_lopes_jr said:**
> I get this error when I try to run the: 

bash lotrolauncher.script

Does anyone know what's wrong?
That suggests that you do not have write permission on the LotRO directory (and those below it), if you copied via the network then the nominal owner of the directories is probably a user called nobody. To fix this from your base home directory (the one that you start in when you fire up a console) type the following
```
sudo chown -R username .wine
```
Where username is the username you use to login and .wine is the name of the wine directory (.wine is the default). That will ensure that everything in the wine folder and below has your username set as it's owner, you should them be able to run the script without the error messages. 

If you do still get the error messages go to the Codemasters directory (./wine/drive_c/Program*/Codemasters) and do a 
```
ls -l
```

If the entry for the LotRO directory doesn't start drwx then do
```
chmod -R u+rwx The*
```
That gives you read/write & execute access to the folder and everything inside.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-06
I have a possible lead on the patching process. Looking at my captures of an enforced update (copied the file out of backup back into the main folder) and todays update I've found where it fetches the files from.

The first update issued this get from lotro.cdnetworks.us
```
/lotro/patch/2007240590_0705240910/all/lotroclient.exe
```

todays did the following request at lotro.cdnetworks.us
```
/lotro/patch/2007060648_0706061418/all/lotroclient.exe
```

Now I can see some pattern there, 20070606 is the date (YYYYDDMM), ignore 48 for now, the 070606 is also the date (in YYMMDD format), the 1418 seems to be the time.

Going back to the 48, for the first update it was 90 and the time was 0910, for the second it was 48 with the time 1418. Both times the 2nd & 4th digit.

Now all we have to do is find out the date & time of updates as well as which files to check for (I tried a wget * but it wouldn't allow it).

14:35:38 URL:[http://launcher.turbine.com/serverinfo.php?s=206.16.158.125](http://launcher.turbine.com/serverinfo.php?s=206.16.158.125) [916/916] -> ".launcher/server.config" [1]

Connecting to world login queue for realm Firefoot...
14:35:39 URL:[https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php](https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php) [127/127] -> ".launcher/WorldQueue.config" [1]

Error: World login queue response indicates failure.

--------------

UPDATE:
I did some debugging on this because I SO wanted this to work.
I subscribe to both DDO and LOTRO from turbine. The lotrolauncher script didn't select the LOTRO subscription. So, here is the change I made:
Changed:
accountNum=`grep -h "<Name>" .launcher/GLSAuthServer.config | sed -e "s/^.*<Name>//;s/<\/Name>.*$//"`
To:
accountNum=`grep -h "<Name>" .launcher/GLSAuthServer.config | sed -e "s/^.*<Game>LOTRO<\/Game><Name>//;s/<\/Name>.*$//"`

Once I put this in, everything works nicely.

Thanks a lot for all the hard work guys!

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-06-11
> **carajean said:**
> Ok I been tweaking it for some times it did have to do with graphics. Its all fixed now except when im in window mode sometimes i get stuck just running and nothing i do will stop it except hitting random keys.

This is not a bug with the game. By having the application thinking it is running in full screen mode, clicking outside the window whilst holding a key will make the game think that you are continuing to push that button, thus making you run around in circles or what have you. In order to fix this, click back in the window, hold the opposite direction key, then click outside the window again. It works for me, usually.
Selecting "Allow Direct3d apps to stop cursor leaving window" may fix this, but I haven't tried it.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-13
OK a general update now that I have my internet connection back.

I have added a how-to to the [AppDB]("http://appdb.winehq.org/appview.php?iVersionId=8094") page for this (ignore the 1.2 version, there never was a version 1.2 but when I remove it it keeps getting re-added for some reason).

I haven't added the altering of the MemoryUsage option in userprefs because before I lost my connection I was running tests, it seems that if your graphics card has a lot of memory you can lower the setting for better performance but if you have low graphics memory increasing the value helps.

As for the automated updates, I'm working of a system that will check if an update is needed so that it can tell the user (the files that are downloaded are encrypted so decrypting them could be difficult) but a warning system would help.

I have been working on a Mono based launcher (based on SNy's script), it's at a very early stage but when I get it working I will post a link to it (will let you run the game without having to go into the game folder so you can add it to your menu bar and/or have a desktop short cut).

also downloaded the URL encoding script.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-14
> **Mrwasab1 said:**
> there is a lot of information here but i would like to know if anyone has created a step by step tutorial on how to do this?

I have all these things but i have no idea what to do next, where do i put the lotro friles? how do i launch this script because doing a chmod a+x wont work... what about the url encoding script?
I am fairly new to the world of linux so go easy on me. I would really appreciate if someone could tell me step by step how to get this working.

OK if you haven't yet run anything in wine you won't have created your wine system. So start up a console (ie get to the command line) and type
```
winecfg
```
Wait while it creates the wine system for you and you should then see a window with various tabs on it. Check that the windows version is windows 2000 (should be the default but check anyway). Then click on the audio tab, you will get some sort of message about no audio system set up,  ok that and you should see that it has set up either OSS or Alsa, leave that as it is for now. Close winecfg.

Back at the command prompt type regedit, if you have used regedit on a windows box it is identical. Set up the keys listed on the AppDB page (I posted the link a couple of posts up). Exit regedit once that is done.

You now need to get LOTRO onto your linux box, how you do it depends on how your system is setup, mine is dual boot so I can do a straight copy from the windows partition (remember at present you still need windows to perform the updates).

From your home directory (the one you start in when you fire up a terminal), your should be able to
```
cd ./wine/drive_c/Program*
```
That takes you into your wine systems Program Files directory. Copy LOTRO into here (I copied the, in EU case, Codemasters directory which the LOTRO directory lives in).

Once that is copied, put the two script files into the directory that lotroclient.exe lives in. To run you can simply go into the LOTRO directory and type
```
bash ./scriptname
```
where script name is the first script (ie not the urlencode one).

Since this would be the initial run of LOTRO it will bomb out with an error because you need to tweak the UserPreferences.ini file. That file should live in a directory called The Lord of the Rings Online that sits just off your home directory. Again look at the AppDB page for the changes that need to be made.

Hopefully this time when you run the launcher script the game will start and you can happily play, if you get any errors post them here and we'll try to help you.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-14
**Important change for 1.2 - Shores of Everdim**

They have activated an option in the client (lotroclient.exe) launch process that is causing the current script problem.

If you get as far as connecting and then see a screen saying connection to server has been lost, you need to make a small adjustment to the script (note this is a hack fix, a proper fix should arrive soon).

Find the line that reads
```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} $@
```
Change it to read
```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} --HighResOutOfDate $@
```

The reason for this is that in one of the config files (launcher.config), the option GameClient.AlwaysPatchHighRes is now false (it used to be true) so now the additional parameter defined by GameClient.HighResPatchArg has to be passed when lotroclient.exe is ran.

Hopefully SNy will fix the script (better to keep it as one maintained version) soon. On the positive side my gui based launcher is close to being in a fit state to test, so hopefully I will stick a copy for you to test in the near future.

---

### Post by dahli.llama on 2007-06-14
I was wondering if someone could help me out with a quick script.

I want to write a script that will copy only the updated files from one folder to my current game folder.  Basically, my wife's PC runs Windows so I have the game installed on hers, and the updater running.  Then when an update happens, I have to copy the files over to my system.  It takes nearly 2 hours to do this over our network.

What I'd like to have is a script that would compare the files in the two directories (my install and her install), and then only copy the ones that are different.  I have a decent understanding of scripting, but this is evading me.  I could really use some help on the reading of the files from the directories and recursing into the subdirectories.

Of course, this won't help the time a whole lot for big updates where a lot of files change, but I hope that it will make it so that smaller updates don't take as long.

### Post by Mrwasab1 on 2007-06-14
> **ajackson said:**
> OK if you haven't yet run anything in wine you won't have created your wine system. So start up a console (ie get to the command line) and type
```
winecfg
```
Wait while it creates the wine system for you and you should then see a window with various tabs on it. Check that the windows version is windows 2000 (should be the default but check anyway). Then click on the audio tab, you will get some sort of message about no audio system set up,  ok that and you should see that it has set up either OSS or Alsa, leave that as it is for now. Close winecfg.

Back at the command prompt type regedit, if you have used regedit on a windows box it is identical. Set up the keys listed on the AppDB page (I posted the link a couple of posts up). Exit regedit once that is done.

You now need to get LOTRO onto your linux box, how you do it depends on how your system is setup, mine is dual boot so I can do a straight copy from the windows partition (remember at present you still need windows to perform the updates).

From your home directory (the one you start in when you fire up a terminal), your should be able to
```
cd ./wine/drive_c/Program*
```
That takes you into your wine systems Program Files directory. Copy LOTRO into here (I copied the, in EU case, Codemasters directory which the LOTRO directory lives in).

Once that is copied, put the two script files into the directory that lotroclient.exe lives in. To run you can simply go into the LOTRO directory and type
```
bash ./scriptname
```
where script name is the first script (ie not the urlencode one).

Since this would be the initial run of LOTRO it will bomb out with an error because you need to tweak the UserPreferences.ini file. That file should live in a directory called The Lord of the Rings Online that sits just off your home directory. Again look at the AppDB page for the changes that need to be made.

Hopefully this time when you run the launcher script the game will start and you can happily play, if you get any errors post them here and we'll try to help you.

Thanks for the help. I got as far as running winecfg and making those changes.
Then when i open regedit, i dont have a Direct3D folder there. So i created one with the keys...i dont know if i had to do this...
Then i moved on to doing cd ./wine etc...
./wine does not exist in my home drive, or any drive for that matter. Thinking that the install was corrupted, i reinstalled wine, however i still dont have a .wine directory...

what else should i try?

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-15
> **Mrwasab1 said:**
> Then when i open regedit, i dont have a Direct3D folder there. So i created one with the keys...i dont know if i had to do this...
Yeah sorry should have mentioned that part.

> Then i moved on to doing cd ./wine etc...
./wine does not exist in my home drive, or any drive for that matter. Thinking that the install was corrupted, i reinstalled wine, however i still dont have a .wine directory...

what else should i try?
you can try typing
```
locate user.reg
```
That should show you where the user.reg file can be found (it is part of the windows registry). Another thing you can try is
```
echo $WINEPREFIX
```
If it is set it points to the default wine directory.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-15
**Fix for hi-res graphics**
OK reading the LOTRO EU forums to get the hi-res graphics patched you need to start LOTRO (on windows) and log in.

On the character selection screen ensure that your graphics are at least set to very high, you should then see a message about needing to restart LOTRO to patch, if you don't then your version is already patched.

If you get the message, exit LOTRO, restart the launcher (again on windows) and it should then update the hi-res graphics files. No need to log in this time though.

Copy the updated files over to linux and if you edited the lotrolauncher script to append --HighResOutOfDate, then edit it again and remove that addition (it is used by the launcher so not needed by our script).

@dahli.llama
To do a selective copy I think the command you are looking for is **rsync**, that only copies files that are different (and since it is designed for copying over networks I think it checks that the copy worked correctly).

The launcher will probably never get sorted until either Mono works better under wine or somehow you can install .NET (unlikely).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-16
There are two ways I know of checking which graphics driver version you have, first is to try to run nvidia-settings, if it works it shows the driver version. Second is to fire up synaptic and search for nvidia, you should find you have nvidia-glx (maybe nvidia-glx-new) installed, the version installed is the version number.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-18
For the latest deb of wine have a look [here]("http://www.winehq.org/site/download-deb"), it tells you how to get the latest version.

If I remember right (it's been a while since I did it), you have to tweak something with the restricted kernel modules when using nvidia's own installer. I found [this]("http://www.nvnews.net/vbulletin/showthread.php?t=72490") page that tells you to remove them, though doing that you will need to check for new kernel versions yourself as linux-generic gets removed. That is fine if you don't need any of the restricted drivers (others are mainly wifi I think).

If you want to keep the ubuntu drivers and try with a newer wine then try running nvidia-xconfig to re-create your xorg.conf (I think it needs to be ran using sudo, oh and take a back up just in case), that might correct those errors you were getting. As for the beryl stuff, is it still enabled? It might have got disabled when you used the nv driver.

---

Hello to all,

first: Great Work! 

now my problem:
I've followed the instructions given on this board and all worked perfectly well :) I was able to play for about a week.
Until today :(

Yesterday I updated the game files using my windows xp installation, today I updated wine from 0.9.38 to 0.9.39 using teh WineHQ Ubuntu package. 

Now, the game still starts,  but is very slow and after a while crashes.

The error messages are:

lots of:
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders

and at last:
err:ntdll:RtlpWaitForCriticalSection section 0x7e30e520 "x11drv_main.c: X11DRV_CritSection" wait timed out in thread 0010, blocked by 0009, retrying (60 sec)

I tried downgrading wine to 0.9.38 again without success.

I'm running Ubuntu Feisty Fawn, my graphics card is a Nvidia Geforce 7600 GTS.

So, is it possible, that the last Turbine update causes these problems or have I gone "sleepscrewing" my configuration?

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-18
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> 
The error messages are:

lots of:
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders

and at last:
err:ntdll:RtlpWaitForCriticalSection section 0x7e30e520 "x11drv_main.c: X11DRV_CritSection" wait timed out in thread 0010, blocked by 0009, retrying (60 sec)
Unless you have tweaked the launcher script you shouldn't be seeing the fixme messages (there tends to be a few which causes additional lag, which is why we disabled them in the script).

The err line is the one I get when the game crashes due to settings in UserPrefs being wrong, check that StencilShadows=Disabled, VideoPostEffects=False and LandscapeStaticObjectShadows=Disabled. Also check that your registry (winecfg) has an entry for the correct amount of graphics ram for your card, no entry means it defaults to 64MB (I think) which tends to be crash happy for the bombardment of graphics LOTRO throws at you. While there check that UseGLSL is set to enabled.

If all that is OK post your UserPrefs and I'll see if I can spot anything and give it a go on my system.

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-06-19
Uh, regarding that driver problem, I had a few issues with the last setup I did as well.
Unknown reasoning on NVidia's side has led them to believe that enabling the now built-in composite extension by default would be a great idea. Only direct rendering and/or GLX don't work when it is enabled.

Either use

```
Section "Extensions"
  Option "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

or set the other option that allows both to be used (probably causing problems, though)
Under the Device Section:
Option "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"


I will give that gui thingy a try later today, ajackson, kudos so far, the screenshot looks good. :)

HTH,
SNy

---

That also could have been the problem that was being reported in the earlier posts but if the gui fails to work can you try Sny's script immediately afterwards, if that then works it means the gui has a problem, if both fail it suggests server problems.

Thanks for the feedback so far.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-21
The patching is a three stage process:

1 - Identify that a patch exists
2 - Identify where to download patches from
3 - Decrypt the downloads

I have a rough idea how (2) is being calculated but without (1) I can't be certain. If I can at least get (1) working it would be a bonus.

For (3) the functions they use for decrypting are in one of the dlls in the LOTRO folder, I'm certain of that but I need to work out the key they are using to encrypt the files in the first place but I haven't done any work on that so far.

I think when I release the next version of LotROLinux I'll remove the .exe extension as it is a bit confusing (Monodevelop sticks it on automatically and I can't work out how to turn it off).

Edit: for running the gui you should be able to just type ./LotROLinux.exe (ie shouldn't need to stick mono in front of it)

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-06-21
Hey ajackson,

Regarding 2), I would assume that the result from the server contains the location. Unfortunately, the result looks the same binary garbage as the request.

In essence, it would be necessary to get an insight into the data they sent. I had a brief look at search results regarding that WinInetClient they claim to be and being .NET the thought that it is maybe just some serialized C# class object briefly crossed my mind. I do think it is some rather obscure way of using HTTP as transport for a web service of sorts. Why they have implemented two seemingly disjoint web service implementations for the patch and the login stuff, I have no idea.

Regarding the gui launcher, I have not yet come around to test it, as I was busy mining ores d; and don't have enough free space for the patch content on the disk under linux ATM, anyway. Need to clean up a bit, maybe get rid of the old 32 bit debian that is lurking somewhere on that drive in the process. I will give it a shot as soon as that has happened.

Regards,
SNy

### Post by Xanius on 2007-06-21
> **ajackson said:**
> The patching is a three stage process:

1 - Identify that a patch exists
2 - Identify where to download patches from
3 - Decrypt the downloads

I have a rough idea how (2) is being calculated but without (1) I can't be certain. If I can at least get (1) working it would be a bonus.

For (3) the functions they use for decrypting are in one of the dlls in the LOTRO folder, I'm certain of that but I need to work out the key they are using to encrypt the files in the first place but I haven't done any work on that so far.

I think when I release the next version of LotROLinux I'll remove the .exe extension as it is a bit confusing (Monodevelop sticks it on automatically and I can't work out how to turn it off).

Edit: for running the gui you should be able to just type ./LotROLinux.exe (ie shouldn't need to stick mono in front of it)

I can tell you which DLLs are being used for decryption. They are using Zlib,Zlib1 and Zlib1T...they are used to compress the files, but I guess it encrypts at the same time.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-22
OK then version 0.2 is now available, see the link in my signature for the current web page that I will use for maintaining it (it looks a bit weird but the whole site is under reconstruction so should get a little tidier as I get around to that).

@Mrwasab1 thanks for the buddy key, I've created an account and I can get it to authenticate, it seemed to be their server complaining about how mono was sending the soap packet, fixed it now so (fingers crossed) it should work fine for people using the US server.

@Xanius, thanks for finding out about the decrypt libraries, should make that part of the process a bit easier. As to the weird texture problems, check your UserPrefs, especially the StencilShadows option, if it isn't disabled you tend to get some very weird glitches (doesn't crash, just looks odd).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-22
> **kasulstyls said:**
> I would like to say thanks for everybody on contributing to this cause!! But my question is, that i am not able to get the GUI to run. Yes, i am a noob! I have install the windows client of mono to run the GUI but I am not getting anything to display.  What command or method am i missing to start this application?  I put the gui.exe and the icon in my wine directory as well as the scripts.

To run the gui (you have it in the right place), type either
```
./LotROLinux.exe
``` or ```
mono LotROLinux.exe
``` despite it having a .exe extension it isn't a windows executable, monodevelop likes to stick the .exe on the end. Once I'm certain it is working fine for both EU and US based servers I will continue work on patching and maybe another couple of enhancements and for version 0.3 get rid of the .exe extension and tidy up the documentation.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-23
OK version 0.3 is rady (see link in sig), it is a source tarball so that should get around the library version issue. If the confgure stops with an error have a look at the README as that lists two of the packages I had to install on a fresh dapper install (the gtkhtml error is very confusing when you see what package you actually need to install).

Oh and the executable is now called LotROLinux.run to avoid confusion over it having a .exe extension.

Let me know how you get on.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-23
> **kasulstyls said:**
> 
checking for mcs... no
configure: error: mcs Not found
---------

I have install these tool to assist on source file but not sure if this is all that i need
build-essential,manpages-dev, autoconf, automake, libtool,flex, bison, gcc-doc, g++

Is there anything that i am messing up or am not understanding to compile this source file?

You need to install either mono-mcs or mono-devel (the latter is just a dummy package that installs a few development utils/tools for mono). Try with just mono-mcs if that doesn't work use mono-devel

I will add that error (mcs not found) to the README.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-23
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> I have used the 0.3 release to build LotROLinux.run, which worked smoothly. But when I try to start the application, nothing happens, not even any kind of error message.
When you say nothing happens does the launcher close itself? If so then you can try getting rid of the WINEDEBUG settings in the options windows (just blank the line) to see if wine is doing anything.

If the launcher stays up what does the status window (bottom right) have in it, hopefully it will contain an error message.

There will be a version 0.3.1 soon (I'm disabling the check for using embedded gecko as I am getting crashes on one of my test systems).

Edit: 0.3.1 is up and available, only change was to remove the use of the gecko renderer due to crashes on my test systems.

---

### Post by CSWScrooge on 2007-06-23
Well I think it doesn't come to do anything with wine.

2 Scenarios:

1.) I have a link on my desktop to start LotRoLinux which brings up a window that looks like the image in the attachement (I hopefully added). Pretty empty with hits of the input fields etc. Nothing else happens. The window can only be closed by killing the process.

2.) I start LotROLinux from console. No window comes up. No output at all. 

When I look with 'ps aux', the status of the process is 'Sl+'. On page 29 of this thread you can find the last lines of the output of the verbose mode of mono.

installing 0.3.1 didn't help either :(

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-23
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> 
2 Scenarios:

1.) I have a link on my desktop to start LotRoLinux which brings up a window that looks like the image in the attachement (I hopefully added). Pretty empty with hits of the input fields etc. Nothing else happens. The window can only be closed by killing the process.

By pretty empt do you mean nothing in the realm and language drop-downs and nothing in the news pane (the one below the logo)? If that
is the case then it can't find the language files client_local_*.dat, and it should hopefully be complaining about this in the box bottom right.

> 
2.) I start LotROLinux from console. No window comes up. No output at all. 

When I look with 'ps aux', the status of the process is 'Sl+'. On page 29 of this thread you can find the last lines of the output of the verbose mode of mono.

I don't know what the + signifies on the ps status but mine reports as Sl. If you are getting exactly the same output as you posted on page 29 then I don't think you are running the right version, the executable changed to LotROLinux.run in version 0.3 and in 0.3.1 I removed the initial check for gecko which your log seems to be referring to.

Double check that it is LotROLinux.run and it is in the same directory as lotroclient.exe and the client_local_*.dat files. Also check that you don't have any rogue LotROLinux.exe sitting around that are somehow being ran.

Did you create the link on the desktop as an actual link or a launcher (ie when you right click on the desktop you get the option to create a launcher), the gui picks up where it was ran from and assumes that all the files it needs are also in that place.

---

```

System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo:set_RedirectStandardOutput (bool) emitted at 0xb430dd18 to 0xb430dd27 (code length 15) [LotROLinux.run]
Method System.Diagnostics.Process:WaitForExit (int) emitted at 0xb43110b8 to 0xb43110e7 (code length 47) [LotROLinux.run]

I think I have worked out what is going wrong here, those lines I left in the code block tell me that somehow you are still compiling my gecko detection code (which was dodgy as hell).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-24
@kasulstyls

I've had that connection lost with server error on a windows box as well, from what I can tell when you logon you join the world queue with a queue id (which is passed to the client so that the client knows when it can connect proper), it seems that every once in a while items just vanish of the queue so when the client asks "can I logon proper" it gets told "you aren't in the queue", then you get that error.

@CSWScrooge

I'm glad you have it loading correct now, as for the authentication error, either you have been unlucky with the game servers or there is something in your username and/or password. Do you have any special characters in either of them? If so then the gui might not be sending them out properly to the authentication server.

I will look into adding better feedback for errors from the server as that should aid problem fixing, for now though give it another go, if it still fails give SNy's script a go, if that doesn't fail we know it is something in the gui.

@metabeardo

What nvidia card have you got? If it is a fairly new one (late series 7 or series 8 ) you might need to use the beta drivers as I understand the series 8 cards are still a bit problematic.

Also double check that you have the registry entry for graphics memory correct as that does make a huge difference.

### Post by Fitzy_oz on 2007-06-25
> **lpc911 said:**
> When i try to use ALSA instead of OSS, i get these errors when the client starts:

Also, the real problem with using ALSA is that once im in the game, it is _extremely_ choppy when using ALSA. im not sure why this is. However i do hear somewhat choppy sound. :)  If i set niceness to -20, it the game speeds up to around 3 fps. literally.

What settings do you use in your winecfg audio tab to use ALSA? hardware acceleration, driver emulation, etc?

The trick I used is from another game - CSS - It would only work with ALSA if i used the following guide to set it up, however once it's setup it seems to work quite well and applies to everything that uses ALSA under wine.  I tried messing around with the niceness but it didn't seem to yield a massive change in performance for me in OSS or ALSA.  I should also point out that earlier I meant that the cutscenes don't have sound under OSS, the rest of the game was fine.  

```
To use ALSA with CSS:


Create ~/.asoundrc with the following content: 
pcm.css {
    type asym
    playback.pcm "hw:0"
}

Add the following registry entries: 
[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\Alsa Driver]
"AutoScanCards"="N"
"DeviceCount"="1"
"devicePCM1"="css"

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\DirectSound]
"DefaultBitsPerSample"="16"
"DefaultSampleRate"="44100"
"HardwareAcceleration"="Full"

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\Drivers]
"Audio"="alsa"


```

### Post by metabeardo on 2007-06-25
As a side note, I have found that toggling the interface display (F12 by default) refreshes everything, and this has made the game at least playable for me. If I really get into it I might even spring for a newer card;) As it is I'm not sure whether this is going to pull away from WoW, not because it isn't a great game but because I'm not thrilled about starting over at the bottom of the power scale.

Something that I might recommend for people who use a chat client like Teamspeak or Ventrilo and want to run an MMO at the same time under linux: Put in an extra sound card. They're cheap, and cheaper if you pick up a used one (sometimes older is better with linux, which thrives on legacy technology). Then run the game sound through external speakers from one sound card and the chat through the other card in your headphones. I'm even a little weirder- I have the game sounds in ear buds with headphone over top of them. I realize that LOTRO has integrated chat, but there are many reasons why you might want to keep your other client. This also sorts out conflicts between OSS and alsa- OSS handles my chat card, because some software (Teamspeak) requires OSS, and alsa handles game sound. The game sound, btw, for LOTRO has worked very well for me under alsa from the start. I'm running with Emulation at 22050 and 8 bits, which I believe are the defaults.

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-06-25
Actually, this 'second soundcard' is also a good idea if you intend to use the built-in voice chat. You can set the output device independently and therefore have the voice stuff on headphones (I use my old Xbox controller + headset, for instance, which is just a USB soundcard).
Using the integrated voice chat over the same device and speakers as the game sound does require to put all volume levels down but the voice level way up to be able to hear properly.

The lacking driver support for the headset is practically the only thing that hinders me playing this under linux all the time. Aside from the lack of far-sight under wine the graphics are about comparable (also in terms of performance, thanks to the suggestions of you guys). I had it running for about 6 hours straight on saturday with no problems stability-wise whatsoever, ran quests, ported and rode across middle earth, farmed ore and worms, crafted, everything. The one thing I have not come across was a cut-scene movie so I don't know wether I have working sound there.

There's just two issues with the game I did encounter:
 - right clicking degraded from three clicks to one reaction to totally being unable to register at all, not reproducible, worked flawlessly after restart
 - keyboard mapping is wrong (no umlauts, circumflex key does not work properly)

The latter might even be fixable with wine settings, haven't checked yet. Once I get around to fiddle with the xbox headset and usb-sound again and this time get it work, I will not be using windows to play this anymore at all. d; Currently I do use it for group quests where it is essential to communicate.

SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-25

 - right clicking degraded from three clicks to one reaction to totally being unable to register at all, not reproducible, worked flawlessly after restart

The first one is linked to the memory leak problem they have nearly fixed, funnily enough the memory leak was less of an issue under linux than under windows, I think linux handles freeing up memory better than windows and has more available die to less system-bloat.

The keyboard mapping, I seem to remember there being a solution to that, it could well be buried in all the old WoW posts on the AppDB (and we are talking about an entry from last year). If you launch a text editor (via wine) can you enter the keys correctly?

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-26
> **SNy said:**
> I just checked out the wineHQ appDB and in case that Alanj ist you, ajackson, then again kudos four your work.
That how-to is really well done and therefore I put a link to it onto my page as well.
Yeah that's me.


Update on the patch detection, I've followed the code as best as I can but it starts making calls in non .NET dlls, I've tried disassembling them but to be honest it's been quite a while since I trawled through assembly code. I can see what it uses to initialise the call to it's patch handler but I can't see what data it actually sends to the patch server (other than the encoded capture using ethereal/wireshark).

I have found that the original launcher uses data found in a hidden directory in the users "home" directory (in Local Settings\Application Data), that info may or may not be useful (there is a nice log file showing what it receives and how it calls lotroclient which could have saved a lot of hassle earlier :)).

I still might be able to get renice working using sudo (as most linuxes seem to have sudo on them) as I can redirect the input and pass the password to it that way.

Just a thought on those having problems with sound/mics/etc, I had to recompile the latest alsa driver after I fiddled with something and lost sound entirely on my system. I followed the instructions [here]("http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Comprehensive_Sound_Problems_Solutions_Guide"), try at your own risk though.

---

### Post by bsleys on 2007-06-26
I had to add the following

--no-check-certificate

To in the following 2 places this morning to get this to work

[FONT="Courier New"]echo "Requesting GLS authentication ticket..."
wget -nv \
    --header 'Content-Type: text/xml; charset=utf-8' \
    [COLOR="Red"]--no-check-certificate[/COLOR]\
    --header 'SOAPAction: "http://www.turbine.com/SE/GLS/LoginAccount"' \
    --post-data "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"utf-8\"?><soap:Envelope xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\" xmlns:xsd=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema\" xmlns:soap=\"http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/\"><soap:Body><LoginAccount xmlns=\"http://www.turbine.com/SE/GLS\"><username>${account}</username><password>${pass}</password><additionalInfo></additionalInfo></LoginAccount></soap:Body></soap:Envelope>" \
    "${authServer_URL}" -O .launcher/GLSAuthServer.config


echo -e "\nConnecting to world login queue for realm ${serverNames[$selectedServer]}...";
wget -nv [COLOR="Red"]--no-check-certificate[/COLOR] --post-data="${worldQueue_ARGS}" "${worldQueue_URL}" -O .launcher/WorldQueue.config[/FONT]

EDIT:
Oh and this was for US servers if that matters.

### Post by CSWScrooge on 2007-06-26
> **ajackson said:**
>

Curiosity took me today, so to better understand what's the problem, I looked into the code and think, I got at least some of it ;). 
I'm not so deep into the operating system principles, therefore I have to ask:
Is there some security or stability risk in just starting a process for 'nice' and then deliver the complete wine command as argument?
Nice should then immediately start a new process for wine with the desired niceness and no root rights should be required.

> **ajackson said:**
> 
Just a thought on those having problems with sound/mics/etc, I had to recompile the latest alsa driver after I fiddled with something and lost sound entirely on my system. I'm going to give it a try, now.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-26
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> Curiosity took me today, so to better understand what's the problem, I looked into the code and think, I got at least some of it ;). 
I'm not so deep into the operating system principles, therefore I have to ask:
Is there some security or stability risk in just starting a process for 'nice' and then deliver the complete wine command as argument?
Nice should then immediately start a new process for wine with the desired niceness and no root rights should be required.
I might be completely wrong but I don't think a normal user can set a nice value below/above (minus is better in nice value terms) the normal level 0.

I tried doing
```
nice -n -10 gedit
```but the command failed because I didn't have super user privs, doing ```
sudo nice -n -10 gedit
``` ran gedit with a nice of -10.

So while you can in theory kick off a process with the program as "sudo" and the params as "nice -n -10 gedit" you still have to get around being able to pass the password into that. Running lotroclient as root would also change where it looked for things like UserPrefs. Add to that the inherent dangers of damage to your system caused my a program error (under a normal user you might bork that users area, under roor you could bork the entire system) and you get to the situation I was working on where the game is kicked off as a normal user but a separate process then becomes root, changes it's nice value then exits. But again it is getting that to work across a range of linuxes, I could probably do it for Ubuntu but then it might not work on SuSE/Fedora/BrandX or vice versa.

As I found out by distributing a compiled version, even different versions of Ubuntu did not like that. If I could be certain that all linuxes impiimented sudo in the same way (one option in particular, the one that allows you to redirect the input) then I could get it to work by requesting the password myself and feeding that into the renice process.

Glad you found looking at the code interesting and by all means if you want to tweak it to suit your own needs then do it.

Well, ähm hmmm, 
I think it's a bit funny actually ...
I was starting the client with the following command:

```
nice -20 wine ... 
```

"Now that I can see" (better at least)  .... of course this command doesn't set the processes niceness to -20, but rather to +20. (checked that with top)

the funny thin is, that with that command the game runs a lot better than without it .... I'm puzzled, 

comparing the processes with and without the nice-command via top again, showed that with nice the process seems to get a higher priority (35 to 18). Does that make sense?

When I start lotro without nice, I get a black screen for a few minutes before character selection and then again before the game starts. Ant overall it's not playable.

When I start it with that nice command all runs almost perfectly (all but the sound in the cut-scenes).

> **ajackson said:**
> 
So while you can in theory kick off a process with the program as "sudo" and the params as "nice -n -10 gedit" you still have to get around being able to pass the password into that. Running lotroclient as root would also change where it looked for things like UserPrefs. Add to that the inherent dangers of damage to your system caused my a program error (under a normal user you might bork that users area, under roor you could bork the entire system) and you get to the situation I was working on where the game is kicked off as a normal user but a separate process then becomes root, changes it's nice value then exits. But again it is getting that to work across a range of linuxes, I could probably do it for Ubuntu but then it might not work on SuSE/Fedora/BrandX or vice versa.

As I found out by distributing a compiled version, even different versions of Ubuntu did not like that. If I could be certain that all linuxes impiimented sudo in the same way (one option in particular, the one that allows you to redirect the input) then I could get it to work by requesting the password myself and feeding that into the renice process.

Glad you found looking at the code interesting and by all means if you want to tweak it to suit your own needs then do it.


I think, maybe, this whole nice thing was a bad idea ...

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-27
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> comparing the processes with and without the nice-command via top again, showed that with nice the process seems to get a higher priority (35 to 18 ). Does that make sense?
I think, but again not certain, the lower the priority the better in terms of processing so a 5 would get processed more often than a 10.

As for getting a stable performance I think I know why, when the client loads (and probably while the game is running) it tries to adjust itself to your system (not by much though), if the responses to it's performance queries are wrong (remember they are going through wine so there is a possibility of then not working 100% right) then it's tweaks will be wrong.

By in effect slowing your machine (altering the nice value of the clients process) you might be inadvertantly correcting the balance on the demand/deliver scale.

There are two options in UserPrefs EngineSpeed and MaximumFrameRate. On my system to get a smoother performance I had to adjust the MaxFrameRate down to 50, on the EU LOTRO forums in the thread on performance tweaking they say that setting the MaxFrameRate to 50-60 and/or setting the EngineSpeed down to high can help with performance.

---

### Post by enigma83 on 2007-06-27
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> Well, ähm hmmm, 
I think it's a bit funny actually ...
I was starting the client with the following command:

```
nice -20 wine ... 
```

"Now that I can see" (better at least)  .... of course this command doesn't set the processes niceness to -20, but rather to +20. (checked that with top)

the funny thin is, that with that command the game runs a lot better than without it .... I'm puzzled, 


I noticed a similar issue with running WoW under wine, with nice.  With WoW, we used to "renice" the wineserver process, not the actual WoW process.  Sort of like, speed up the API translation, rather than speed up the game itself.
Maybe when you run nice -20 wine ... it sets the games nice level to 20, while wineserver keeps 0, giving a similar effect.

### Post by CSWScrooge on 2007-06-27
I tried a few things:

1) 
I edited my UserPrefs.ini and checked that all values were according to the howtos and your last post. Then I started LoTRO without 'nice'.

Result:
Intro was played very slowly and in Char Selection there was no sound, no char was shown the interface didn't respond (that is, mouse pointer could be moved, but klicking had no effect) .
- I tried this with different values for MaximumFrameRate with no effect.

2)
I tried to renice 
  a) the 'lotroclient' process
  b) the 'wineserver' process
  c) both processes
both to several higher and lower nicenesses.
- again no effect (so, maybe you should not waste your time anymore on this matter, unless anyone else has better results with renicing processes).

3)
I started lotro with nice and several nice settings > 0. With increasing niceness, the performance got better and better. With 'nice -n +19' I'm able to 
 - activate 'Pixel Shader'
 - set 'VertexShader' to 'Hardware'
 - and play with (compared to the recommendations in several 'lotro on linux' threads) relatively high details.
 - the only setting, that still seems to be crtitical is 'LandscapeStaticObjectShadows'. As soon as this is activated, the game doesn't start. Everything else works, although it doesn't alway look nice ;)

by the way, with nice -20 (or -n +20 or -n +19) both processes (wineserver and lotroclient) get the niceness of +19 and a priority of  35.

So it's true after all: 'Be nice to others and others will be nice to you' ;)

P.S: Even cut-scenes have sound now. The problem was an incomplete dmix configuration in my .asoundrc.

### Post by CSWScrooge on 2007-06-27
Just in case you didn't read this thread completely:

Could it be that only your high resolution file are out of date?

Graphics settings need to be set to at least very high ingame, or else these files are not updated.

If this is the case then a little modification to the launcher script should help:

```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} --HighResOutOfDate $@
```

...as already postet on page 23 by ajackson.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-27
> **Marigny said:**
> I updated all files and I copied the entire installation directory in the wine installation directory. But it doesn't work.
And I am on the EU servers.

It works on WIndows yet ! :(
Just for a test select a server you don't normally use, see if it is something server related. Each server has two login queue urls, both the script and launcher take the first one listed. I could be an error with that particular queue url.

Failing that only thing I can think off is to delete LOTRO from your linux box (empty it out of trash as well if it ends up in there), copy it over from windows again and give it another try. Oh make sure all files belong to your user id (use chown), I don't know if it makes any difference but it can't hurt.

### Post by CSWScrooge on 2007-06-28
> **ajackson said:**
> 
I know there is a log file that gets written to that is hidden in the users profiles directory on windows, it shows how the client is being called (if we found that earlier it would have saved a bit of work), unfortunately it doesn't tell us a great deal about the patching which is the current stumbling block but the EU is getting a patch today so I get a fresh capture of network traffic to analyse. I know where it is sending data but I'm not certain how or what it actually sends.


Yes I know there are several files, some Launcher_<some_number>.log files and the PatchClient.log, but I think by file system monitoring some additional information can be gathered. Problem is, I do not know exactly what kind of information is really needed.

Some examples:

Although I'm not certain about that, for instance comparing the timestamps in the Launcher.log and the FileMon.log could help locating the version information of the local game resources, like:

```

Examine Game Data: Waiting                                                                           28.06.2007 08:57:03
Examine Game Data: Done                                                                              28.06.2007 08:57:05

```

```

13100	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	FLUSH	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS		
13101	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ 	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 320 Length: 104	
13102	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 320 Length: 104	
13103	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 256 Length: 64	
13104	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 814958808 Length: 2460	
13105	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 1146193724 Length: 2460	
13106	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 1113417460 Length: 2460	
13107	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 1094478592 Length: 2460	
13108	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 1141893448 Length: 45	
13109	08:57:04	TurbineLauncher:1356	READ	G:\Spiele\Herr der Ringe Online\client_surface.dat	SUCCESS	Offset: 87648632 Length: 36	

```

There are other things that might be interesting ...

for example, after looking at the log I saw that the TurbineInvoker during it's initialization 'executed' some DLL's I didn't expect (like: OFFICE12\GROOVEUTIL.DLL) , as well as seardched all my Cookies for something.

At last, although .NET 2.0 is required by the Launcher it only seems to access .NET V1.1.4322 system libraries.

I can see that some crypt libraries are used, as well as webservices and soap, and there is some encryption key information stored in the user settings folder.

If something of that can help let me know. 

By the way, if you have not yet decompiled the code of the launcher, you can do that with Reflector from [http://www.aisto.com/roeder/dotnet/](http://www.aisto.com/roeder/dotnet/).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-29
Well I've made an interesting discovery, using two packets of data from separate windows logins (a month apart) I wanted to see if they would get the same response from the patch server (because at the end of the day, the patch server should be telling the launcher about the current version). It didn't, so OK I assumed that the patch server used the same encryption as the request, which means that the data I sent contained the decryption key in it.

So I send the same request data out twice expecting to receive two identical responses but again I don't, there is a larger portion of the responses identical but also differences. So the replies are being sent with a different encryption key and again that key has to be part of the data sent out.

"Loggin in please wait" then black screen with the quit button and the label  "the connection to the server has been lost!".

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-30
> **Lukather said:**
> Hi all!
I've read all the 37 pages and tryin' all but can't fix the problem. GUI Launche works perfectly (thanks SNy) game starts but i t fail at the login page:
"Loggin in please wait" then black screen with the quit button and the label  "the connection to the server has been lost!".

Yes! I've updated the game under windows and I can play from there so this isn't the problem...what to do now?
You normally get that error when one or more of the files are out of date, fire the game up under windows and while on the character selection screen open the options menu and select a base configuration of very high (or ultra high), if you then get a message about the hi-res patch being out of date then restart the launcher and it should patch those files.

If you get no error (or can't set graphics to very/ultra high) get SNy's script (see link in his signature) and edit the line that runs wine to read 
[cpde]wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} --HighResOutOfDate $@[/code]
Give that a go to see if it gets you past that error. If it does let us know and I'll add an option in the GUI to pass that flag.

Whatever you try make sure that the copy of LOTRO on linux is up to date, easiest way is to delete the whole LOTRO directory and copy the windows one fresh (removes the possibility that a file may not be copied due to it already existing).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-06-30
**Changes for wine 0.9.40**

The most noticeable change that 0.9.40 had brought for LOTRO is that now you don't have to disable LandscapeStaticObjectShadows (I had mine on medium and played for a couple of hours without any problems, with 0.9.39 I would have crashed after 20 minutes max).

StencilShadows and VideoPostEffects still need to be Disabled/False, though with VideoPostEffects you don't seem to crash any more but get the occasional weird graphical glitching. With StencilShadows the world goes dim and objects like doors seem very weird.

The help still doesn't work but that relies heavily on IE and while wines implementation is getting better it is still not good enough for LOTROs needs.

### Post by drummingpariah on 2007-07-01
just out of curiosity, what's keeping the installer/patcher from writing files in Wine?

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-07-02
It's not that it couldn't write, the problem is that the patch process uses an encryption of sorts over it's HTTP transport.
As of yet (ajackson, correct me if I am wrong here), it is not known how the launcher gets to know the location and names/number of files. Launcher and patch server obviously communicate over the encrypted channel to compare versions and compile a list of files that are to be downloaded for updates. The update messages from the launcher also suggest that the downloaded files need to be decrypted first.

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-02
> **SNy said:**
> It's not that it couldn't write, the problem is that the patch process uses an encryption of sorts over it's HTTP transport.
As of yet (ajackson, correct me if I am wrong here), it is not known how the launcher gets to know the location and names/number of files. Launcher and patch server obviously communicate over the encrypted channel to compare versions and compile a list of files that are to be downloaded for updates.
Yeah basically the launcher sends out what I assume is encrypted hash keys of some sort for the main files and gets told which ones are out of date (again encrypted), decifering what is sent is almost impossible as I'm assuming it is using 128 bit encryption which is virtually uncrackable unless you have a computer with some really serious power and a spare few years or so.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-02
> **william_bob said:**
> Hi,
  I am a fan of the Lord of the Rings movie and I would be happy if I will be able to plat it online.Can you please post the link to the site so that I can get with it.
Head over to [here]("http://appdb.winehq.org/appview.php?iVersionId=8273"), it is the AppDB page for the latest version of LOTRO. The guide should hopefully be straight forward enough.

The basic overview is:

- install wine (version 0.9.40 is the latest but 0.9.37 onwards work fine)
- install the game on a windows machine (updates need to be done via windows at the moment)
- copy LOTRO to your linux box
- get either SNy's CLI launcher or my GUI launcher (needs to be compiled, see the info in the README file)
- tweak your UserPreferences.ini file as a few settings cause problems

You should then be able to play, any problems post here and we'll try to help out.

Well, I would recommend heading over to [http://winehq.org]("http://winehq.org") and following the instructions there.

I don't use Fedora--this is an Ubuntu forum after all :p--but 0.9.39 works just fine as well, so the upgrade isn't necessary from a functionality standpoint.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-04
After a very quick google of "compiling wine fedora" I found [this]("http://wiki.winehq.org/Recommended_Packages") page which tells you what packages you need to install to compile wine, you may also need to install the build-essential package (contains the base compilers).

As for compiling the launcher, I made a mistake in the README,
the commands needed are
```
./configure
make
```
Sorry about that.

The configure will probably complain about not finding mcs and/or gtkhtml, hopefully you can find fedora's equivalent to the two packages mention for mcs and the one for gtkhtml.

I'll fix the README file and upload the new version to my website.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-04
Version 0.3.2 of the gui launcher is up.

I used a newer version of monodevelop to generate the configure scripts and associated file so just in case don't copy these files into version 0.3.1 (or earlier) folder to build, shouldn't matter but better safe than sorry.

One major fix - README now says to use configure instead of compile (I can't even blame alcohol for that mistake :)).

One change - Each realm has two login queues, the launcher now randomly decides which queue to use rather than using the first one, probably makes no difference to anything but it was an easy modification.

---

### Post by ObiWan2001 on 2007-07-04
In fedora the latest wine gets added to the standard repo sooner or later, so usually there is no need to use an extra-repo or compiling.

Edit:
Compiled it for Fedora 7, just unpack the attached file into the LotRO folder,
and launch it with ./lotrolauncher.run.

PS:
To install mono do this in the console:
```

su
yum install gnome-sharp

```
everything else should be selected as dependency

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-05
Just installed a copy of Fedora 7 into a virtual machine, I found that installing the following packages (and any they then pull in) lets you install the gui launcher.
```
gnome-sharp-devel
gtk-sharp2-devel
gtkhtml38
```

This game is slightly more complicated than many windows games to run because of the launcher it uses, so you are getting a bit of a baptism by fire.

I am trying to compile a list of packages by distribution so that I can add that information into the README but I also don't have all day to spend installing distros just to try and compile the launcher.

The gui launcher is based on a script by SNy, you don't need any additional packages to use that so it makes a useful fall back if you are having problems with the gui launcher.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-06
Version 0.4 of the GUI now available.

Changes:

- The fetching of the news feed is now a separate thread so the main window should load quicker now.

- Available languages are now shown as names rather than the languages code (if you use the US version you might be saying "what?" but the EU version showed EN_GB, FR and DE).

### Post by SNy on 2007-07-15
Marigny, I have two comments to your tutorial.
I don't understand much French, but anyway, good work.

One thing is about adding the option "--HighResOutOfDate" to the script itself.
Notice the two characters $@ at the end of the line, this is bash for "put all command line parameters here", used in that place it appends every parameter you give to the script to the wine/game start command. Hence, for the one time you have not updated the highres files, just start the script with the appended parameter.

Then about the certificate. I am not sure why different wget versions behave differently here. I have used at least two that didn't complain without the parameter, others do complain. I will add it to the script after checking with what the servers actually respond with.

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-15
> **SNy said:**
> Then about the certificate. I am not sure why different wget versions behave differently here. I have used at least two that didn't complain without the parameter, others do complain. I will add it to the script after checking with what the servers actually respond with.
I think the only problematic one is the call to authenticate the user as it is via https rather than http. I think the default compile options for wget vary from distribution to distribution, I know for the gui I had to create a dummy certificate handler, otherwise I kept getting errors.

**wine 0.9.41**
Been trying the new version out, no changes for the current set of work arounds in the UserPrefs but the game does seem smoother (might have been a fix server side though).

### Post by SNy on 2007-07-15
I have also tried with wine 0.9.41 and I have an update on the help system.

Good news first, it basically works. BUT.
Bad news: when activating it, the display gets corrupted in a strange way. Top/right edge of screen flicker between some cyanish color and the parts of the display that were there before. Flickering can be stopped with ALT+TAB. It seems to permanently shift the actual display to the lower left, though, as afterwards the mouse cursor and its hotspot differ, so you need to place the cursor way above the item you want to activate.
Then, the help browser itself... it doesn't exactly display anything unless you grab its window and move it around. If you do, you see the actual content. If you let got of it, it's all black again. This seems to be a problem with the way it is rendered into the game, causing both the above effect as well as the absence of the rendered pages when not moving it. I think I will file a bug report with wine tomorrow.

SNy

Hence, for the one time you have not updated the highres files, just start the script with the appended parameter.

Thanks for your advices !

About "--HighResOutOfDate", I'm adding this option to the line that contains gameClient_ARGS :

```
gameClient_ARGS=`echo "${gameClient_ARGTMPL}" | sed -e "s/[{]0[}]/${accountNum}/;s/[{]1[}]/${serverAdress}/;s#[{]2[}]#${glsTicket}#;s/[{]3[}]/${serverChat}/;s/[{]4[}]/${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --HighResOutOfDate  /"`
```

Is it correct ?

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-07-17
> **Marigny said:**
> About "--HighResOutOfDate", I'm adding this option to the line that contains gameClient_ARGS :

```
gameClient_ARGS=`echo "${gameClient_ARGTMPL}" | sed -e "s/[{]0[}]/${accountNum}/;s/[{]1[}]/${serverAdress}/;s#[{]2[}]#${glsTicket}#;s/[{]3[}]/${serverChat}/;s/[{]4[}]/${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --HighResOutOfDate  /"`
```

Is it correct ?

Uh, well, no.
My suggestion was to not edit the script for the parameter at all, but to call the script with it appended, like so:
```
./lotrolauncher.script --HighResOutOfDate
```

As the script will just append all parameters it gets to the command line it finally executes, this will end up being a parameter to the game. You can also add various other options to the game client this way.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-18
> **Fitzy_oz said:**
> Has anyone managed to get it working under Cedega yet, i wonder whether the direct X implementation under it might yield some different results for the currently unusable graphics opitions? or speed?

I have tried it but am still hampered by the permissions error that is mentioned quite a few times earlyin the thread.
The only thing I can think of for the error under cedega is it not being able to find the "My Documents" folder to read/create the directory where it stores the UserPrefs file, the client has an option --prefs followed by a string which specifies what UserPrefs file to use. Maybe try setting that to the directory that definitely exists and see if you get the same error message.

Edit: Another thought is to get a file tracker to see if you can work out what files it is trying to access.

### Post by mr_padawan on 2007-07-18
Hello everyone!

i'm actually a Gentoo user, but there doesn't seem to be any posts on the gentoo forum about Lotro.

Well, I'm having a problem:
I got this mail today about trying Lotro for free for three doys, so I downloaded the game from one of their supplied mirrors and installed it using wine.
I've followed the guide and fiddled in the registry and so on, and I've built the GUI-launcher too.
However, when the game has launched, it just gets past the first screen and comes to a black screen with only a "Quit" button. Nothing else.

Do I need to update the game, or what's happening?

---

### Post by thak on 2007-07-18
> **mr_padawan said:**
> Hello everyone!

i'm actually a Gentoo user, but there doesn't seem to be any posts on the gentoo forum about Lotro.

Well, I'm having a problem:
I got this mail today about trying Lotro for free for three doys, so I downloaded the game from one of their supplied mirrors and installed it using wine.
I've followed the guide and fiddled in the registry and so on, and I've built the GUI-launcher too.
However, when the game has launched, it just gets past the first screen and comes to a black screen with only a "Quit" button. Nothing else.

Do I need to update the game, or what's happening?

Yes.  As noted in earlier portions of the thread, you have to use a Windows installation to patch the game before you can play it in Linux.  Unfortunately.

However, you should be able to use a virtual machine to do this, since you're not actually going to run the game in the VM...

Good luck...

---

### Post by Beren Camlost on 2007-07-18
I would guess that the downloaded client is not up to date as far as patches go. What you will have to do is patch up LotRO on either a Windows partition or thru a virtual machine, and then copy the updated files (preferably the whole LotRO folder) back into the Wine LotRO folder and then launch the game. 

It's simply a result of the original launcher doing all the patching and we haven't been able to replicate the patching process in wine just yet.

---

### Post by beerfan on 2007-07-19
One solution to circumvent the patching issue would be to write a script which determines which files have changed, tars them up, and creates a torrent of them. Then others could just grab the torrent and not worry about having to load up windows to update.

Caveats:
1. Someone would have to update, with Windows, and publish and everyone else would be suffering until then.
2. Security risk of infected binaries cropping up if this caught on.
3. It would likely be as much of a hassle as just updating with Windows.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-20
The trouble you have is that the patches for the two versions are different and you are guaranteed to get someone who uses the US patch on the EU version (or vice versa) who then becomes abusive towards the person providing the patch. Also I think Turbine have stated that they will come down hard on people hosting their files on the internet, so it is not worth the hassle or aggro.

The patching is definitely something that will be hard to replicate, I know which dll does the work but so far I can't quite work out how it does it and it seems the patching process for the EU version is slightly different than the US version (going by network captures) despite the two respective patching libraries being identical.

### Post by delmar on 2007-07-22
Thanks alot for your efforts SNy and Ajackson !!!.

By following the info on wineHQ regarding the .ini file settings and the registry entries, plus using the GUI Launcher for Linux, I was up and running in no time on my Ubuntu Feisty box, and i didn't need to buy Crossover or fight with Cedega.  I actually got it working and logging in a few weeks back, but haven't had a chance to play at all until today. 

There is only one problem I am having, and that is the HUGE amount of memory usage.

My wifes system is win2k (and is the source of the patched content for my system, when a patch is released). We both started new toons, both Hobbits, and after a few minutes met up in Archet in BreeLand.

To start with my game was fine. Nice and smooth, and my only concern was the amount of time I could see myself sinking into LOTRO.   Soon however, it just got slower and slower. I checked the memory usage and it was almost 1gig (which is all I have). 

When I first start the game and login, lotroclient.exe is using 600mb or so, and while moving around in Archet it soars up to 900mb or higher.  At one point we left the town and ran around killing things, and the memory seemed to hold around 700mb and it was playable.
All the while, my wifes memory usage on her win2k system starts at under 400mb, and doesn't climb up past 500mb.   

I'm sure the quick fix for me is going to be another gig of ram, but the memory usage for LOTRO does seem excessive under Linux/Wine, compared to regular Windoze. 

Does anyone have any other suggestions apart from installing more ram?

Cheers.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-22
I'm glad you got the game working quickly. With regards the memory usage the only solution is to play around with some of the options in the client. There are couple that I would suggest looking at, AggressivelyCleanupGraphicsMemory, set that to "High" and see what MemoryUsage is set to and try adjusting it also MaximumFrameRate set to around 50 or 60. There are a few others that improve performance and/or reduce memory usage but they really are a try and see as it varies from system to system.

The only other thing I would add is to make sure you keep up with the latest release of wine as each refinement (normally release fortnightly) has improved the graphics and smoothness of the game.

### Post by Beren Camlost on 2007-07-22
Ok, so I finally got around to try running LotRO on my own linux system (Kubuntu Feisty Fawn 7.04) with Wine 0.9.41. Copied over the fully patched LotRO directory, downloaded Mono and compiled the launcher. So far so good.

So I run the game with this:

```

mono LotROLinux.run --HighResOutOfDate
```

The launcher start, I fill in my dettails and click connect. The game starts up, I get the screen where it says Connection Succesful, then couple of seconds later screen goes black with a box saying "Your connection to the server have been lost!" and a Quit button.

So, I would guess that this issues is perhaps somehow related to my highres files being out of date, as I don't use them. Worth to note is same happens without the --HighResOutOfDate parameter. Any clues what I should do?

---

### Post by Fitzy_oz on 2007-07-23
> **Beren Camlost said:**
> Ok, so I finally got around to try running LotRO on my own linux system (Kubuntu Feisty Fawn 7.04) with Wine 0.9.41. Copied over the fully patched LotRO directory, downloaded Mono and compiled the launcher. So far so good.

So I run the game with this:

```

mono LotROLinux.run --HighResOutOfDate
```

The launcher start, I fill in my dettails and click connect. The game starts up, I get the screen where it says Connection Succesful, then couple of seconds later screen goes black with a box saying "Your connection to the server have been lost!" and a Quit button.

So, I would guess that this issues is perhaps somehow related to my highres files being out of date, as I don't use them. Worth to note is same happens without the --HighResOutOfDate parameter. Any clues what I should do?

Pretty sure you'll need to run the Launcher on youj're windows box and set the details to high or ultra high and update it again and then copy the game files across again, it only updates the high res files if the detail set for it.

Failing that, could there have been another update in between when you copied the files down?  I have found this once or twice to be the case even when I thought it was up to date... Try copying the whole folder over again if all else fails...

---

### Post by boozereaper on 2007-07-23
why does mine lag so much?

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-23
> **dlange said:**
> Ok that problem is not so important, I think I have a syntax Error in my customization ... but I can let me print out the Arguments and try to start it Manually over Darwine and there I get the following Errormsg from lotroclient.exe

[PHP]Can't open the data files. Check that they exist and that you have permission to write to them. The program will now exit. [201][/PHP]

Does anybody know, what's the problem right here ?
That error is probably down to the game not being able to find the equivalent of your "My Documents" folder, try adding the parameter --prefs followed by a string to indicate where and what you want the UserPreferences.ini file to live/be called.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-23
> **Beren Camlost said:**
> ```

mono LotROLinux.run --HighResOutOfDate
```

The launcher start, I fill in my dettails and click connect. The game starts up, I get the screen where it says Connection Succesful, then couple of seconds later screen goes black with a box saying "Your connection to the server have been lost!" and a Quit button.

So, I would guess that this issues is perhaps somehow related to my highres files being out of date, as I don't use them. Worth to note is same happens without the --HighResOutOfDate parameter. Any clues what I should do?
The gui launcher doesn't accept parameters so passing them is pointless. The connect lost error is normally caused by the game files (including the hi-res) not being up to date, so you need to patch using your windows box (to patch hi-res you may have to start the client and alter your configuration to get it to complain about the hi-res files being out of date, it is a known problem with LOTRO). The other main cause of the connect lost error is a problem server side (it sometimes losing login entries on it queues) but if you get it consistantly then it would be out of date files or the server is not up.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-23
> **dlange said:**
> Hello, thanks for the hint, but it doesn't really work. I added this [PHP]--prefs /Codemasters/lotro/UserPreferences.ini[/PHP] at the end, but I get the same error message
Try passing "./UserPreferences.ini" or maybe even just "UserPreferences.ini" to see if it can be persuaded to use the current directory (that should rule out a path error).

### Post by dlange on 2007-07-23

> **Posh said:**
> Ok I have read through this thread and the appdb page but I'm having trouble getting this working and hopefully someone here might have a suggestion that will get me going in the right direction.

Can you get to the character select screen via windows? (just want to rule out your modem or router blocking access).

Other things to check are file ownership/write permissions on the game files as I've noticed that all the client*.dat files get their timestamp updated everytime I run the game. Of course double check that your files are up to date.

Does the screen show anything or is it just the loading screen and hung?
