### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-27
Have you got firestarter installed (the gui front end for the linux firewall), if you have start with it opened (ie so you can see the icon in the top menu bar), see if that changes to the lightning style symbol which indicates it is blocking something.

Are you using the UserPrefs from your window box (with the tweaks needed for wine)? If so try deleting/renaming it and allow the game to create a fresh one, it might be the some hardware being treated slightly differently between linux and windows.

Lastly wine 0.9.42 came out today so give it a go with that.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-28
> **Posh said:**
> Still no luck although I do actually make it to the character selection screen... eventually.... it takes a while and I get some errors about thread timing out from wine.  I havent sat around long enough to see if my character ever shows up on the screen as I just get the screen without my character actually shown but I do hear the music.  Just a few notes.. I use beryl and a dual monitor set up.. i have tried disabling beryl (not uninstalling).. any suggestions on what to try next?
Try having system monitor running before you start the game and alt-tab so that you can see it while the game is loading, see if your processor is getting overworked, also check the temp on your graphics card (it things start over heating I think the processors today throttle down and therefore lose speed until the cool). After the only other thing you can try is swapping to a different sound system in winecfg (ie if you tried with ALSA switch to OSS and vice versa), that has caused problems in the past with other games, also try using/not using driver emulation.

Did you let the game create a new UserPrefs? If not let it do so just incase (yep this one is a clutch at straws solution)

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-29
> **Posh said:**
> I did let it create a new UserPreferences.ini from scratch and that didnt seem to help.  I have tried OSS and ALSA.. only ALSA gave me sound... I'll try the driver emulation next.  The error im getting from WINEDEBUG +all seems to indicate some kind of video issue.  I have a Geforce 9700 gs with the latest Nvidia drivers.   I'll also try the new wine once the repositories get updated.
One other thing you can try is to have a separate console running, start up LOTRO and tabbed to the spare console and use renice to adjust the priority of both the wineserver and lotroclient processes, try setting it to -15, if that doesn't work try 15 (which in theory should make the game have less priority but for a couple of people has improved performance).

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-29
> **enigma83 said:**
> Ok, so I'm slowly working through the list of things to do to get LotRO working.  I've got a vmware'd Windows install, running the free VMWare Server.
Does anyone know the best way to "share" the installed copy of LotRO?  At the moment I'm going to just risk a spare 20GB fat32 partition, and mount it under linux and vmware both.  Is there any safer option?  I really don't want to have to do this over a network if possible.
That should be safe enough to use (at worst you will corrupt a game file but that can be fixed with a reinstall or running the repair utility), I know the wine people don't recommend running stuff directly from a ntfs partition but I haven't seen any reported problems with a vfat partition being used.

### Post by enigma83 on 2007-07-29
Ok.  So, I got it running now.  Well, it starts, I can create a character, log in, and finish the tutorial.
However, it runs damned slow on my rig, 3500+, 2GB RAM, 6600GT.  The video card isn't that flash, but I'm barely getting 10fps, with all settings down low.  I've added the two settings to the registry.
Should my rig get me something playable, at 1280x1024?

I do have to renice the lotroclient.exe process (with -5) to get the game to do anything.  If I don't, the game pauses whenever I move the mouse and won't unpause until I alt-tab.
And also, occasionally I have to double-click to register a single-click.  I'm not sure exactly what causes this, I'll see if I can work it out.  The big issue is the terrible performance though.


Edit:  All 3 issues partially sorted.  If I swap to OSS sound, i get great performance, no need for nicing, and working mouse.  I just get no sound for some reason (yes OSS emulation is enabled in my kernel)
Edit 2:  I checked winecfg.  No wave devices for OSS.  I restarted winecfg.  One wavein device.  Restarted winecfg, and I finally have wavein and waveout.  I don't know why they were missing, or why restarting winecfg fixed things.  Now I'm crashing on world entry, but I have decent performance at least.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-29
Is it bombing out on the same low graphics setting or did you select a better graphics quality? Do you get any sort of error spewed out to the console when it crashes, if so does it by any chance mention textures.c (or something like that?) if it does try increasing the MemoryUsage value in the UserPrefs, I had the game bomb out when I was doing the performance testing with errors about textures and tweaking the MemoryUsage made it a bit more stable.

-snip-

Thanks for the help this far ajackson it has been appreciated

```
Section "Module"
# This loads the DBE extension module.
    Load        "dbe"   # Double buffer extension
# This loads the miscellaneous extensions module, and disables
# initialisation of the XFree86-DGA extension within that module.
    SubSection  "extmod"
      Option    "omit xfree86-dga"   # don't initialise the DGA extension
    EndSubSection
# This loads the Type1 and FreeType font modules
    Load        "type1"
    Load        "freetype"
    Load        "speedo"
# This loads the GLX module
    Load        "glx"   # libglx.a
    Load        "bitmap"
    Load        "ddc"
    Load        "extmod"
    Load        "int10"
    Load        "vbe"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier "NV6600"
    Driver "nvidia"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier  "Screen0"
    Device      "NV6600"
    Monitor     "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth 24
    Option      "AddARGBGLXVisuals"     "true"
    Subsection "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes       "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        ViewPort    0 0  # initial origin if mode is smaller than desktop
#        Virtual     1600 1200
    EndSubsection
EndSection


```
Those are my 3 sections.  Screen, Device and Modules.  Mine works fine at 1280x1024 with a 6600GT.

Note, does your desktop environment support multiple workspaces?  If it does, try starting LotRO, wait till it "freezes", then swap to a different workspace with keyboard shortcuts, then swap back.  If this unpauses it, you're up with the same issue I had, which I fixed by going to OSS sound.

### Post by beerfan on 2007-07-29
> **Malh said:**
> those of you who are new to this thread please sign our petition--

[http://www.petitiononline.com/lotro4lx/petition.html](http://www.petitiononline.com/lotro4lx/petition.html)

going to need a lot more sigs, currently have 95

Unless you think you can get about 95 million, please don't bother. There is no way that Turbine, or any other game developer, is going to spend resources to redevelop an existing game the size of an mmorpg with a native linux client.

Petition to have the windows client use a Wine compatible launcher. Petition for something reasonable but not for something absurdly unreasonable. You might actually get their attention, and more signatures for your petition if you go that route. Asking for a native client is likely to make them just turn on the blinders and ignore your request for support entirely.

We would all love to have our favorite platform supported and the spirit of your petition is admirable so don't get me wrong. But MacOS X is a far more likely alternative platform for game developers to support and even that is ignored.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-07-30
> **Zaphrod said:**
> I cant seem to get passed this error. I have patched through windows, copied the entire directory and have all the settings as suggested. I didn't get any update messages when I set the graphics to ultrahigh then restarted. I am not sure where to go from here.
Can you connect via windows? It could be (going from some of the posts on the LOTRO EU forum) that certain ports are being blocked either on your modem/router or the ISP you are using. If you can connect via windows then that rules out your modem/router and ISP so it could mean that linux's firewall is blocking stuff.

Another thing to check is that your account is active as I noticed that an expired account gets past the authentication process but fails on the game load (this is for DDO but I assume the process would be the same for LOTRO).

### Post by Beren Camlost on 2007-07-30
Set emulation to Windows 2000 in winecfg. Should sort the mouse/keyboard issue.

On that note I might add that I finally got around to LotRO/Wine again. Patched it with highres textures in Windows and then copied it over to Kubuntu. Worked a charm.

Doesn't explain why I could play in Windows weeks on end without the patched highres files but it's not as it's really important anymore ;)

### Post by Fitzy_oz on 2007-07-31
> **mike r said:**
> Been getting the following error the last week or two trying from the CLI script by Syn. I have checked in Windows and it launches fine. Anyone know what it may be?

18:44:09 URL:[http://www.lotro-europe.com/splash.php?return=/index.php](http://www.lotro-europe.com/splash.php?return=/index.php) [2451] -> ".launcher/GLSAuthServer.config" [1]

Error: Could not extract auth result from GLS auth server response.

From that error it ooks like you're running the script from the wrong directory.  The launcher script needs to be in the Lotro installation directory as it extracts that information from the *turbing.config.exe* file.
 Try copying it to the directory you installed Lotro into ie ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online is where it usually installs to.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-01
> **mike r said:**
> Been getting the following error the last week or two trying from the CLI script by Syn. I have checked in Windows and it launches fine. Anyone know what it may be?

18:44:09 URL:[http://www.lotro-europe.com/splash.php?return=/index.php](http://www.lotro-europe.com/splash.php?return=/index.php) [2451] -> ".launcher/GLSAuthServer.config" [1]

Error: Could not extract auth result from GLS auth server response.

Have a look in the .launcher folder where the script was ran from, you should find a file called GLSAuthServer.config, open in in firefox (it doesn't include new lines so opening it in a text editor is messy).

It should look something like this
```

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
  <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">
    <soap:Body><LoginAccountResponse xmlns="http://www.turbine.com/SE/GLS">
      <LoginAccountResult>
        <Subscriptions>
          <GameSubscription>
            <Game>LOTROEU</Game>
            <Name>XXXXXX</Name>
            <Description>Add Sub</Description>
            <ProductTokens>
              <string>AngmarPromo4</string>
              <string>AngmarEBPre</string>
              <string>AngmarPromo6</string>
            </ProductTokens>
            <ExpirationDate>1/1/1970</ExpirationDate>
            <Status>Active</Status>
            <NextBillingDate>1/1/1970</NextBillingDate>
            <PendingCancelDate />
            <AutoRenew>TRUE</AutoRenew>
            <BillingSystemTime>8/1/2007 1:23:45 PM</BillingSystemTime>
           </GameSubscription>
         </Subscriptions>
         <Ticket>very long string</Ticket>
       </LoginAccountResult>
     </LoginAccountResponse>
  </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

```

If you have more than one <Game> entry that could be the problem, I think someone posted a fix for this earlier (quite earlier in this thread).

Edit also look at the file GLSDataCenter.config, there should be an entry near the bottom.
```

<AuthServer>https://lotroeugls.com/GLS.AuthServer/service.asmx</AuthServer>

```

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-02
OK a couple of things to try, fire up a terminal and type
```
glxinfo | grep GL_ARB_multitexture
```
Hopefully you will see some output (from the problematic line in the log it is this extension that seems to be failing and returning the wrong value).

Using regedit create the key
```
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\OpenGL\DisabledExtensions = GL_ARB_multitexture
```
you'll probably have to create from OpenGL onwards. This disables the use of that extension.

Give it another go, if it works then I'd try removing and reinstalling your graphics drivers just in case something is damaged, if you have a spare video card try that (not many people do).

Either way it is probably worth raising a bug over at [http://winehq.org](http://winehq.org) (unless reinstalling the graphics drivers fixes it of course).

---

### Post by Posh on 2007-08-03

### Post by SNy on 2007-08-06
> **maltrin said:**
> P.S. This is not the modified script, this is the SNy script from [His appdb page]("http://appdb.winehq.org/appview.php?iVersionId=8273").

The reason I am asking is that the error looks much the same as before the script included support for the US servers, which differ in that they only accept XML requests (SOAP). See around page 10 for more info.

### Post by maltrin on 2007-08-06
> **SNy said:**
> Uh, what exactly does that mean? Did you download the script from the URL contained in my signature (or the AppDB  link, FWIW) or did you get it elsewhere?

The reason I am asking is that the error looks much the same as before the script included support for the US servers, which differ in that they only accept XML requests (SOAP). See around page 10 for more info.

SNy

Woohoo! =D Thanks so much. I was useing the wrong script. :mad: Only thing that I had to do to get it to work was what thealb suggested:

> **thealb said:**
> Any idea why this is happening?

Please enter your account details now.
account: 
password: 
Requesting GLS authentication ticket...
14:35:37 URL:[https://gls.lotro.com/GLS.AuthServer/Service.asmx](https://gls.lotro.com/GLS.AuthServer/Service.asmx) [1741/1741] -> ".launcher/GLSAuthServer.config" [1]
Logged in.

The following servers are available:
        0:      Meneldor
        1:      Brandywine
        2:      Windfola
        3:      Gladden
        4:      Silverlode
        5:      Elendilmir
        6:      Landroval
        7:      Arkenstone
        8:      Firefoot
        9:      Nimrodel
        10:     Vilya
Your choice (enter the number on the left)? 8
14:35:38 URL:[http://launcher.turbine.com/serverinfo.php?s=206.16.158.125](http://launcher.turbine.com/serverinfo.php?s=206.16.158.125) [916/916] -> ".launcher/server.config" [1]

Connecting to world login queue for realm Firefoot...
14:35:39 URL:[https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php](https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php) [127/127] -> ".launcher/WorldQueue.config" [1]

Error: World login queue response indicates failure.

--------------

UPDATE:
I did some debugging on this because I SO wanted this to work.
I subscribe to both DDO and LOTRO from turbine. The lotrolauncher script didn't select the LOTRO subscription. So, here is the change I made:
Changed:
accountNum=`grep -h "<Name>" .launcher/GLSAuthServer.config | sed -e "s/^.*<Name>//;s/<\/Name>.*$//"`
To:
accountNum=`grep -h "<Name>" .launcher/GLSAuthServer.config | sed -e "s/^.*<Game>LOTRO<\/Game><Name>//;s/<\/Name>.*$//"`

Once I put this in, everything works nicely.

Thanks a lot for all the hard work guys!

Thanks a ton again for the fast response. =)

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-10
> **buffcorePhil said:**
>  but before I get to character selection, I get a black screen saying the connection to the server has been lost.
That error is normally always related to files being out of date, I know they did a change yesterday while they did maintenance.

Other things to check are that you hi-res graphics are up to date (ie start the game under windows switch to very high or ultra high and see if you get any messages), check that the user you are running it as under linux has write permission on the various .dat files (it updates the time stamp when playing for some reason).

Finally if that doesn't work try deleting the files on the linux partition and recopying them from windows (I have a niggly feeling that sometimes Ubuntu doesn't do overwriting files correct all the time but I've yet to prove it).

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-10
> **HeWhoWalks said:**
> 
```

PE        340000-  355000       Deferred        vorbisfile

```

Anyone have any idea what might be going on?

That line in the crash log suggests a sound problem, try playing with the options on the audo tag in winecfg, ticking the emulation box is a good one to try and swapping between ALSA and OSS.

Another possible problem could be a dodgy memory chip, if playing with the audio settings doesn't work try booting into memtest and leaving it for a few hours to see if your memory is OK.

---

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-08-11

EDIT: It does indeed, shiny water a plenty

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-14
The wine developers are doing a very good job, considering directx is not the nicest of things to try to reverse engineer (too many undocumented features). But us users can help by filing bug reports when we hit glitches, then we can supply them with the information they need to correct the issues.

The bug with switching to windowed mode has been fixed so come wine-0.9.44 that should be ok again. But if you find a glitch, raise a bug and let me know so I can add it to the AppDB page bug tracker.

---

### Post by Posh on 2007-08-14
I thought I would post what the current issues I'm having running LOTRO under wine are.

- Antialiasing & Anisotropic Filtering - can't be turned on in game or forced via Nvidia Control Panel.

- Stencil Shadows - Causes all kinds of graphical glitches

- VideoPostEffects - Causes all kinds of graphical glitches

- Landscape Shadows can't be set to high - Causes areas to turn black

- Distant Imposters - Doesn't seem to work

- using Wine built for AMD64 the cursor doesn't render properly.  Works fine using i386 build of wine

The last issue is interesting as I'm not sure what is causing it.  I would use the i386 build on my amd64 install except it causes the game to use both screens (I have dual monitors) instead of just one like the amd64 does.  I haven't had any success with devilspie in getting this remedied.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-14
> **Freakmanshows said:**
> I'm using windows XP. When I launch LOTRO I get an Error message that says "Hardware texture compression support was not detected. This video card feature is required to run the game. [129] What do I do to fix this?
Err do you mean you are using wine set as windows xp or you are using windows xp? If it is the first one then change the OS to windows 2000 and check which video drivers you have installed. If it is the latter, try going to the official LOTRO forums ([www.lotro.com](www.lotro.com) or [www.lotro-europe.com](www.lotro-europe.com)) or maybe find a windows support forum.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-14
> **Posh said:**
> - using Wine built for AMD64 the cursor doesn't render properly.  Works fine using i386 build of wine

I had that problem read [http://wiki.winehq.org/WineOn64bit](http://wiki.winehq.org/WineOn64bit) and check out the bit about creating the sym links in /usr/lib32, what I did was check in usr/lib32 to see if that had a .so.* version and created the .so pointing to that.

---

### Post by Posh on 2007-08-15
> **ajackson said:**
> I had that problem read [http://wiki.winehq.org/WineOn64bit](http://wiki.winehq.org/WineOn64bit) and check out the bit about creating the sym links in /usr/lib32, what I did was check in usr/lib32 to see if that had a .so.* version and created the .so pointing to that.

Interesting... I have hundred or more of files like libXcursor.so.1 that then point to libXcursor.so.1.0.2... but no libXcursor.so file.  Any quick command that can add 100+ symbolic links for me automatically :).

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-15
> **Posh said:**
> Interesting... I have hundred or more of files like libXcursor.so.1 that then point to libXcursor.so.1.0.2... but no libXcursor.so file.  Any quick command that can add 100+ symbolic links for me automatically :).
You only need the ones that the ./configure of wine mentions which for me was around 10-15 (can't remember but it was in that region).

Edit: and you only need a link for the top level one so you would need libXcursor.so pointing to libXcursor.so.1 and don't need to worry about the .so.1.0.2 file at all.

---

### Post by Silenz on 2007-08-18
they're working.. download and unpack them to
**home/*YOURNAME*/The Lord of the Rings Online/ui/skins/*SKINNAME***
and select them in the game... it works

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-18
> **enigma83 said:**

As silenz says try the /home/username directory.

If you fire up winecfg and look on the Desktop Integration (I think that is it's name) tabe you'll see where My Docs points to, the default is your home directory.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-20
> **Garyu said:**
> don't have time to read all of the posts here
Good job I don't take that attitude eh? :)

Getting stuck on loading screens could well be an issue either server side or on the net. I know when I play DDO the only place I get hang ups is when swapping in and out of dungeons and it is a known problem.

In LotRO I would guess that depending on the server the same could be the problem, if the server is under heavy load then the sudden change in amount of data you need from the server (ie when you jump into an entire new area) is large and if several hundreds are doing it as well the delays happen.

### Post by Garyu on 2007-08-20
> **ajackson said:**
> Getting stuck on loading screens could well be an issue either server side or on the net. I know when I play DDO the only place I get hang ups is when swapping in and out of dungeons and it is a known problem.

I know for (pretty) sure that it is not a server-side issue. Played from windows fine, then switched to ubuntu and got stuck on loading screens. Installed the latest nvidia drivers, did a reboot and got through a couple of loading screens, but then got stuck in bree where there was heavy load. Kill the game proc, start up again, getting stuck a second time in bree. Repeat process, getting stuck in a loading screen for AH. Repeat process got stuck again at horsey man in east Bree. Repeat process, got stuck east of ost guruth when entering the Haragmaur (?) swamp. Switched back to windows, everything works fine. 

Seems to me, there are problems whenever there is a heavy system load. Maybe it will work fine if I set graphics levels to ultra low, but I want to enjoy the graphics of the game too, not just the gameplay. The thing is that in between getting stuck, graphics flow better than in windows. Everything is much snappier and faster in linux, until I get to a point where there is very heavy load and everything fails.

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-08-20
Hm, did you set the amount of your graphics memory properly as per the instructions in the AppDB-HowTo?
It used to just crash when using the default 64MB and entering crowded areas (ie AH in Bree or Bree itself, FWIW, needing to load up lots of textures for all the player characters, items and such). Maybe the wine team improved that part to not crash (but naturally slow down while trying to load everything and discarding other stuff in the process).

I at least have very good performance, I even went into helegrod with a group of 24 and albeit it did lag quite a bit in the actual battles (unsurprisingly with all the additional monsters and special spell effects and whatnot, when set to ultra-high [all but the textures]) it did not load unreasonably long and it didn't stall or crash either.

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-08-24
**0.9.44**
Just had a quick 5 mins with this.

- Starting in windowed mode still doesn't work
- StencilShadows still makes the world go dark and things to look odd
- Switching between full screen and windowed mode works again now but I found I had to alt-tab a couple of times to be able to click the OK to accept the new settings before the timer ran out
- Help, make sure you switch to windowed mode first or you get a funny blue flashing border, still doesn't show up unless you move your mouse over it and it briefly flickers to life and then goes black again
- Post processing, in my short 10 min run around Bree I didn't get any obvious glitches so I will tentatively say this is OK now

Overall the game feels smoother and the sound a bit crisper (I no longer get a strange static hiss when the game initialises the audio).

Edit: Played for an hour or so with post processing enabled, no glitches so it seems that option no longer needs altering.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-02
> **BatPenguin said:**
> Edit: Actually, the screen also says "Connection the server has been lost" in addition to the Quit button.
The most common cause of this is files not being up to date, I think there is a problem somewhere with the patching of hi-res textures files, sometimes you have to start the game (in windows) select a non-hi-res graphics mode, quit, restart and reselect a hi-res graphics mode to force the game to recognise that the file needs patching so that it can relaunch the original launcher and make it update the file.

I think it was on the previous page that I put a post about a change you could make to either the script or gui to check if this is in fact the problem (using the flag --HighResOutOfDate or something like that).

I'm going to tweak the gui so that you can set that flag via the options page to save having to alter the source code but if that is what is causing the problem you need to force the windows launcher to update the files for you.

I also think that occasionally when overwriting files (especially when doing a lot of them) that sometimes one isn't copied. I have never proved it but it has happened a couple of times for me but that could just that I didn't select all the files in the first place.

---

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-09-06
I think I've said this before, but I get frequent game crashes if i enable settings that seem to work well for other people. What happens is that the game disappears but for a small square in the bottom left hand corner of the WineDesktop, but continues to run (you can hear the in game sounds) until you 'killall wineserver'.

The wine error message is:

```
wine: Call from 0x7b843f40 to unimplemented function faultrep.dll.ReportFault, aborting
```

I'm using the latest version of wine and the most recent nvidia drivers installed via Envy.

Has anyone else experienced this problem?
Have you fixed it and how?
Does anyone have any ideas whatsoever?



By the way, if you're getting lines of landscape unrendered, turn down Landscape Shadows to 'Low'.

A small addition to the startup script:
Replacing
```
wine program.exe arguments
```
with
```
wine explorer /desktop=LOTRO,1280x800 program.exe arguments
```
eliminates the need to 'globally' emulate a wine desktop, and thus all bugs switching to window mode become unencounterable

To add this to ajackson's wonderful gtk launcher, go to Tools>Options. In the parameters box, type 'explorer /desktop=LOTRO,1280x800'.
Change the resolution to whatever you like, but remember to set the resolution the same in your lotro UserPreferences.ini file.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-07
> **sprocket1985 said:**
> I think I've said this before, but I get frequent game crashes if i enable settings that seem to work well for other people. What happens is that the game disappears but for a small square in the bottom left hand corner of the WineDesktop, but continues to run (you can hear the in game sounds) until you 'killall wineserver'.

The wine error message is:

```
wine: Call from 0x7b843f40 to unimplemented function faultrep.dll.ReportFault, aborting
```

Someone else kept getting that error message and it turned out they were missing a library or something. I've just done a locate faultrep and got the following two entries of note

```

/usr/local/lib/wine/faultrep.dll.so
/usr/i586-mingw32msvc/lib/libfaultrep.a

```
It could be that when you did the configure part it failed to pick up whatever faultrep relies on.

I have however encountered the style of crash you are getting (sound continues but the only bit of client to remain is a small square) when I was doing my testing of settings, it seemed to occur every time I ran out of graphics memory, in my case everytime I was in Bree looking down the hill by the Prancing Pony with VeryHigh textures on. Double check that the amount of memory you have set in the registry is correct and not too high (I seem a master of typos so I've done that one).

---

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-09-09
Hmmm, I don't appear to have that second file you listed. Any ideas why?
Thanks

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-09-10
Mingw32 is a cross-compiler for linux that creates normal windows executables. Unless you have it installed you will lack that second file.

Regarding the error, it rather seems that it is not the .dll itself missing but just that function "ReportFault". It is either not present in wine in general and all of us (but you) are lucky enough to never get the error or it is just missing in your version. Do you use a pre-built wine binary?

HTH,
SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-12
I know for 0.9.43 they did a major overhaul of the underlying stuff that their d3d stuff uses, it has caused a few problems (wine bug traffic jumped nearly 300% the first 3 days of the release).

I'd definately keep using the latest nVidia drivers that match your card as they fix bugs along the way with them. Other things you can try is a memtest to see if your memory is ok and maybe reboot when you crash and call up the bios computer health screen (or whatever it is called), see if your rig is overheating.

If you can compile your own version of wine I would say do it as then it is setup to an exact match of your system, for most apps a pre-compiled binary doesn't matter that much but for stuff like wine that is translating system calls I always feel happier knowing that it was built using my system (can't base my reasoning on any firm evidence though).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-16
**Fix for 0.9.45**
Seems to be a general problem caused by a partial implementation of a new part of directx functionality.

Apply the following patches in the order given and the game will now run. Environmental stencil shadows is still not working right and the help function is still dodgy.

[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043859.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043859.html")
[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043864.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043864.html")
[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043895.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043895.html")

---

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-09-16
> **ajackson said:**
> **Fix for 0.9.45**
Seems to be a general problem caused by a partial implementation of a new part of directx functionality.

Apply the following patches in the order given and the game will now run. Environmental stencil shadows is still not working right and the help function is still dodgy.

[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043859.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043859.html")
[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043864.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043864.html")
[http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043895.html]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-September/043895.html")

To patch the source code:
1) Navigate to your wine source code directory and copy the three patches here
2)
```
patch -p1 --dry-run <attachment.patch
```
If all goes to plan (i.e. no errors are reported):
```
patch -p1 <attachment.patch
```
3) Repeat for the other two patches (don't forget to do them in order).

Incidentally, I've regressed my nvidia driver back to 9755 without any joy. I cannot work out what is causing the random game crashing. I've even gone so far as taking apart my computer and putting it back together!

### Post by Guinivere on 2007-09-18
I just started trying to get LOTRO working in wine yesterday, and I was impressed at how well it -did- do for the first try.  I'm using Xorg on Debian Etch (I know this is the Ubuntu forum, but I'm hoping that someone might have an inkling of an idea of what might be going wrong, and this is the most extensive LOTRO-in-Linux thread I've seen).

I compiled wine 0.9.45 from source with --enable-opengl and --with-x11 or whatever those two options are.

I have fglrx drivers installed for my ATI Radeon 1600X 256MB.  'planetpenguin-racer' runs at a steady 38-42fps.

I've done all the tweaks to the UserPreferences.ini file that need to be done and the CLI launcher works splendidly.

I've made the wine registry entries that were recommended for LOTRO.

My machine is a 3GHz (256KB cache) Celeron D with 1.2GB of RAM.

The game loads, the splash screens look beautiful.  The initial videos (the turbine logo, etc.) are very choppy and have no sound.  That doesn't really matter to me, but it's something to consider in analysis.

The character screen comes up and runs at, I'd say, about 10FPS maybe.  Again, that doesn't matter to me at the moment, because the main thing that's keeping me from playing the game right now is the fact that the menus have a really weird 'squashed' effect to them.

The character and the background are proportioned properly, but all of the elements of the GUI are 'shoved up,' looking like an image that has had its vertical size shortened in GIMP without the horizontal size being changed appropriately.

I have to actually hover my cursor a fair distance below buttons I want to press.  The buttons will highlight when I move the cursor to the area corresponding to where the buttons *should* be but aren't because of the GUI squashing.

Needless to say, this is unplayable and I can't proceed with performance tweaks until I've overcome this strange hurdle.

I have wine using a 1024x768 desktop and I've got LOTRO running fullscreen at 1024x768.  Switching to windowed mode doesn't help.

Is this some kind of bizarre video driver problem, you think, or did I compile Wine badly?  I don't want to pay the cash to try it in Cedega, so testing it there is not currently an option.

EDIT:  I decided to include a screenshot; note where the tiny box 'enter middle earth' is below the character select box.  That's where I have to hold the cursor to highlight the 'Enter Middle Earth' button in the box.  Strange, eh?

EDIT 2:  Included another screenshot of what the game looks like when I've actually entered it.  Dear me.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-18
> **Guinivere said:**
> I'm using Xorg on Debian Etch (I know this is the Ubuntu forum
No worries there there are users with different distros who use this thread to ask questions and the like.

> I compiled wine 0.9.45 from source with --enable-opengl and --with-x11 or whatever those two options are.
Did you get those options from a guide or something? As far as I know you should just be able to do a straight ./configure under etch.

> The game loads, the splash screens look beautiful.  The initial videos (the turbine logo, etc.) are very choppy and have no sound. 
Double check what the windows version is set to in winecfg (it should be windows 2000) and see what the audio settings are (have a play with them see if you get better results).

> The character and the background are proportioned properly, but all of the elements of the GUI are 'shoved up,' looking like an image that has had its vertical size shortened in GIMP without the horizontal size being changed appropriately.
Did you add the three patches listed in a previous post, it could be that you don't get the crash they fix but get glitches instead (or if you did include them try again without them, it should crash but you never know).

> I have wine using a 1024x768 desktop and I've got LOTRO running fullscreen at 1024x768.  Switching to windowed mode doesn't help.
Try not running wine in desktop mode see if that changes anything, it shouldn't but worth a test.

> I don't want to pay the cash to try it in Cedega, so testing it there is not currently an option.
Cedega can't actually run the game at the moment for some strange reason.

Have a look at [http://wiki.winehq.org/Recommended_Packages](http://wiki.winehq.org/Recommended_Packages) and check that you have all the required packages installed, try just using a straight ./configure. Hopefully one of those will get you going.

### Post by Guinivere on 2007-09-18
> **ajackson said:**
> Did you get those options from a guide or something? As far as I know you should just be able to do a straight ./configure under etch.

I did get the options from a guide, yes.  I compiled wine fresh from scratch today with the patches and no extra flags on ./configure.

> **ajackson said:**
> Double check what the windows version is set to in winecfg (it should be windows 2000) and see what the audio settings are (have a play with them see if you get better results).

It is Windows 2000; I tried it with other versions today and got some unusual, but less functional, effects.  For example, when I try it in Windows XP mode, the entire screen - not just the GUI elements - is squashed up as if it were resized strangely in an imaging program.  Couldn't use the mouse or keyboard in XP mode.

> **ajackson said:**
> Did you add the three patches listed in a previous post, it could be that you don't get the crash they fix but get glitches instead (or if you did include them try again without them, it should crash but you never know).

Installed the patches.  I also reinstalled my ATI drivers.  The video performance of character animations, etc. improved dramatically, but the GUI glitch remains.

> **ajackson said:**
> Try not running wine in desktop mode see if that changes anything, it shouldn't but worth a test.

Tried.  No dice.

> **ajackson said:**
> Have a look at [http://wiki.winehq.org/Recommended_Packages](http://wiki.winehq.org/Recommended_Packages) and check that you have all the required packages installed, try just using a straight ./configure. Hopefully one of those will get you going.

I got all of those packages before recompiling wine today.  I suspect it may have helped contribute to the video performance boost mentioned above.

I have to admit that I'm not too familiar with the patching utility, though, and sometimes I worry whether I'm using it correctly.  All three patches modified only one file, and that was something called 'surface.c'.  Was it supposed to say it was doing any more than that?

--

So, overall, there is some progress.  I've attached a screenshot of what the game looks like now; there are less bizarre artifacts, fortunately.

I suspect the GUI issue may have something to do with the 'vertex shading' and 'pixel shading' options.

**Vertex shading off, pixel shader off**:  GUI only squashed upward as in the screenshots of my original post.  3D models, etc. are fine.

**Vertex shading 'Hardware', pixel shader off**:  As above.

**Vertex shading off, pixel shader on**:  As above.

**Vertex shading 'Hardware', pixel shader on**:  Very very very dark, highly distorted login screen (see dark screenshot) with squashed GUI.

--

I'm happy with the progress I've made, that's for sure.  I'm starting to run out of visible options as far as things I can try, though!

I do appreciate the advice you've given me thus far.  I hope that if I do get my issue resolved here, it'll help people in the future who might have the same problem.

EDIT:  It occurs to me that I should include the error messages I'm getting in the wine terminal when I run LOTRO.

```

Ready. Now starting the client...
err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered
err:ole:CoGetClassObject no class object {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} could be created for context 0x1

err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered
err:ole:CoGetClassObject no class object {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} could be created for context 0x1

```

It is a widely held view that the ATI drivers are poor on linux but I'm sure others on here have ATI cards who maybe able to shed a little light on any quirks they have noticed.

> I have to admit that I'm not too familiar with the patching utility, though, and sometimes I worry whether I'm using it correctly.  All three patches modified only one file, and that was something called 'surface.c'.  Was it supposed to say it was doing any more than that?

### Post by SNy on 2007-09-19
ajackson wrote:

> It is a widely held view that the ATI drivers are poor on linux

That is an understatement. Regardless of all the promises ATi made so far, the driver has been really crappy for years.
As AMD has now come forward and said that they will open the specs and come up with some newly created framework of their own Real Soon Now (TM), maybe there is hope. But, call me pessimistic if you like, my personal record of ATi and their drivers leaves me with enough doubts to not consider buying one of their cards anytime soon again.
Switched from an X800 to the 6800GT I have now and while it is technically a tad slower, overall functionality and performance under linux is so much better.

> but I'm sure others on here have ATI cards who maybe able to shed a little light on any quirks they have noticed.

I have tried to get LOTRO to run on a buddy's rig some six weeks ago. While I can't remember any of these nasty glitches, I can tell you that performance was simply horrible. It somehow took ages to load and then came across with what seemed more like seconds per frame than the other way around.

I am bit unsure about the latest version of the driver, 8.41.7. There had been various reports about it improving performance a lot, even on older cards.
The release notes, however, claim that it should only be used for the HD2xxx series and older cards should be kept at the old driver. Then again even in the release notes, they list certain options to check for the older cards... very strange.

SNy

> **enigma83 said:**
> Is it possible to "get" the high res files, if you don't have a real windows install to play with?  All I have is a vmware'd Windows 2000 machine, so I can't start the game to select any high res options.

```



[Render]

LandscapeShoreEffects=High

ModelDetail=UltraHigh

DisplayAdapter=0

### Post by Guinivere on 2007-09-20
> **SNy]Switched from an X800 to the 6800GT I have now and while it is technically a tad slower, overall functionality and performance under linux is so much better.[/quote]

I've been a little wary of going out and buying a new video card specifically for Linux, especially since my computer is a bit outdated and it doesn't have any PCIe slots.

I do still run Windows XP as a security blanket for when I can't get my favorite games running in Linux said:**
> Not that I know what the latest version of the ATI driver is but what version have you got installed? I use Envy ([http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)) for installing my drivers, it handles ATI as well so might be worth looking at.

I have driver 8.40.4 for x86 Linux on ATI Radeon X1600.  [http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html](http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html)

I'm pretty sure they're installed correctly since 'direct rendering' says yes in glxinfo and planetpenguin-racer runs pretty decently at about 40fps.  I'd like it to run faster, but, I attribute the fact that it's relatively slow to the fact that ATI drivers for Linux aren't so great.  My card is also one that was considered 'midrange' when it was made, so I guess it's kind of slow in the first place in comparison to a lot of other stuff out there...

### Post by sprocket1985 on 2007-09-20
> Interesting how I got an 'out of memory' message.  Maybe I didn't set it right in the registry?  Here's a screenshot; let me know if these values that I put in the registry look okay.

That might be because the registry is case sensitive. Try changing the key name from 'DIRECT3D' to 'Direct3D'. I had a problem a while ago that turned out to be because I'd written 'Direct3d' (note lower case d) by accident.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-20
> **Guinivere said:**
> Interesting how I got an 'out of memory' message.  Maybe I didn't set it right in the registry?  Here's a screenshot; let me know if these values that I put in the registry look okay.
As sprocket said change it to Direct3D and it should look a bit better (hopefully).

> That said, can anyone recommend a nice fast nvidia AGP card that can play LOTRO and perhaps Oblivion really well in Linux?  :D
I'd say anything with at least a GeForce 7xxx should handle both games well (and I think most have a AGP version as well as PCI-E version). Go for one with atleast 256MB memory on it though. In the past I've had a couple from a company called BFG who tend to make overclocked versions of the normal cards (so have a little extra oomph) but I also find the PNY ones quite good (I have a PNY version of a 7600GT does a nice job, certainly good enough for my needs.

### Post by SNy on 2007-09-21
Guinivere wrote:

> At this point, I think I'm willing to accept - unless we make some major breakthrough -
> that I probably won't even want to pursue trying to play LOTRO on Linux with my ATI card.

The breakthrough would have to be coming from ATi or the new open source initiative, driver wise.

If you wait long enough, maybe the open source driver that has recently sprung to live [1] - written from scratch using newly published ATi information - and that already knows how to operate the 2D world to a major extent will reach a state comparable to that of the NVidia drivers.
One can hope that the information suffices and enough talented and motivated people get to work and get it to work. :)

[1] [http://www.phoronix.com/scan.php?page=article&item=842&num=1](http://www.phoronix.com/scan.php?page=article&item=842&num=1)

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-21
Came across [http://www.uesp.net/wiki/Oblivion:Linux](http://www.uesp.net/wiki/Oblivion:Linux), looks like a high end 6xxx series card handles Oblivion well and I'm fairly sure it will handle LOTRO fine as well, might be a slightly cheaper option but if you can get a mid to high end 7xxx (don't think the 8xxx series has AGP versions) it would last longer (ie not go out of date as quick).

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2007-09-21
> **ajackson said:**
> Came across [http://www.uesp.net/wiki/Oblivion:Linux](http://www.uesp.net/wiki/Oblivion:Linux), looks like a high end 6xxx series card handles Oblivion well and I'm fairly sure it will handle LOTRO fine as well, might be a slightly cheaper option but if you can get a mid to high end 7xxx (don't think the 8xxx series has AGP versions) it would last longer (ie not go out of date as quick).

I could actually comment about that a bit: I have an Athlon XP 2800 (2 GB RAM) and the 256 MB Geforce6800 Ultra AGP (fastest one they made, I believe). In Windows, I get very nice performance with both Oblivion and LOTRO but under Cedega Oblivion was much slower for me and required turning down graphics a lot, and other things like Cedega & Battlefield 2 / Half-Life 2 are clearly much slower, as well. Playable, I guess, but not really very enjoyable. LOTRO I didn't actually even ever get to work under wine, kept having black screens even after updating graphics, so I can't really comment on the speed since I just gave up...but Oblivion was clearly much slower than Windows. Enough that I thought the hassle of dual-boot was worth the nicer game experience.

So I dunno, unless you can get one for very cheap, I would probably advice against trusting that the 6800 AGP is fine for playing these games under wine. Windows certaintly, but according to my experience, the speed difference was very large under wine. Just my two eurocents here, others might get good performance with similar hardware, I suppose. My drivers etc. are fine, native Linux games (Quake 4, Doom 3) work just great, so I do think the peformance I have is probably close to what anyone with a similar machine should get.

I'm upgrading my machine in a month or so, so I'll just try to get LOTRO to work under wine then and I'll be back crying for help then if it doesn't work :). At the moment I'll suffer the dual-booting into XP while I wait for my new hardware to arrive.

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-09-21
Hm, as stated before, I do have a 6800GT working just fine here with LOTRO, very high details except post processing and no ultra high textures (too much loading and only 256 MB VRAM) at 1680 x 1050. Performance isn't really much different from Windows and very good.
About the only other half-decent AGP NVidia card left is the 7800 GS which is a bit faster than mine but came at a higher price at the time I switched mine.

### Post by SNy on 2007-09-26
Hey, ajackson.

I made a little modification to your launcher before compiling it because I found that not being able to use umlauts has been caused by my environment being utf8 which wine apparently can't handle properly.
So I added the environment variable LANG with "de_DE" to the Run() method, statically for now. Calling the GUI launcher with the setting doesn't work without a lot of additional hassle as it then attempts to have all of the UI, gtk and even perl stuff (wonder where that is coming from) in that locale, which it complains missing or not configured.
If you could either change the WINEDEBUG setting in the options to be a string of multiple environment settings "ENV=VAR" and split it up or make it so that you could otherwise add additional ones, that would be great.

Otherwise, keep up the good work. :)

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-26
SNy,

The problem with LANG that you mention seems specific to using wine on a 64bit version of linux (something to do with locale being 64bit and wine being 32bit).

Try the following
```

ln -s /usr/lib/locale /usr/lib32/

```

It should get wine to recognise what the correct settings for languages are.

---

I have checked the following things -

[*]All reg key settings have been entered correctly and with the correct spelling
[*]I have tried all the detail settings on low - No Change

Any thoughts? Wine ver is 0.9.43 64bit.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-27
> **Fitzy_oz said:**
> I have weird one guys, While playing lotro, the game periodically slows to a crawl and then within a few seconds, returns to normal. 

I even put beryl on so I could make the window partially transparent and watch the cpu monitor - it peaks out at around 110% when the issue occurs and then returns to steady 60%. 
Do you have any unusual processes running in the background or are you on a local network and someone (or maybe just another computer) is accessing yours for some reason?

It could be that the game still has a memory leak problem but linux being slightly better at handling memory is doing a memory purge which eats a bit of processor time. Or some strange thing with the computer swapping stuff from RAM to virtual RAM or some other strange automated task.

Try doing it again but watching the running processes instead, see if anything jumps up the list when the slowdown occurs.

---

### Post by Fitzy_oz on 2007-09-28
I was watching for anything and didn't see anything unusual and my pc is the only PC on the network at home, and the wifi is mac address filtered.  It only appeared to be the lotorclient.exe process that jumped up with cpu usage.  I might just wipe wine out and start again and see if that fixes it.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-28
Since there doesn't seem to be any external program suddenly hogging resources my guess would be garbage collection (ie resource freeing) in either wine or LOTRO, in UserPrefs there is an option (under troubleshooting) called Agressive something-or-other, try setting that to High or Medium see if it helps at all. It could be that LOTRO is waiting too long between clearing junk that when it does it it doesn't do it quietly.

I think the new wine is out today which hopefully has the fixes that made LOTRO crash on startup in so if it was a wine issue it might have been fixed already.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2007-09-28
**0.9.46**

New version of wine out today, good news for LOTRO as without any additional patches the game now launches fine and StencilShadows now works. I was able to select High on the general graphics page of the options menu and suffered no darkening of the screen or weird door being misplaced graphics.

Help still doesn't work properly and you still need to launch the game in full screen mode but apart from that everything seems fine, haven't had an extended play just a quick run around Bree.

With Mono-1.2.5 for Windows installed I am getting better results trying to run the original launcher, now getting proper error messages rather than a blank crash. Seems to be complaining about not finding a font family so I'll see if I can work out which font it needs and see what happens then.

### Post by ipx on 2007-10-02
> **Guinivere said:**
> I just started trying to get LOTRO working in wine yesterday, and I was impressed at how well it -did- do for the first try.  I'm using Xorg on Debian Etch (I know this is the Ubuntu forum, but I'm hoping that someone might have an inkling of an idea of what might be going wrong, and this is the most extensive LOTRO-in-Linux thread I've seen).

I compiled wine 0.9.45 from source with --enable-opengl and --with-x11 or whatever those two options are.

I have fglrx drivers installed for my ATI Radeon 1600X 256MB.  'planetpenguin-racer' runs at a steady 38-42fps.

I've done all the tweaks to the UserPreferences.ini file that need to be done and the CLI launcher works splendidly.

I've made the wine registry entries that were recommended for LOTRO.

My machine is a 3GHz (256KB cache) Celeron D with 1.2GB of RAM.

The game loads, the splash screens look beautiful.  The initial videos (the turbine logo, etc.) are very choppy and have no sound.  That doesn't really matter to me, but it's something to consider in analysis.

The character screen comes up and runs at, I'd say, about 10FPS maybe.  Again, that doesn't matter to me at the moment, because the main thing that's keeping me from playing the game right now is the fact that the menus have a really weird 'squashed' effect to them.

The character and the background are proportioned properly, but all of the elements of the GUI are 'shoved up,' looking like an image that has had its vertical size shortened in GIMP without the horizontal size being changed appropriately.

I have to actually hover my cursor a fair distance below buttons I want to press.  The buttons will highlight when I move the cursor to the area corresponding to where the buttons *should* be but aren't because of the GUI squashing.

Needless to say, this is unplayable and I can't proceed with performance tweaks until I've overcome this strange hurdle.

I have wine using a 1024x768 desktop and I've got LOTRO running fullscreen at 1024x768.  Switching to windowed mode doesn't help.

Is this some kind of bizarre video driver problem, you think, or did I compile Wine badly?  I don't want to pay the cash to try it in Cedega, so testing it there is not currently an option.

EDIT:  I decided to include a screenshot; note where the tiny box 'enter middle earth' is below the character select box.  That's where I have to hold the cursor to highlight the 'Enter Middle Earth' button in the box.  Strange, eh?

EDIT 2:  Included another screenshot of what the game looks like when I've actually entered it.  Dear me.

No, dont give up! Im experiencing the exact same problem!

Using ATI Radeon 9800pro 128mb with the latest ATI propertairy driver (8.40.4) with the latest wine. Did all the tweaks in registry and userconfig-file as it said in winehq.com. You are not alone with this problem!

Anyone got any idea?

### Post by ipx on 2007-10-03
> **Fitzy_oz said:**
> I would probably say regressing to the older driver (before the HD2000 enhancements), the performance will probably be worse but with any luck it should take care of the artifacts.

Trying to not sound stupid, but which version number is that? 8.28.8?

EDIT:

Actually I tested downloading and installing the latest ATI-driver that was actually created for HD2000 (8.41.7) and it increased my performance ALOT! But i still have these UI-glitches that Guinivere shows in his pictures. Ironically, the UI was not messed up before, but then it was all black and laggy (1-2fps) (see pic1).

EDIT2:

I want to describe my issues more detailed:
- The game flows at a decent fps (20-30?) and is fully playable in that aspect
- I get a few graphic glitches, but its a huge increase since i installed latest driver, and its only a few ones every here and there, not a big problem.
- The game UI is _VERY_ weird-looking and hard to use. Look at my attached pictures to see what i mean...
- The mouse isnt pointing right, I actually have to point a big down to the right to click on things, which is very annoying.
- The mini-map also looks messed up and does not seem to work right
- The movies is unwatchable (both video and sound work 1 second, then it freezes, repeat). I am actually experiencing the exact same problem as i do in windows when the lotro window is not 'targeted', so I assume this is what is causing it.
- Im not 100% about the sound yet, I could hear it in the movie sometimes but not ingame, but I havnt really bothered. (Im using OSS atm)

- The game flows at a decent fps (20-30?) and is fully playable in that aspect
- I get a few graphic glitches, but its a huge increase since i installed latest driver, and its only a few ones every here and there, not a big problem.
- The game UI is _VERY_ weird-looking and hard to use. Look at my attached pictures to see what i mean...
- The mouse isnt pointing right, I actually have to point a big down to the right to click on things, which is very annoying.
- The mini-map also looks messed up and does not seem to work right
- The movies is unwatchable (both video and sound work 1 second, then it freezes, repeat). I am actually experiencing the exact same problem as i do in windows when the lotro window is not 'targeted', so I assume this is what is causing it.
- Im not 100% about the sound yet, I could hear it in the movie sometimes but not ingame, but I havnt really bothered. (Im using OSS atm)

Picture1(How it looked with ati driver 8.40.8 ): 
[http://img522.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro1jm1.png]("http://img522.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro1jm1.png")

Picture2(Login-screen with newer driver): 
[http://img527.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro2ei6.png]("http://img527.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro2ei6.png")

Picture3(The UI with newer driver): 
[http://img266.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro3wa4.jpg]("http://img266.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro3wa4.jpg")

Picture4(The UI with newer driver, you can also see the minimap problem there): 
[http://img502.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro4wx4.jpg]("http://img502.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro4wx4.jpg")

Picture5(A little more 'funny' picture ^^):
[http://img264.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro5fx9.jpg]("http://img264.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lotro5fx9.jpg")


**A question for those who plays LotRO on linux and have ati; What driver version and wine version are you using?**

Sorry, pre-phoronix... Im not sure what number, I have'nt used ATi for some time

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-04
> **Garyu said:**
> [http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?&postid=1155183](http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?&postid=1155183)

[http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=93411](http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=93411)

From book 11, lotro will support directX 10. Will this have any affect on linux compatibility of the client?
The wine implementation of directx 10 is pretty non-existant but LOTRO will continue to work with directx 9 so there are no issues with the additional support of directx 10. If Turbine/CM stopped supporting directx 9 they would lose most of their customer base, while they have done some daft things in the past even they wouldn't do something that daft.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-05
I know the size and some of the content of the data being sent to the patch server but it is different for US and EU version (the EU version seems to send an additional package first), where it gets what it sends I don't know and how it is encrypted I don't know (but I'm fairly sure the encryption key is send with the inital request or maybe all of the requests/replies).

Using the native launcher has hit another set of errors under wine, there is someone working on getting wine and mono to co-operate but it is slow work. I do try installing .NET with every new wine release but don't get any joy out of it.

So my launcher is basically sticking where it is at the moment unless a change means that I need to make a modification.

### Post by sgoyette on 2007-10-05
Hi All,

I'm having some graphical issues with LOTRO in CrossOver.  Large parts of the landscape are see through and I've ensured that the stencil buffer settings are disabled.  I have an Nvidia 8800 GTS with 320 megs of memory (eVGA) and dual monitor setup.  The nVidia TwinView settings had me stumped earlier as the application would open across both LCDs.  I since worked around that by setting up a virtual window for the bottle (with or without the virtual makes no diff to the landscape issues btw).  Other then this, the performance is quite good...but the landscape being see through like it is makes the game un-playable :(

Can anyone think of anything to try?

Thanks,

Steve

---

### Post by Fitzy_oz on 2007-10-05
> **sgoyette said:**
> Hi All,

I'm having some graphical issues with LOTRO in CrossOver.  Large parts of the landscape are see through and I've ensured that the stencil buffer settings are disabled.  I have an Nvidia 8800 GTS with 320 megs of memory (eVGA) and dual monitor setup.  The nVidia TwinView settings had me stumped earlier as the application would open across both LCDs.  I since worked around that by setting up a virtual window for the bottle (with or without the virtual makes no diff to the landscape issues btw).  Other then this, the performance is quite good...but the landscape being see through like it is makes the game un-playable :(

Can anyone think of anything to try?

Thanks,

Steve

I would just try it with plain old wine, the latest version seems to have eliminated a lot of issues such as post processing, stencil shadows. I always found performance under crossover with most things was slower than Vanilla wine.  It's just a suggestion though.

### Post by drummingpariah on 2007-10-08
> **ipx said:**
> No, dont give up! Im experiencing the exact same problem!

Using ATI Radeon 9800pro 128mb with the latest ATI propertairy driver (8.40.4) with the latest wine. Did all the tweaks in registry and userconfig-file as it said in winehq.com. You are not alone with this problem!

Anyone got any idea?

If it's just on the videos, try changing your framerate (ingame, under the Troubleshooting tab) down to 59fps.  Also, try changing "allowfakefulscreen=true" to "allowfakefullscreen=false" in the userpreferences.ini file (assuming you haven't already)

> **drummingpariah said:**
> If it's just on the videos, try changing your framerate (ingame, under the Troubleshooting tab) down to 59fps.  Also, try changing "allowfakefulscreen=true" to "allowfakefullscreen=false" in the userpreferences.ini file (assuming you haven't already)

Thanks for trying to help, but none of those helped. :/ Im pretty sure its about the drivers now. There was a new wine release yesterday, 0.9.47, and it didnt fix anything for me (although jumping from driver version 8.40.4 to 8.41.7 gave me a HUGE fps-improvement even though it gave some graphic glitches) and the huge UI-glitches remains.

I ask once again;
**Is there any ATI-user out there that runs lotro playable on wine? What ati-driver and wine-version are you using?**

### Post by drummingpariah on 2007-10-13
> **ipx said:**
> Thanks for trying to help, but none of those helped. :/ Im pretty sure its about the drivers now. There was a new wine release yesterday, 0.9.47, and it didnt fix anything for me (although jumping from driver version 8.40.4 to 8.41.7 gave me a HUGE fps-improvement even though it gave some graphic glitches) and the huge UI-glitches remains.

I ask once again;
**Is there any ATI-user out there that runs lotro playable on wine? What ati-driver and wine-version are you using?**

Last try (for the wonky cinematics, anyway).  Try disabling sounds altogether.  Some sound drivers can cause real issues when playing videos (especially the Realtek drivers, for whatever reason).  It might not even be the video rendering that's causing problems, it could be the sound rendering.

### Post by deanus on 2007-10-15

ATI 9800 Pro
Wine 0.9.46 + Catalyst Drivers 8.40.4

LOTRO loads fine - running 1024x768 in a wine virtual desktop, good fps too. The only problem is that the UI isn't drawn properly. The character select screen looks fine but the game picks up the mouse cursor as being about an inch off where it appears to me. (see the first screenshot)

### Post by deanus on 2007-10-16
Fitzy_oz,

I was actually looking at doing that but not quite sure how. Normally, I should just be able to do this from System > Administration > Display (i'm running FC7 not Ubuntu, as mentioned above). However, as I have installed the catalyst drivers this apparently breaks the Display options in FC.

```

[Dean@localhost ~]$ /usr/bin/system-config-display
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/share/system-config-display/xconf.py", line 381, in <module>
    dialog = xConfigDialog.XConfigDialog(hardware_state, xconfig, rhpxl.videocard.VideoCardInfo())
  File "/usr/share/system-config-display/xConfigDialog.py", line 646, in __init__
    self.xml.get_widget("secondMonitorLabel").set_text(monitor_list[1].modelname)
TypeError: GtkLabel.set_text() argument 1 must be string, not None
```

So, I tried manually editing my X config (this is where my linux knowledge kind of fails me). My config /etc/X11/xorg.conf is as below.

```

# Xorg configuration created by system-config-display

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier     "single head configuration"
        Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
        InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Keyboard0"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Monitor1"
        VendorName   "Monitor Vendor"
        ModelName    "LCD Panel 1024x768"
        HorizSync    31.5 - 48.5
        VertRefresh  40.0 - 70.0
        Option      "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        Option      "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
        Option      "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Videocard0"
        Driver      "radeon"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Driver      "fglrx"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen0"
        Device     "Videocard0"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
        Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
        Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I tried manually editing both sets of 24 under the section "Screen" to 32 but X failed to load so I had to use a knoppix cd I had to mount and edit the values back to 24.

Can anyone suggest another way to change this value? I tried looking in the Catalyst Control Centre but there is no colour depth option.

Francesco

### Post by ipx on 2007-10-17

### Post by Garyu on 2007-10-20
OK... I've been trying to get this to work in gutsy gibbon. I've been following the WineHQ AppDB guide by ajackson.

1) Copied the updated LOTRO installation from windows to /home
2) Set winecfg to Windows2000
3) Clicked the Sound tab to get sound installed and tested in winecfg
4) Put the following keys in regedit:
```
HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/UseGLSL = enabled
HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize = < amount of video memory ie 256 >
```
5) Put this into UserPreferences.ini:
```
AllowFakeFullScreen=False
FullScreen=True
```
6) Dowloaded the scripts:
[http://bmx-chemnitz.de/%7Emfr/LOTRO/lotrolauncher.script](http://bmx-chemnitz.de/%7Emfr/LOTRO/lotrolauncher.script)
[http://www.shelldorado.de/scripts/cmds/urlencode.txt](http://www.shelldorado.de/scripts/cmds/urlencode.txt)
and renamed them both to *.sh but it would not work... (Error validating certificate, could not establish SSL-connection)
7) Downloaded and compiled and installed the GUI launcher from [www.lotrolinux.com](www.lotrolinux.com)
```
sudo aptitude install build-essential mono-mcs
./configure
make
```
When I run the launcher I get the login screen with the servers, then I log in and the first screen with the witch-king displays "experiences may vary". Then I am thrown out and I get this in the terminal:
```

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-22
> **ernie247 said:**
> Ok ive got a problem that ive been trying to sort out for along time now. Im on a restricted connection
There is a good chance that the ports the game needs to be open are not open if the connection itself is restricted in some way (at university by any chance? They do tend to block certain ports).

Unless you can persuade whoever looks after the connection to open the required ports your only other option is to look on the official forums to see if there is a way of altering the ports the game uses.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-23
> **ernie247 said:**
> Yup you guessed right :P 
I did ask for the ports to be unblocked however they said no due to the security risk ( they unblock most games just not lotro it seems ). However i have no trouble running  other applications  which are blocked by the uni connection because of the external proxy i connect to. This is the only thing that wont work. ( well it almost works its just the client itself that  wont authenticate for some reason, my client log says its not even getting the account details so its as if something is forcing it not to connect via my proxy  ). There really has to be a solution... just not a simple one.
I found the following on the official EU site about tweaking the set-up if you are using a proxy (if you are using the US version check for something similar on their forums but it should be the same).

```

Error accessing Service.asmx

Problem: The following web error occurred while trying to access ...service.asmx

Solution: Make sure you have installed the .NET framework 1.1 Service Pack 1 via the Windows Update site or a download from Microsoft's main site.

- Turn off any ad-aware programs you may be running.
- Try to log on - if you get the same or similar error, please proceed to next steps.
- Make a copy of the launcher.exe.config file before making the changes below and store that copied file someplace outside the LoTRO directory. This will allow you to quickly revert the changes.
- Open your launcher.exe.config file (Notepad will work)
- Right above "<appSettings>" in the launcher.exe.config file, add the following block:

<system.net>
<defaultProxy>
<proxy usesystemdefault="false"/>
</defaultProxy>
</system.net>

This will cause the launcher to bypass the proxy server settings set in Internet Options.
- Save the file and try to log in.

If that does not help, you might need to have very specific proxy settings for use with the game. Your settings might need to be adjusted even more with the additional flags stated in the MSDN article (link below), and your ISP will most likely be the ones able to help you with it.

http://msdn.microsoft.com/library/default.asp?url=/library/en-us/cpgenref/html/gngrfdefaultproxyelement.asp

```

### Post by BatPenguin on 2007-10-24
> **ajackson said:**
> 
Definate first thing to do is copy your linux UserPrefs to windows and see if it is selectable under that (if not see what differences there are between the original windows UserPrefs and the linux one).

OK, it actually looks like it's working now. Here's what I did:

- enabled antialiasing in the userprefs
- forced antialising on in the nvidia-settings program
- restarted the computer (for some strange reason restarting X didn't seem to be enough...but restart worked, weird)

...and now it looks like I have antialiasing on. The game still says "DISABLED" for it, but the graphics did change since edges look a lot nicer now so it must be working, I don't think any of the other graphics options could affect the edges like that. I'll play some more with the settings and try different antialiasing settings in the nvidia-settings program to double-check what looks best, but it definitely looks better now. At least on my machine and (rather low) resolution this doesn't seem to slow the game down at all. But the machine's brand new and pretty fast I guess, though: a Quad Core with 4 GB RAM and Nvidia 7950 GT with Gutsy and nvidia restricted driver, playing on a LCD TV at a resolution of 1360x768.

Thanks for the help!

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-26
> **XanderVincent said:**
> 
[www.lotrolinux.com](www.lotrolinux.com)

I can't get that way to work.
By "can't get that way to work" what exactly do you mean?

> Problem is in my regedit there is no /direct3D folder to change those settings in.
You have to create the Direct3D folder if it doesn't exist (also watch the case as the Windows registry is case sensitive), which it normally doesn't.

> That and I have no idea where to put those scripts. So yeah, I have no idea how to get that way to work.
If you are using the command line scripts (the ones from SNy's site) the AppDB page clearly states:

"These scripts must be placed in directory containing the file lotroclient.exe"

> Any idea at all? Please... god.. anyone. And yes I checked the other thread, but to me that was a bunch of jumple.
Out of interest which thread is that?

> I didn't understand any of it.
I feel that way reading this :)

> I just got Ubuntu, and don't know much. I know how to access the terminal, rename things, unpack things.. thats about it.

Please help me.
May I suggest that you find a guide that will show you the basics of using the command line, renaming, unpacking, etc as getting LOTRO to work under Linux isn't one of the easiest starting places if you are new to linux (not all stuff is this complicated but LOTRO is one of the awkward applications at the moment).

I didn't put this warning on the AppDB page for no reason.
> Also at present to get the game working requires a fair amount of fiddling with configuration files and wine/registry settings and a base knowledge of the Linux command line (checking permissions/ownership and changing permissions/ownership.

Welcome to the CLI launcher for LOTRO v0.9.6.
(C) 2007 by SNy

Reading launcher configuration...

account: x

I Tried using the script with the -no-check-certificate and it still dies.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-29
> **OlympicSoftworks said:**
> ..
ERROR: Certificate verification error for gls.lotro.com: unable to get local issuer certificate
To connect to gls.lotro.com insecurely, use `--no-check-certificate'.
Unable to establish SSL connection.

Error: Could not extract auth result from GLS auth server response.

That is a oddity caused by how wget has been compiled by the people doing the distros.

Edit the script that you run and search for wget. Stick the "--no-check-certificate" in the wget command lines (there are a few dotted around the script) and the error should stop occuring.

Edit: You should only need to change the wget after the line that rwads
```
echo "Requesting GLS authentication ticket..."
```

Also I noticed the use of "windoz", word of advice you want support from me don't use terms like that, windows helped bring computing to the masses, show it some respect. If you don't want to use it fine (I don't) but calling it silly names just gets us linux users a bad image.

---

Sounds like the copy of Lotro your using is out of date, you need to copy a fully patched installation from a windows virtual machine/installation as the Turbine launcher does all the patching and is currently incompatible with wine. ### Post by ajackson on 2007-10-30
There is an interesting thread on the EU forums ([http://community.codemasters.com/forum/showthread.php?t=229021&page=3](http://community.codemasters.com/forum/showthread.php?t=229021&page=3)) which has found out that it is the pixel shaders causing a problem with some of the older graphics cards (included some of the ones listed under recommended on the box).

It suggests using a tool to force the pixel shaders to be 1.4 or 2.0, I don't know if that tool would work under wine or if there is a registry setting that can force the change (the GLSL registry entry might be the one to try first).
