### Post by SNy on 2007-11-01
emerged wrote:

> ... and in very few minutes I even hat the lotro gui launcher ready and working with
> my (fresh patched and copied!!) lotro installation from my windoze sys...
> and what happens? hehe the same thing ...

It might be the high resolution textures again. The thing with the patching is that they don't patch the high res files automatically anymore. So, in order to update those, you need to log into the game using windows, activate high res textures in the options menu. It will then tell you it needs to restart and patch those.
(edit) Alternatively, you can also start the game so that it ignores the out-of-dateness. Do so by adding "--HighResOutOfDate" to the command invoking the script launcher (ie: "./lotrolauncher.script --HighResOutOfDate"). I am unsure about how to set this in ajackson's UI.

> i have further question... when using ls -a the hidden files are shown as well...

Yes, but the hidden directory with the downloaded files is only created by the launcher, not the game.

> am i so stupid in using cp -r lotro....
> and using it without an -a option (if that exists in this way here)

It does exist and it means "archive", keeps permissions and all and implies recursiveness.

> that i copy all the files except the hidden ones and so the game crashes at that point?

No worries, the concept of ".hidden" files does not exist in windows, they have an attribute for that.
Check and see if the high res stuff is the culprit.

good luck and best regards,
SNy

PS. My site is currently down due to the provider moving everything into a new datacenter. They told me *yesterday* in *a mail*, haven't even finished yet as proclaimed (naturally) and I am rather *_not_* amused.
Might be a bit until all DNS stuff has been updated as well, so you'll need to be patient if you want to get the script and stuff.

### Post by SNy on 2007-11-01
While patching under windows is still needed, you might try using the following little helper script to update the existing copy on linux without copying everything again, assuming you have it residing on some NTFS partition.

Place the following code in a bash script somewhere. Replace all the settings on top with values appropriate for your system, then run it using sudo to rsync the updates.

```

#!/bin/bash

DEV=/dev/sda2
MNT=/mnt
SRC="Lord of the Rings Online"
DEST="/usr/local/games/LOTRO"
USR=mfr
GRP=games

mount -tntfs -oro $DEV $MNT
rsync -rtuv "$MNT/$SRC/" "$DEST/"
find "$DEST/" -type f -exec chown $USR.$GRP {} \;
umount $MNT

```

SNy

PS. Server STILL down. Ticket request pending. :/

### Post by ajackson on 2007-11-02
> **emerged said:**
> the only thing I'd like to be able to is playing in windowed mode... time i try to run in windowed mode it tells me i should use 16 or more bit colordepth on my desktop...
That is something the wine guys are thinking about implementing, basically the application (LOTRO in this case) asks the system what colour depth it is running at, the system responds 24 (since xorg doesn't have 32 bit depth) and the application complains as it doesn't know what to do with 24 bit depth, so they are thinking of faking the return when the colour depth is 24 bit to fool the applications.

Once the game is running you can switch to windowed mode but you'll need to change it back to full screen every time you start the game.

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-11-02
That depends on which desktop environment you use and wether or not you have additional programs running at the moment.

If you start lotro with the script, chances are you have a terminal window open and can just Alt-Tab to that, giving you access to your desktop.
With gnome's default keybindings, you can also switch to another virtual desktop using Ctrl-Alt-RightCursor (or left one). That will work when you use ajackson's GUI which closes itself before starting the game and hence leaves no other windows if you just run lotro.

For other desktop environments you will have to consult the manuals that come with them or check hotkeys and such.

SNy

---

### Post by Guinivere on 2007-11-02
Success!

A few of you may remember me as the first who came to this thread with complaints of a squashed GUI.  I also had the common problems of very low FPS and a basically unplayable game.

At the time, my computer's major specs were as follows:

3.066GHZ Celeron D with 256KB cache
ATI Radeon 1600X 256MB AGP video
1GB DDR RAM

My computer just got a huge upgrade that resulted in the following:

3.2GHZ Pentium IV with 2MB cache
GeForce 8600GTS 256MB PCIe video
2GB DDR2 RAM

Goodness gracious, this made all the difference.  I'm buying nvidia from now on, I think.  No squashed GUI, and the game is totally playable on Ultra High.  I have to disable landscape shadows to keep the textures from wigging out, but that is no problem at all.  

I started by downloading the latest drivers from the nvidia website for this card.  I installed them, allowed the installer to make a kernel interface, and let it configure X.  Boom.  No problems there.

Then I compiled a fresh version of wine just for this occasion to make sure I was completely up to date.  No configure options, just hit ./configure and then let it do its thing for 24 minutes in make.  Did winecfg, and I have this as my graphics settings currently:

Allow DirectX apps to stop the mouse leaving their window
Allow window manager to control the windows
Emulate a virtual desktop is disabled (yet, for some reason, it's still doing so)
Vertex Shader Support:  Hardware
Allow Pixel Shader disabled
Screen Resolution is 96DPI

In regedit, I have the UseGLSL and VideoMemorySize or whatever it is set, no other values, in the Direct3D key.

Copied over the updated LOTRO from my Windows partition.  Had a few little issues with getting it to boot because I forgot to change permissions on a dll.  Once I changed those permissions, it booted right up and MAN, is it impressive.  I get about 20-40FPS on average with the game running at ultra high.  VERY playable.  The game sound works perfectly, too, to my surprise (except for the mic, no luck with that, but I think it's a system configuration thing rather than a problem with the game itself).

The only graphical glitch I appear to have is that the top portion of the 3D portrait is cut off.  Simple to ignore by turning off 3D portraits since they're not necessary anyway.  The game also doesn't like me trying to take screenshots, but I suspect that's just because I'm pushing the graphics so hard on ultra high.  I managed to grab a quick screen with GIMP, though, as seen below.  The animation is super smooth and the game overall just runs... beautifully.  I am in complete amazement at the achievements of the wine developers and the people in this thread who have made it possible for others to hack their way to a working LOTRO-in-Linux installation.

Thanks so much for what you've done, guys.  I figured I'd put in my final report, as it were, to give a heads-up to others who have computers similar to mine and might want to try getting it to run.

And now I'm going to tab back to the wine process running so happily and unobtrusively in the background and go enjoy this some more.  :D

Oh!  I dunno if this has been asked in the thread yet; quite a lot to read through.  If it's been asked, just say so and I'll search through to look for that particular post.  Anyway, is there anti-aliasing support in wine?  The game is a little bit jagged for me, and it'd be great to have anti-aliasing available, but it's completely disabled and I can't select anything in the game menu.  I was wondering if anyone discovered a hack that would allow this to work.

Edit:  I forgot to mention - I'm running this on a Debian Etch installation that I installed from the ground up using the netinst CD.

---

### Post by Garyu on 2007-11-02
> **Garyu said:**
> OK... I've been trying to get this to work in gutsy gibbon. I've been following the WineHQ AppDB guide by ajackson.

1) Copied the updated LOTRO installation from windows to /home
2) Set winecfg to Windows2000
3) Clicked the Sound tab to get sound installed and tested in winecfg
4) Put the following keys in regedit:
```
HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/UseGLSL = enabled
HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize = < amount of video memory ie 256 >
```
5) Put this into UserPreferences.ini:
```
AllowFakeFullScreen=False
FullScreen=True
```
6) Dowloaded the scripts:
[http://bmx-chemnitz.de/%7Emfr/LOTRO/lotrolauncher.script](http://bmx-chemnitz.de/%7Emfr/LOTRO/lotrolauncher.script)
[http://www.shelldorado.de/scripts/cmds/urlencode.txt](http://www.shelldorado.de/scripts/cmds/urlencode.txt)
and renamed them both to *.sh but it would not work... (Error validating certificate, could not establish SSL-connection)
7) Downloaded and compiled and installed the GUI launcher from [www.lotrolinux.com](www.lotrolinux.com)
```
sudo aptitude install build-essential mono-mcs
./configure
make
```
When I run the launcher I get the login screen with the servers, then I log in and the first screen with the witch-king displays "experiences may vary". Then I am thrown out and I get this in the terminal:

err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered
err:ole:CoGetClassObject no class object {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} could be created for context 0x1

```
along with some notices about sound first, but I get those when I run winecfg as well and sound works fine so I don't think that is the problem...

Anyone with a quick fix?

wine-0.9.47

### Post by ajackson on 2007-11-03
> **Garyu said:**
> Well, that would be strange since I have sound in other games run through Wine. I have to switch between OSS or ALSA to get sound working in different games, but it's easy to set through winecfg.
Did you copy your UserPrefs.ini file from windows or let the game create it's own one under linux (and crash first time because of the AllowFullScreen and FullScreen options)?

Try recreating your UserPrefs.ini file and see what option it has for SoundProvider, mine has OpenAL, the other is DirectSound so try swapping one with the other see if that does anything. 

> And as I said, I have sound working in both ubuntu and in other wine applications...
Unfortunately it isn't always that simple as some apps under wine act different depending on various settings (such as the windows version).

> Personally, I'm worrying more about this error message:
```
fixme:win:EnumDisplayDevicesW ((null),0,0x33f7fc,0x00000000), stub!
```
If I turn WINEDEBUG off I get that warning as well, just means that that function has it's template but hasn't been worked on yet.

If fiddling with the SoundProvider setting doesn't work I can only suggest raising a bug over at the wine main site.

### Post by ipx on 2007-11-03
> **Guinivere said:**
> Success!

A few of you may remember me as the first who came to this thread with complaints of a squashed GUI.  I also had the common problems of very low FPS and a basically unplayable game.

At the time, my computer's major specs were as follows:

3.066GHZ Celeron D with 256KB cache
ATI Radeon 1600X 256MB AGP video
1GB DDR RAM

My computer just got a huge upgrade that resulted in the following:

3.2GHZ Pentium IV with 2MB cache
GeForce 8600GTS 256MB PCIe video
2GB DDR2 RAM

Goodness gracious, this made all the difference.  I'm buying nvidia from now on, I think.  No squashed GUI, and the game is totally playable on Ultra High.  I have to disable landscape shadows to keep the textures from wigging out, but that is no problem at all.  

I started by downloading the latest drivers from the nvidia website for this card.  I installed them, allowed the installer to make a kernel interface, and let it configure X.  Boom.  No problems there.

Then I compiled a fresh version of wine just for this occasion to make sure I was completely up to date.  No configure options, just hit ./configure and then let it do its thing for 24 minutes in make.  Did winecfg, and I have this as my graphics settings currently:

Allow DirectX apps to stop the mouse leaving their window
Allow window manager to control the windows
Emulate a virtual desktop is disabled (yet, for some reason, it's still doing so)
Vertex Shader Support:  Hardware
Allow Pixel Shader disabled
Screen Resolution is 96DPI

In regedit, I have the UseGLSL and VideoMemorySize or whatever it is set, no other values, in the Direct3D key.

Copied over the updated LOTRO from my Windows partition.  Had a few little issues with getting it to boot because I forgot to change permissions on a dll.  Once I changed those permissions, it booted right up and MAN, is it impressive.  I get about 20-40FPS on average with the game running at ultra high.  VERY playable.  The game sound works perfectly, too, to my surprise (except for the mic, no luck with that, but I think it's a system configuration thing rather than a problem with the game itself).

The only graphical glitch I appear to have is that the top portion of the 3D portrait is cut off.  Simple to ignore by turning off 3D portraits since they're not necessary anyway.  The game also doesn't like me trying to take screenshots, but I suspect that's just because I'm pushing the graphics so hard on ultra high.  I managed to grab a quick screen with GIMP, though, as seen below.  The animation is super smooth and the game overall just runs... beautifully.  I am in complete amazement at the achievements of the wine developers and the people in this thread who have made it possible for others to hack their way to a working LOTRO-in-Linux installation.

Thanks so much for what you've done, guys.  I figured I'd put in my final report, as it were, to give a heads-up to others who have computers similar to mine and might want to try getting it to run.

And now I'm going to tab back to the wine process running so happily and unobtrusively in the background and go enjoy this some more.  :D

Oh!  I dunno if this has been asked in the thread yet; quite a lot to read through.  If it's been asked, just say so and I'll search through to look for that particular post.  Anyway, is there anti-aliasing support in wine?  The game is a little bit jagged for me, and it'd be great to have anti-aliasing available, but it's completely disabled and I can't select anything in the game menu.  I was wondering if anyone discovered a hack that would allow this to work.

Edit:  I forgot to mention - I'm running this on a Debian Etch installation that I installed from the ground up using the netinst CD.

God, this seems SO promising. I've been posting around in this post with similiar problems as you had with your ATI-card, and ive never had it running on linux playable at all. But now, my GFX-card is broken (dont ask me why :P) and im ordering a new laptop today with similiar gfx-card that you bought. :) Im so excited! :)

---

### Post by Garyu on 2007-11-05
> **ajackson said:**

Believe me - I'm trying my best to solve this problem. If that is not good enough for you, then thank you for your attempts at helping me and I'm sure I'll manage to go forward with other peoples help. In fact, I just got a reply from Codemaster Tech Support where they said that my error on the low-end machine with a FX 5200 card is due to a change where Shader Model 2 is no longer supported.

Lotro uses Shader Model 3 as of late (and Shader Model 4 in DirectX 10) which is not supported by some older cards, including my GeForce FX 5200. Codemasters say the sound error can be ignored, it is the shader model 3 incompatibility that is the issue and they are working on a fix. For windows there is RivaTuner which will allow a forced conversion to Shader Model 2, but it is not available for linux. So it seems I have to wait for Codemasters to fix this one unless someone knows of a way to force Shader Model 2 in linux.

### Post by SNy on 2007-11-08
You are missing the proper driver for you graphics card. Install it from the restricted drivers menu entry on ubuntu 7.10.
Then that first line about missing XFree-DRI should have vanished.

To verify working 3D, try glxinfo and look for "direct rendering: yes".

The game itself is complaining about a missing 3D feature (naturally, as the software rendering doesn't support fancy stuff).

SNy

---

Two restarts later and direct rendering is working again. :S 
But the problem remains the same. I am even more confused.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-11-09
**wine-0.9.49**
New version of wine out today, game looks smoother but how much is down to the wine changes and how much the tweaks CM did yesterday I don't know.

Help still doesn't work and probably never will due to it's dependance on IE. Starting in windowed mode still produces the error message about 32 bit graphics.

The only noticable change is that you now longer have to set UseGLSL in the registry as it now defaults to enabled. It also supposidly uses pixel shaders V3 only if the card supports it, whether that gets around the problem LOTRO was having with cards that only supported V2 I don't know.

The how-to on the AppDB page is no longer being maintained by me (don't ask) but my web site has a how-to page now that I will maintain.

### Post by ipx on 2007-11-11
This is a story how I got lotro running on linux. The main part is that I changed from ATi to Nvidia. I'll start from the beginning...

This was my current computer spec;
AMD64 3000+
ATi Radeon 9800pro 128MB
1024MB(2x512) PC3200 Corsair DDR-RAM
rest is irrelevant...

I bought lotro about 2-3 months ago together with a couple of windows-friends. I read that lotro should work fine on linux through wine, so I assumed I would get it running. But, after some tweaking and messing around with the configuration(ATi-drivers, Wine-version and configuration files), I soon realized that it would NOT work. I had stuff like textures not showing, GUI all smacked up on the screen, very laggy gameplay etc.

I was then more or less forced to install windows and play it from there; so thats what I did. For about 1-2 months I played lotro now and then though windows. And you all can imagine whats that like huh? A MMORPG on WINDOWS when you're a LINUX-FANBOY! You get stucked in a pissy operating system for a long time. ^^
During this time I also tried to get it running with linux very much, as soon as a new driver- or wine version came up, or a new hint somewhere. :)

After some time (I think I got to level 39) I had just gotten a new GPU-cooler, so I thought I'd install it on my ATI-card to make it cool better. Here, something really annoying, but maybe it was the destiny. ;) I Accidentally broke the card, and 3 of the metal leaders on the card broke. This gave me some artifacts, a headache and no computer to play games on. (This is a disaster!)

(I first thought I'd wait for my new laptop that comes in a few days, but i just couldn't wait)

So, after some time I bought a new card, second hand, a Nvidia Geforce 7600GS with passive cooling (I got it relatively cheap, it was almost never used, got the recipe and the deal went very quick). This card turned out to be one of the best hardware-purchases I've ever done! (Would probably not been if i wasnt a linux-user). 
The drivers worked really good, and not only did I get lotro running like a charm (well, lowest gfx-settings and so, but I am pretty sure that with the new 1GB-stick of RAM I'm getting today it will work a lot better and i don't have to use the swap ;)), but I also got some other games up and running that didn't work with my ATi-card; ETQW(worked before, only REALLY  slow), Call of duty 2 (didnt even run before), Hidden and dangerous (old game, didnt work at all before, works 100% now) and probably more (Havnt had time to try much). 

To conclude my post into the most important parts;
1. For you ATi-users out there who gets GUI-errors, its because of shitty drivers. You could wait for new ones to come out, get a nvidia-card or wait for some kind of fix.
2. For the ATi-users of you that cant get games running while wine says it would work; Get a nvidia-card or wait for good drivers.
3. AIGLX works ALOT better on nvidia compared to ati(and fglrx).

---

### Post by carlosjuero on 2007-11-11
> **ipx said:**

1024MB(2x512) PC3200 Corsair DDR-RAM
rest is irrelevant...

I bought lotro about 2-3 months ago together with a couple of windows-friends. I read that lotro should work fine on linux through wine, so I assumed I would get it running. But, after some tweaking and messing around with the configuration(ATi-drivers, Wine-version and configuration files), I soon realized that it would NOT work. I had stuff like textures not showing, GUI all smacked up on the screen, very laggy gameplay etc.

I was then more or less forced to install windows and play it from there; so thats what I did. For about 1-2 months I played lotro now and then though windows. And you all can imagine whats that like huh? A MMORPG on WINDOWS when you're a LINUX-FANBOY! You get stucked in a pissy operating system for a long time. ^^
During this time I also tried to get it running with linux very much, as soon as a new driver- or wine version came up, or a new hint somewhere. :)

After some time (I think I got to level 39) I had just gotten a new GPU-cooler, so I thought I'd install it on my ATI-card to make it cool better. Here, something really annoying, but maybe it was the destiny. ;) I Accidentally broke the card, and 3 of the metal leaders on the card broke. This gave me some artifacts, a headache and no computer to play games on. (This is a disaster!)

(I first thought I'd wait for my new laptop that comes in a few days, but i just couldn't wait)

So, after some time I bought a new card, second hand, a Nvidia Geforce 7600GS with passive cooling (I got it relatively cheap, it was almost never used, got the recipe and the deal went very quick). This card turned out to be one of the best hardware-purchases I've ever done! (Would probably not been if i wasnt a linux-user). 
The drivers worked really good, and not only did I get lotro running like a charm (well, lowest gfx-settings and so, but I am pretty sure that with the new 1GB-stick of RAM I'm getting today it will work a lot better and i don't have to use the swap ;)), but I also got some other games up and running that didn't work with my ATi-card; ETQW(worked before, only REALLY  slow), Call of duty 2 (didnt even run before), Hidden and dangerous (old game, didnt work at all before, works 100% now) and probably more (Havnt had time to try much). 

To conclude my post into the most important parts;
1. For you ATi-users out there who gets GUI-errors, its because of shitty drivers. You could wait for new ones to come out, get a nvidia-card or wait for some kind of fix.
2. For the ATi-users of you that cant get games running while wine says it would work; Get a nvidia-card or wait for good drivers.
3. AIGLX works ALOT better on nvidia compared to ati(and fglrx).
Cheater! Getting an nVidia card! :p

Unfortunately that isn't an option for me right now, when I can get a new card I am definitely heading nVidias way and dropping ATI like a hot rock (too many issues with too many things - heck, in CoH windows side I have to play around in certain screens because the driver is messed up [enhancement screens, etc]).

### Post by ajackson on 2007-12-01
**wine-0.9.50**
Released yesterday, no noticeable with regards options that don't work (starting fullscreen, AllowFakeFullScreen and in-game help), haven't tested Turbine's launcher but .NET under wine is still poor at best.

The release notes mention a fix for an ATI specific problem, what that was I don't know, there has been more work on the D3D stuff so the game should hopefully run a bit smoother.

### Post by Pucce on 2007-12-10
In (your home directory)/The Lord of the Rings Online/UserPreferences.ini under [Display]-section there should be this line:
AllowFakeFullScreen=True
change it to:
AllowFakeFullScreen=False

---

> **Menthol said:**
> Sorry if this has already been bought up, I tried but I just couldnt make it through all 74 pages of this thread !!!

As pucce pointed out it is the AllowFullScreen setting and FullScreen settings you need to check. There is an enhancement in the pipelines for wine to report the 24 bit colour mode linux uses as the 32 bit colour mode windows uses, when (if) it will arrive I don't know.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-12-18
**wine-0.9.51**
Released last Friday, had the usual improvements to the directx, so game should be a bit smoother.

But by installing mono-1.2.6, copying a few fonts and dlls from a windows box, I can get the native launcher splash screen to display but it then crashes because of an activex problem. It's a little progress but it does help give hope of getting LOTRO working without a temporary launcher.

[IMG]http://www.lotrolinux.com/images/nativeLOTRO.png[/IMG]

Edit: For anyone who wants to have a go themselves try the following

**Important backup your current .wine, that way you can revert to that if something goes wrong**

Get wine tricks [http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks](http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks)

use winetricks to install mono12

(unistall that version of mono, not strickly necessary but I do it to be tidy, I used winetricks
because it set a registry option for .NET compatibility).

Get & install mono-1.2.6-gtksharp-2.10.2-win32-4.exe (from mono-project.org)

Copy schannel.dll & msasn1.dll into LOTRO directory (probably should be windows/system32 but again this is quick & dirty)

use winetricks to install vcrun6

copy *.ttf from windows box to linux box (in .wine/drive_c/windows/fonts)

Save this file [http://bugs.winehq.org/attachment.cgi?id=9204](http://bugs.winehq.org/attachment.cgi?id=9204) and do "regedit fonts.reg"

---

### Post by SNy on 2007-12-19
Hm, assume for a minute it really is the part that tries to show the news panel.

Displaying the ingame help does actually work (although, as we know, the result is hidden unless you move the window around) and my educated guess here is that the basic component used is the same (COM/DCOM/ActiveX kind of component).
The difference between the game client and the launcher is then - clearly - that the latter is written in .NET and needs to invoke the browser component via some wrapper around it, which I would therefore assume to be the culprit. Wether this helps, I currently don't know.

You could try and verify your assumption regarding the crash at attempting to get the news panel with ethereal or similar, could you not?

I am hoping to come up with news regarding patching but I will need to get some more info before I can comment on that.

SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2007-12-19
I've found that you can turn the web browser part off.

You need to copy the file LauncherUserSettings.xml

From c:\documents and settings\username\Local Settings\Application Data\Turbine\The Lord of the Rings Online\Launcher on your windows box to .wine/drive_c//profiles/username/Local Settings/Application Data/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online/Launcher

Check for case, etc and even for the EU version it is Turbine.

Edit it in a text editor and find the following

MainForm.Mode.Compact set this to True
showNews set this to False

Now when you start TurbineLauncher it will say that 1.05 is available (need to track down where in the registry that is stored) just click OK and it will fail with a different Mono error but it does that under Windows & Mono as well.

---

### Post by Gustave on 2007-12-19
> **cameran said:**
> 
i'm assuming that the windows launcher uses IE or something similar to power the news portion?  would an install of IE into wine help?

 
I'm just following the progress you guys are doing with interest and I happened to notice this.  The news reader embedded in the laucher is based off of IE, I believe, as Cameran pointed out.  It opens another IE window when you click on links inside of it.  This is only true of this particular area.  If there's a window with an advertisement in it (Currently Gollum selling the game for 9.95), it's not strictly IE and anything will do.

### Post by yvanvds on 2007-12-26
Installation went fine over here, but I'm not able to play the game because I get between 130 and 200Kb upstream traffic from the moment I log in to a world. I don't know if someone else has had this same problem or even if it has something to do with using linux, but I thought I might as well ask here also. 

On the forums there are some threads about character freezes and such, but the cause of that might or might not be related. In my case, I clearly generate so much traffic my network cannot handle it anymore, thus causing a lot of lag.

But what to do about it? I tried firewalling my outgoing connections, but that does not really help since all data is being sent to the same address, apparently. Using Wireshark does give me some information about this traffic, but without knowing the protocol the game uses, i cannot really do something with that.

Suggestions?

---

Can somebody confirm this for me?

Use winecfg to change to WinXP or WinVista mode.

Start LOTRO (the intro video should be choppy and won't respond to pressing ESC to skip it).

### Post by Beren Camlost on 2007-12-28
Bad-Taste, did you patch your Windows client with the highres textures? If unsure, you probably didn't, which can cause a very similar issue.

To patch the highres textures, start your client in Windows. When at the character selection screen, choose Option, and set 'Texture Quality' (or something like that) to Very High. It will give you a message to restart LotRO, so do just that :)

Once patched, exit LotRO again and copy the folder over to your linux install.

### Post by ajackson on 2007-12-29
**wine-0.9.52**
Released on Friday, you can now start LOTRO in windowed mode, so the only setting you need to watch in UserPrefs is AllowFakeFullScreen.

There is a problem affecting some people (like Bad-Taste in the post above) with errors coming out relating to surface.c, there is a patch that should fix that problem, since it doesn't seem to affect me I can't say if it fixes it or not.

The patch is available [here]("http://www.winehq.org/pipermail/wine-patches/2007-December/048406.html")

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-02
OK. Hold your breath.

Patching. Seems. To. Work.

I fiddled a bit with the dll earlier, creating a .def file for it and linking against it (ipx will remember from my reports in the IRC channel, QuakeNet #lotro-linux, if anyone is interested).

Problem was that I didn't know the function signature and haven't found the time to dig around more.

Now that Robert Getter (thanks, pal, I had no idea rundll could do that, nor would I have thought the dll would actually tell you that much, thanks, Turbine, as well! :D) wrote me an email and I checked with google for what rundll32.exe does, I got the function to perform its job.

I do not yet know if it actually patches, since there are currently no updates, but it does check with the server for files and data and decides that those do not need an update.

```

mfr@gripride:/usr/local/games/LOTRO$ wine patch.exe
err:alsa:ALSA_CheckSetVolume Could not find 'PCM Playback Volume' element
Connecting to 195.33.152.250:5015
Checking files...files to patch: 0 bytes to download: 0
Patching files:
checking data...data patches: 0 bytes to download: 0
Patching data:

mfr@gripride:/usr/local/games/LOTRO$

```

The patch exe there is a totally stupid bit of code that now that it is stripped down even further than before does exactly the same as:

```

wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch 195.33.152.250:5015 --language de --productcode LOTROEU

```

Here is the actual patch.c (which in order to compile needs a bit of other stuff). You could just as well do it JUST like rundll and call LoadLibrary() and GetProcAddress(), my version is dynamically linked to the PatchClient.dll using a .def file and a patchclient.a created via some tools.

```

mfr@gripride:~/LOTRO/patch$ cat patch.c
#include <windows.h>

__declspec(dllimport) void Patch( HWND hWin, HINSTANCE hInst, LPSTR cmdLine, int nCmdShow );

int main( int argc, char *argv[] )
{
    Patch( NULL, GetModuleHandle( "PatchClient.dll" ), "195.33.152.250:5015 --language de --productcode LOTROEU", 0 );

    return 0;
}
mfr@gripride:~/LOTRO/patch$

```

But hey, who cares if rundll works just as good.
I am going to add the call with the correct parameters to the CLI launcher script in a few minutes (because IP, language and productcode need to be given to it properly) and then we will see if the next update Just Works (TM).

SNy

PS. Happy New Year! :D

---

> Requesting GLS authentication ticket...

To connect to gls.lotro.com insecurely, use `--no-check-certificate'.
Unable to establish SSL connection.

---

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-06
Order of options is irrelevant, meaning you can put it before or after any of the other options (just not in between the option specifier and the option value). If you are unsure, just place it directly after the wget command name:

```

wget --no-check-certificate -nv \
     ...

```

HTH,
SNy

---

I assume this is a driver issue (I'm using the intel driver on integrated 945GM graphics). If I recall, it did not work under the i810 driver either. Same error.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-06
**LotROLinux-0.6**
First draft of the patching process now in place (I put it under Options so that you don't have to run it). The website in my signature has been updated to show how you can install the game under linux without first using a windows box, you do need the current version of patchclient.dll but you can download a pre-patched version of LOTRO which includes book 11 from certainly the EU site, don't know about the US site.

One thing I have found is that it currently does not patch the hi-res graphics file, this seems to be down to a problem with patchclient.dll, for some reason it doesn't check this file, even if you add the --highres option to the call. Hopefully our guardian angel at Turbine is looking in and can fix this little problem for us :)

```

```
Do you get direct rendering: yes (or something similar), if not then you have a graphics driver problem.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-09
> **funghi said:**
> My question is: does the EU version of LOTRO use the .NET framework?
Both the EU and US clients use a launcher written in .NET. You can get the game to launch using either my GUI launcher or SNy's CLI launcher (follow the link in my signature for more information on both and a how-to). Once the game launches wine handles it fine except the help system.

> although I understand they are still involved with the LOTRO project in Europe...I just dont know to what capacity....
Codemasters have leased the game from them and run it in the EU area, all patches, new content, etc come via Turbine so the games are more or less identical (EU has language packs for other languages like French and German).

> in addition to the fact that I hear better things about Codemasters and their server management.
Whoever told you that is telling lies, Codemasters are at best on level terms with Turbine's server management but only when CM are having a good day.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-12
> **SNy said:**
> Ah, damn.
I had been thinking that maybe instead of the filenames for the client_local_*.dat files, it should check subdirectories under raw/ or something.
On my windows drive I have the following directories:
en-GB
raw/en_gb

When I run the patch with en_GB it downloaded the EULA and TurbineLauncher.resources.dll into en-gb (i.e. it created the en-gb directory) under windows en-GB and en-gb would be the same so I think we'd need to create a symlink between en-GB and en-gb.

> Could you guys do me a favor and check for their names in different languages? Would be good to have it for Europe and US as well (en_GB, fr, en_US or the like).
for EU English it is en_GB, I can confirm that one. For US English I think it is just en but can't confirm.

Edit: the key seems to be the directory with the language name, I installed the French version and it was fr (and patchclient updated the EULA when language was set to fr), I'd assume the German version is de. So you can base it off that remembering to change the "-" for an "_" if it exists.

> Oh, and ajackson, does it update the highres files when the case for the language is correct, by any chance?
No but I'm guessing it is checking for a filename that doesn't match client_highres.dat on a Linux system but does on a Windows system (ie all or part is uppercase).

Another thing I have found is that if it downloads patchclient.dll you need to do

Edit: seems that zlib1T.dll also need to have selfpatch performed if it was downloaded.
```
wine rundll32.exe SelfPatch,SelfPatch
```
and then restart the rundll32 PatchClient,Patch. So it seems best to run it once with --filesonly, if patchclient is in the list of files downloaded run selfpatch then patch --filesonly again, finally run patch with --dataonly.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-13

Oops, I wonder how I managed to mess that up.

**LotROLinux-0.6.2**
Fixes that slight error with the setting window, sorry about that.

According to [http://www.updatexp.com/0xC0000005.html](http://www.updatexp.com/0xC0000005.html) that message seems to be indicating a memory problem. Whether that is a phyical problem with your memory, a driver issue or a wine issue I couldn't tell you. Double check that you haven't done a typo in the VideoMemorySize setting, try with/without hi-res graphics and if it is still going wrong try running memtest86 see if it is a problem with the actual memory.

Thanks for spotting that error in the settings window.

### Post by Bad-Taste on 2008-01-13
np ;-)

Its working, but now i have this WINEDEBUG row twice ;-)

i searched a little bit and found something interesting:

```
Question/Topic
The application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005). Click on OK to terminate the application.
Answer/Article

The error 0xC0000005 is generated by an illegal "memory access violation".  This usually indicates a NET installation problem. Please try to re-install .NET Framework 1.1 and try again.

.NET Framework 1.1
.NET Framework 1.1 Service Pack 1

If you are still receiving this error message, we recommend that you obtain the latest LOTRO launcher. To do so:

   1. Open your Internet browser and navigate to: http://download.turbine.com/lotro/lotrolauncher_13123_us.exe
   2. Answer "Save" when prompted by the browser, and save lotrolauncher_13104_us.exe to your LOTRO installation folder. e.g. C:\Program Files\Turbine\The Lord of the Rings Online
   3. Open Windows Explorer and navigate to your LOTRO installation folder
   4. Execute lotrolauncher_13104_us.exe by double-clicking on it
   5. Answer "Yes to All" when prompted whether to overwrite the existing files or not

You may now launch the game normally and proceed with the updating process.
```

Looks like monodevelop got a bit excited when regenerating the gui stuff, or I really need some alcohol :)

**LotROLinux-0.6.3** should (fingers crossed) be ok now.

> 
i searched a little bit and found something interesting:

[http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=776](http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=776)

```
Question/Topic
The application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005). Click on OK to terminate the application.
Answer/Article

The error 0xC0000005 is generated by an illegal "memory access violation".  This usually indicates a NET installation problem. Please try to re-install .NET Framework 1.1 and try again.

.NET Framework 1.1
.NET Framework 1.1 Service Pack 1

If you are still receiving this error message, we recommend that you obtain the latest LOTRO launcher. To do so:

   1. Open your Internet browser and navigate to: http://download.turbine.com/lotro/lotrolauncher_13123_us.exe
   2. Answer "Save" when prompted by the browser, and save lotrolauncher_13104_us.exe to your LOTRO installation folder. e.g. C:\Program Files\Turbine\The Lord of the Rings Online
   3. Open Windows Explorer and navigate to your LOTRO installation folder
   4. Execute lotrolauncher_13104_us.exe by double-clicking on it
   5. Answer "Yes to All" when prompted whether to overwrite the existing files or not

You may now launch the game normally and proceed with the updating process.
```

Hmm that does tend to agree it is a memory error but the fix is for the windows launcher. If your game is a copy from windows try copying the lotroclient.exe from the backup directory and running the windows launcher (ie force it to update lotroclient.exe). If it was installed via linux edit PatchWindow.cs, find
```
patch.Start();
```
and insert
```
AddLog(patchParams);
```
after it, see what is being passed for the --language option. If it ENGLISH (or english) change the line that reads
```
patchParams = patchParams.Replace ("{1}", language);
```
to
```
patchParams = patchParams.Replace ("{1}", "en");
```

See if that patches the game correct and stops that access error.

> 
Edit: I found a download for the US-Version you have searching for:

[http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=1554](http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=1554)
Thanks again.

### Post by Bad-Taste on 2008-01-13
Edited PatchWindow.cs
the launcher patched some vidoes but i have the same error

this is how i edited the file, i hope this is right:

```
// PatchWindow.cs created with MonoDevelop
// User: jacks1a at 18:08 04/01/2008
//
// To change standard headers go to Edit->Preferences->Coding->Standard Headers
//

using System;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Threading;

namespace LotROLinux
{
	
	
	public partial class PatchWindow : Gtk.Dialog
	{
		private string argTemplate = "rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch {0} --language {1} --productcode {2}";
	
		private string patchParams;
		private string runDir;
		private string wineProgram;
		private Process patch;
		private bool aborted;
		
		public PatchWindow(string urlPatchServer, string prodCode,
			string language, string runDir,
			string wineProgram, bool hiResEnabled,
			string icoFile)
		{
			this.Build();

			this.Icon = Gdk.Pixbuf.LoadFromResource(icoFile);

			this.runDir = runDir;
			this.wineProgram = wineProgram;

			patchParams = argTemplate.Replace ("{0}", urlPatchServer);
			patchParams = patchParams.Replace ("{1}", "DE");
			patchParams = patchParams.Replace ("{2}", prodCode);

			if (hiResEnabled)
			{
				patchParams = patchParams + " --highres";
			}

			AddLog("Click apply to start patching");
		}

		protected virtual void OnButtonOkClicked (object sender, System.EventArgs e)
		{
			System.Threading.Thread patchThread = new System.Threading.Thread(Patch);
			patchThread.Start();
		}

		private void Patch()
		{
			string temp;

			aborted = false;

			patch = new Process();

			patch.StartInfo.WorkingDirectory = runDir;
			patch.StartInfo.FileName = wineProgram;
			patch.StartInfo.Arguments = patchParams;
			patch.StartInfo.RedirectStandardOutput = true;
			patch.StartInfo.UseShellExecute = false;

			patch.Start();			
			
			AddLog(patchParams);

			ToggleButtons();
			AddLog("Starting patching, please wait...");

			while (!patch.HasExited) {
				temp = patch.StandardOutput.ReadLine();
				if ((temp != null) && (temp.Length > 0)) {
					if ((!temp.StartsWith(".")) && (!temp.StartsWith(" "))) {
						AddLog(temp.TrimEnd('.'));
					}	
				}
			}
			patch.WaitForExit();

			if (aborted){
				AddLog("Patching aborted.");
			} else {
				AddLog("Patching complete.");
			}
			ToggleButtons();
		}

		private void AppendTextWithoutScroll (string text) 
		{
			Gtk.Application.Invoke (delegate {
				Gtk.TextBuffer buf = txtStatus.Buffer;

				Gtk.TextIter iter;
				buf.MoveMark(buf.InsertMark, buf.EndIter);
				if(text != null) {
					if (text.Equals ("") == false) {				
						iter = buf.EndIter;
						buf.Insert (ref iter, text);
					}
				}
				iter = buf.EndIter;
				buf.Insert (ref iter, "\n");
				buf.MoveMark (buf.SelectionBound, buf.EndIter);
			});
		}

		private void AddLog(string message)
		{
			Gtk.Application.Invoke (delegate {
				Gtk.TextBuffer buf = txtStatus.Buffer;

				AppendTextWithoutScroll(message);

				while (Gtk.Application.EventsPending ()) 
					Gtk.Application.RunIteration ();

				txtStatus.ScrollToMark (buf.InsertMark, 0.4, true, 0.0, 1.0);
			});
		}

		private void ToggleButtons()
		{
			Gtk.Application.Invoke (delegate {
				buttonOk.Visible = !buttonOk.Visible;
				buttonCancel.Visible = !buttonCancel.Visible;
				buttonQuit.Visible = !buttonQuit.Visible;
			});
		}

		protected virtual void OnButtonQuitClicked (object sender, System.EventArgs e)
		{
			if (!patch.HasExited) {
				AddLog("Aborting, please wait...");
				patch.Kill();
				aborted = true;
			}
		}
	}
}
		
```

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-17
That error is due to a problem with different versions of wget handling invalid certificates (for https secured sites) differently.

See Page 80 of this very thread ([http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=386480&page=80](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=386480&page=80)) for a fix and where to put it.

Will put up a fix for the other issue (spaces in path) online today and might as well think of some way to improve the wget situation.

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-17
Well I finally think I've worked out how the patching is supposed to work:

- Patch --filesonly
- when that has finished if patchclient.dll or zlib1T.dll were mentioned then rerun patch --filesonly (it detects and handles the self patch itself, or so it seems)
- Patch --dataonly

I've tried every possile name to client_highres.dat, none of them get patched so I'm guessing patchclient.dll has an error in it. The strange thing is the windows installer uses patchclient.dll to do the patching (via patchwrapper.dll) so I'm not too sure what is wrong there.

Languages are as follows (look for directories called):
en = US English
en-GB = UK English
fr = French
de = German

The language code to pass to patchclient.dll is the same as that directory name with en-GB becoming en_GB.

---

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-17
Hm, I think the filesonly part applies for more than these two files. I just deleted chatclient.dll to test the updated script and it seems to need two runs as well, just put it in the ./temp directory. Had to rerun it to get it into ./

So, could we assume it would always be safe to call

patch --filesonly
patch --filesonly
patch --dataonly,

in that order?

BTW, I discovered a few other details, mainly a few interesting outputs in PatchClient.log and that patchcache.bin apparently contains hashes of all the files. I think the log could help to come up with a patch.c that actually works better than just rundll-ing the thing. We will see.

For now, a few minor tweaks to the script for the directory-with-spaces and the wget issue.

HTH,
SNy

Edit: Oh, PS. Could we probably use one of the video file names instead of client_local_*.dat? They seem to have the case correct  (eg. ./raw/de/chargen/class_videos/class_burglar_de.bik) which would help.

And will this sort out my problem of the choppy/lag game play?

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-18
> **dobbyono said:**
> my wine folder in the editor does not contain a Direct3D folder so how do I add these changes.
Right click on the wine setting in regedit (left panel) then select new->key and call it Direct3D. Then click on that entry right click, new->string, rename it VideoMemorySize, double click it and enter the appropriate value.

> And will this sort out my problem of the choppy/lag game play?
It might and it might not, if the choppyness was caused by not having enough graphical memory to use then it will help, if it was caused by network issues it won't. If you have a wireless connection it maybe work trying a wired connection if you can to see if that is an issue.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-18
> **slopis said:**
> I got that script needed to lunch LOTRO. actually all  LOTRO work except it don't connect to server, and mine router is not blocking it. and after i exit game, in terminal is such text:

Well the terminal output doesn't show any problems.

By not connecting to the server do you mean that you get a black screen with a quit button and a message along the lines of "connection with server was lost"? If so then the problem is most likely the hi-res graphics file being out of date. If you installed under wine and then let the script do the updating the hi-res graphics does not get patched at the moment due to a problem. You can get around this by finding the line that reads
```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} $@
```
change that to
```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} --HighResOutOfDate $@
```

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-20
**LotROLinux-0.7**
I've done a major revamp of the gui launcher, switching from being stetic based to glade based (so you need to install an additional library, see the README). I did this because the file that monodevelop was using to generate most of the windows for me kept getting damaged or falsly updated everytime I edited something in MD, the gui side is now handled by glade files.

You now install the launcher using 'make install', this installs the files LotROLinux.run (the executable) and LotROLinux, a launch script. I've added the launch script because I have finally worked out how to get the gecko/mozilla web browser working, the script sets up a couple of environment variables that are required (nicked it straight from the start script for monodevelop :)). The gecko browser is optional, if you wish the launcher to be built with it you need to do
```
./configure --enable-gecko
```.

Since the launcher now installs in the normal linux way the first time you run the new version it will ask you which game you wish to be default (it works with Dungeons & Dragons Online, how well I don't know but the launch process is virtually identical). Then you will be asked to either select your existing configuration file for import or let it make a best guess.

If you choose to import you need to use the file choosing dialog box to find and select LotROLinux.config (or DDOConfig for DDO), it should then setup all the information it needs. If you choose a new configuration the only option you will probably need to alter is the game directory, simply navigate to the directory containing the game. I've added an option for setting WINEPREFIX if anyone needs it.

Finally the patch window looks a little nicer, you may recognise the format from somewhere. The hi-res graphics still do not get updated, hopefully when book 12 comes out those nice people at Turbine will fix the problem (or give us subtle hints on how to make it work properly). The patching process handles self patch and creates a log file in the launchers configuration directory (~/.LotROLinux), the new config file is also in there.

Any problems just shout and I'll do my best to fix them.

---

I'd imagine that its because its going over the threshold of the 256 meg memory card and cant handle it. ### Post by badrunner on 2008-01-21
> **Menthol said:**
> One strange occurance I've noticed is that if I have the high res textures, (ultra high settings I believe) then it tends to boot me out when theres alot happening i.e when in a city.  I'd imagine that its because its going over the threshold of the 256 meg memory card and cant handle it.  The game runs very stable and I played for hours yesterday with no problems what so ever with the textures turned down.

Anyone else experienced this phenomenon ?

Quick edit: Just to make it clear I have fully updated to the high res textures in windows so I dont think its that causing it.

This seems to be a widely reported issue by windows users too. The classic symptoms are high quality graphics -> Crash when in Bree. Currently the lotro guys are telling people just to turn down their graphics  quality. It seems to have been a problem since lotro was released, and there is yet to be a fix. Some people have luck with cranking down the texture cache to 0, others just by chaning one particular setting in advanced graphics. It seems to vary wildly from user to user however, even when on similar hardware.

This thread on the lotro forums might be of some help to you:

[http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=51472](http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=51472)

Have you had the game working under windows? If not (and you can) try under windows, if that connects ok it is a problem with linux.

If the game doesn't work under windows either then either your firewall/router is blocking ports (there are posts on the official LOTRO forums about unblocking them) or your ISP is blocking them, which is a lot harder to fix.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-22
**Repository for LotROLinux**
Since the launcher is now linux-friendly (ie installs properly), I've had a go at making a repository for it. To use simple add
```
deb http://www.lotrolinux.com/ubuntu/ gutsy main
```
or
```
deb http://www.lotrolinux.com/ubuntu/ dapper main
```
as a repository (if you are using Edgy or Feisty the Dapper one should work).

Package names are lotrolinux or lotrolinux-gecko (if you want the gecko/mozilla web browser version).

It should work fine and install dependancies correctly but it is the first time I've set up a repository so I made have made an error here or there.

I'll see about adding a Debian friendly version (try gutsy and dapper first though to see if they work).

As for RPM versions, well they aren't likely to be around anytime soon :)

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-23
As far as I have understood from the various discussions about DDO I read, the main difference with DDO compared to LOTRO is the authentication/login step where the "TakeANumber" command to the world queue is not necessary for DDO.
As for using the graphical launcher, I think ajackson has already implemented it this way (not sure), so apart from patching, which is for LOTRO only and does only work with the newer patchclient.dll file, you should be able to launch DDO. That means you would need to patch under windows and copy the files over (you could use the update-script from my site that uses rsync to avoid copying everything over again).

HTH,
SNy

--
Edit

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-23
> **Hedgewan said:**
> Then it stops and tells me I am not current and shuts my game down.
If you see the movie then the launcher part is working but it sounds like some files are out of date (or you have a problem with your actual account).

> I will be eager to await a miracle solution to the patch problem. I will help in any way I can.
I have found out how to patch DDO but it requires a copy of patchclient.dll (and zlib1T.dll) from LOTRO.

The zlib1T.dll might already be in the DDO directory if it is use that one.

If you can get a copy of patchclient.dll from LOTRO then rename it something other than patchclient.dll (if you don't it will get patched back to a non-working version) and stick it in the DDO directory.

The next stage involves modifying the launcher source code (so you can't use the version from the repository).

Edit PatchWindow.cs find
```
private const string argTemplate = "rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch {0} --language {1} --productcode {2}";
```
change it to read
```
private const string argTemplate = "rundll32.exe mypatch.dll,Patch {0} --language {1} --productcode {2}";
```
where mypatch.dll is the name you gave to the LOTRO copy of patchclient.dll

As I type this the gui launcher is merrily patching DDO.

The problem is getting a copy of patchclient.dll from LOTRO as I'm not certain if it would be ok to stick a copy somewhere to be downloaded.

Thank you ajackson and SNy! I will get it working soon I am certain. 

I will get that PM together soon and I will be eager to await a miracle solution to the patch problem. I will help in any way I can.

You cant just install from retail and use the standalone patchers. they just install the data but dont actually do the patching (and dont update patchclient.dll or lotroclient.exe). You need to either install + update on windows then copy the directory, or install from the book 11 completely patched download (the 8gb one). See the appdb.winehq.org page for more info on this.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-23
**LotROLinux-0.7.1**
Fixed the bug with the realm not being saved correctly and fixed a coredump if you tried to switch between DDO & LOTRO with the gecko version.


> **badrunner said:**
> You cant just install from retail and use the standalone patchers. they just install the data but dont actually do the patching (and dont update patchclient.dll or lotroclient.exe). You need to either install + update on windows then copy the directory, or install from the book 11 completely patched download (the 8gb one). See the appdb.winehq.org page for more info on this.
He's talking about DDO rather than LOTRO, unfortunately the patchclient.dll with the Patch function exposed hasn't made it to DDO yet. But as I said in my previous post you can use the one from LOTRO but you need to rename it and tweak the gui launcher slightly to stop it overwriting the newer version of patchclient.dll

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-23
No, unfortunately, you need a slightly NEWER version of the patchclient.dll, which is exactly the problem (catch-22).

Turbine have changed this .dll a while back, but without a working patch mechanism in place, you can't get the update, hence you either need to patch it one time under windows or you need to get that one file off the net.
Now, the latter is exactly what concerns ajackson (I do share that concern), since it is likely to be a problem if we just put it somewhere public. I am not yet done with analyzing what the library does in the first place, so ATM we are dependant on the thing.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-23
There are two places that you can get the correct version of patchclient.dll but it involves downloading the re-packaged LOTRO setup (patched up to book 11). Trouble is it is about 8GB.

The locations are:
[US version]("http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=1554")
[EU version]("http://community.codemasters.com/forum/showthread.php?t=172760")

The terms of service specifically mention redistribution, so while they may well turn a blind eye to a copy being available I will not make one available without the consent of Turbine.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-24
> **Hedgewan said:**
> I then try patching DDO. I did everything right this time. I click apply, nothing happens, no error message or anything. I hit the stop button and I get this:
The abort process isn't the best in the world (relies on killing the patch process).

The next error concerning sharing violation is because the patch didn't abort properly so the file was still locked. You can try manually deleting the patch.log from the .LotROLinux folder see if it will let you.

One thing I did notice once while testing the patch process was that for some reason it didn't write what it was doing out, since my progress stuff relies on that it makes it look like it has hung when in truth it hasn't. Trouble is I could never recreate it so fixing it was impossible.

If you can clear the lock on the log file it is worth giving it another go, see if it feels like co-operating this time.

Edit: Though I will amend the program to catch that crash and display a more graceful message

---

### Post by badrunner on 2008-01-24
> **cameran said:**
> hello,

thanks for your reply but how do i do that?

thanks!

cameran

At the moment, not easily. It depends largely on the hardware you have etc. and relies on fiddling with alsa internals to get the result you want. Essentially there is a bug in wine in that it appears to be hardcoded to use card 0, and there isnt really a lot you can do about without a reasonable knowledge of alsa. 

There is work on getting an OpenAl driver for wine that wraps the linux one (lotro, and most modern games use openal for sound), but i dont think its in mainstream wine yet. There is also lots of work going on with sound support anyway, so it may turn out to be unneccesary in a future wine version that gets this all right.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-25
> **cthulhu666 said:**
> Long time lurker, first time poster.

I have to admit I use Gentoo, but please help me on this one anyway.
That's OK, this thread might live on the Ubuntu forum but it is a generic LOTRO (and DDO) with wine thread.

> err:wineboot:ProcessWindowsFileProtection WFP: L"urlmon.dll" error 0x7)
Those errors could well be the problem, try the following commands see if they show any errors:
```

wineserver -k
wineboot

```
The first stops any running versions of wine and the second mimics a windows reboot. I've done a quick google for that error message but it doesn't show a great deal of useful information.

> *EDIT: Is Mono a requirement when using the lotrolauncher.script?*
No only for the gui launcher.

> *EDIT2: I guess commenting this line in the script could provide usefull information:```
WINEDEBUG=fixme-all \
```*
That was put in because the game used to throw out thousands of fixme messages and it was slowing the game down. If you are still having problems it might be work removing that to see what messages are displayed just before the client exiting.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-25

If it is the launcher config file for DDO it is called dndlauncher.exe.config, the one I have looks like this:
```

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<configuration>

	<system.windows.forms jitDebugging="true"/>

	<startup>

		<requiredRuntime version="V1.1.4322" safemode="true"/>

		<supportedRuntime version="V1.1.4322" />

	</startup>

	<appSettings>

		<add key="Launcher.DataCenterService.GLS" value="http://ddoeugls.com/GLS.DataCenterServer/Service.asmx"/>



		<add key="DataCenter.GameName" value="DDOEU"/>

		<add key="DataCenter.DefaultCenter" value="DDOEU"/>

		<add key="Product.DocumentFolder" value="Dungeons and Dragons Online"/>

		<add key="Launcher.DocumentFolder" value="Launcher"/>

	</appSettings>

</configuration>


```

It's virtually identical to the LOTRO one except for that blank line after Launcher.DataCenterService.GLS.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-27
**LotROLinux-0.7.2**
Hopefully I have tracked down most of the areas where the launcher bombs if it hits an unsuspected problem (if you find another just shout).

I've also added an option to save the stuff wine chucks out when running the game, in case you need to save the log for bug report purposes. It gets saved to a file called run.log in the .LotROLinux folder, default is off.

One thing I will say is that if you are logging, when you quit the game the launcher window will be present and looking like it has hung, it hasn't and it will close once it has finished writing the log out.

Did a scan of the LOTRO logo from the box, so it looks better than the last one.

### Post by Bad-Taste on 2008-01-27
Hi,

I never had a solution for this (ACCESS_VIOLATION 0x0000005) thing.

I think my big problem is this one:

```
ixme:d3d_surface:flush_to_framebuffer_drawpixels >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> GL_INVALID_VALUE (0x501) from glDrawPixels @ surface.c / 1060
```

I searched a lot and found only this:

```
"( i checked surface.c from wine and my guess is, that there is a bug when updating only one line of pixels or one pixel alone - the height value becomes 0 and is invalid - i havent compiled wine yet though with some checks to test that, i might do that later on today)"
```

from: [http://forums.s2games.com/showthread.php?t=6963&page=4](http://forums.s2games.com/showthread.php?t=6963&page=4)

and

```
On Wednesday 16 January 2008 20:31:51 you wrote:
> > fixme:d3d_surface:flush_to_framebuffer_drawpixels >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
> > GL_INVALID_VALUE (0x501) from glDrawPixels @ surface.c / 1061
>
> You should get see what gets passed to glDrawPixels. According to the
> opengl man pages GL_INVALID_VALUE can occur if e.g. the dimensions are
> invalid. If these are indeed invalid, you need to figure out why they are
> wrong (I see that the pitch is 6400, which could mean a width of 1600
> assuming bpp=4)

This is a trace of the variables used to create the glDrawPixels call:

trace:d3d_surface:flush_to_framebuffer_drawpixels right 0, left 0, bottom 0,
top 0, mem 0x5db0040, bpp 4, pitch 6400

I assume GL is complaining because the rectangle passed to it has a size of
(0,-1).

Adding a simple right>left and bottom>top condition at least resolves this
error. However, whether that is correctly emulating D3D behavior, I don't
know.

```

from: [http://www.nabble.com/Shader-compiler-GL-error-td14883870.html](http://www.nabble.com/Shader-compiler-GL-error-td14883870.html)

The same information i got from winehq,
but I dont know how to solve this.

Lotro can not log-in cause there is this surface.c bug I think.

I am hoping for every new wine release :-(

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-01-28
> **Estupido said:**
> I'm to the part in the install process where I get incorrect account information by using the GUI launcher.

I've only been using Ubuntu (even Linux for that matter) for a day now after my Windows crapped out on my laptop's hd, so I'm not too familiar with some things. For example, I didn't have a Direct3D folder in the Registry Editor using wine and no matter what I do, the user .ini file will not create.
~E
What are the permissions on your "The Lord Of The Rings Online" folder?

If you don't know how to find out, here's how:

In a terminal/shell *cd* into the folder mentioned above and execute this command:
*ls -la*

The permissions for the each file is 10 first letters on each line:
lrwxrwxrwx

The first letter doesn't matter, the rest are groups of 3. First 3 letters describes the file owners permissions, Read, Write and eXecute. The next 3 the groups and the last 3 describe other users permissions.

The current folder is the entry with just a single dot as the "file name".

Damn, I hope the above makes any sense at all, if you're not too familar with Linux.

The missing .ini file is no doubt not created since LOTRO never starts, since the account information is wrong (possibly not saved, due to incorrect permissions).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-28
> **cthulhu666 said:**
> Thanks for the suggestion, but "no", I'm not running 64 bit. I've read about far too many problems and too few advantages, so I decided against it (so far).

I don't know what version of GCC Gentoo uses but on my Gutsy system it is 4.1.3.

### Post by SNy on 2008-01-28
OK, mainly interesting for Hedgewan, I just put up a new version of the launcher script onto my site (v0.9.9b).

(No, the file you sent was not the right one, but I downloaded the thing myself now and found the issue.)

To the issue:
For DDO, the downloaded GLSDataCenter.config file does actually contain two <Datacenter> sections, one for DDO, one for DDOBeta (which doesn't have any game servers associated but exists anyway).
What happened there was that every piece of extraction happened to see the entries from BOTH the DDO as well as the DDOBeta <Datacenter> sections. That's why there were these strange wget requests with the line breaks in them (%0A).

I now changed the script to only put the <Datacenter> section matching the target game name into a GLSDataCenter.config.${game} file (e.g. GLSDataCenter.config.LOTROEU) and use that one for all further processing.
I have tested this with my LOTRO installation and it works fine. I have also tested it with the DDO exe.config file Hedgewan provided and it at least started with downloading the launcher settings. Cannot test further as I don't own DDO.

Anyway, please test and report back.

SNy

--
Edit:
Oh, PS, I will not be around until Thursday in the evening (CET) due to a business trip to Hungary. Just in case I did something horribly wrong, please keep a backup of the script launcher if you test the new version. d;

---

### Post by Estupido on 2008-01-28
> **cthulhu666 said:**
> What are the permissions on your "The Lord Of The Rings Online" folder?

If you don't know how to find out, here's how:

In a terminal/shell *cd* into the folder mentioned above and execute this command:
*ls -la*

The permissions for the each file is 10 first letters on each line:
lrwxrwxrwx

The first letter doesn't matter, the rest are groups of 3. First 3 letters describes the file owners permissions, Read, Write and eXecute. The next 3 the groups and the last 3 describe other users permissions.

The current folder is the entry with just a single dot as the "file name".

Damn, I hope the above makes any sense at all, if you're not too familar with Linux.

The missing .ini file is no doubt not created since LOTRO never starts, since the account information is wrong (possibly not saved, due to incorrect permissions).

The . file when just into the LotRO folder is: rwxr-xr-x (thanks for the memory of the meanings again, i remembered what they stood for, but not why there were three)... I used linux for a year back when I was in computer engineering, 4 years ago. So i'm a rusty noob at best, but I have good memory once I start using things again. I made my own launcher from the desktop, so I'm stoked for that.

And no, no crazy symbols in my l/p, just letters and numbers.

~E

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-29
> **lunadog said:**
> Anyone know why patching the hires graphics does not work under wine?
One of two reasons, either we aren't calling LOTROs patching dll right or that patching dll has a bug in it. If it is the latter then hpefully come book 12 it will be fixed. If it is the former then hopefully we will get a helpful hint or someone who can understand assembly better than me can explain how we need to call it.

> Also, what are the chances patching will work -> next book to be released?
Almost guaranteed, Turbine have used this method of patching for DDO and LOTRO, they seem to like it and it seems to work (Codemasters could do with getting a more powerful patch server though).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-30
**LotROLinux-0.7.3**
- Improved exception handling including warning if game directory not found
- Make a change to how the username and password is passed when being authenticated, should resolve the verification failing
- The intermediate configuration files fetched are now saved in the .LotROLinux directory and named the same as the CLI launcher, in case they are needed for problem tracking purposes

---

### Post by Alpha4 on 2008-01-30
Try installing Envy:
[http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html]("http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html")
It automatically downloads and sets up the latest ATI or NVIDIA drivers from their websites.
It's very simple and fast, you don't need to do anything, only let him download the dependencies and drivers...
Good luck! :)

### Post by DarthMort on 2008-01-30
> **ajackson said:**
> Ah are you trying to run LOTRO with Cedega? The last time I tried (ok 3 months back) it didn't work.

Hello,

No Im not trying to run LOTRO under Cedega. I was using Wine to run Lotro.

Error below was displayed by Wine not by ubuntu *(thats why I was trying to install Catalyst drivers under wine).*
Hardware texture compression support was not detected. This video card feature is required to run the game. [129]

I have installed proprietary drivers for my ATI card and all tests shows that 3D and rendering passed these tests. Im sorry Im using Ubuntu for 4-5 days and sometimes documentation its not clear to me , even how nicely its written.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-01-30
> **DarthMort said:**
> Error below was displayed by Wine not by ubuntu *(thats why I was trying to install Catalyst drivers under wine).*
Hardware texture compression support was not detected. This video card feature is required to run the game. [129]
I think I might know why you got that error, if you look in your home directory there should be a folder called Lord of the Rings or something similar, in there there should be a file called UserPreferences.ini, edit that and find AllowFakeFullScreen, change it to False (I'm assuming it is True at the moment). I forgot that LOTRO does crash with an error the first time you run it (as it hasn't created the UserPref file yet).

> Im sorry Im using Ubuntu for 4-5 days and sometimes documentation its not clear to me , even how nicely its written.
No worries we all had to learn (and get confused) at some stage.

---

### Post by DarthMort on 2008-01-30
Hello,

I noticed that Registry settings has to be changed 

HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/UseGLSL = enabled
HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize = < amount of video memory ie 256 >*


Im missing Direct3D section completely, so I guess I have to add that to registry settings. Could you please post screenshot of those registry settings, so I could better understand how it should look like.

and then this

[http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=1276&SearchType=advanced&referrer=&CustID=&rfield=&usertype=&formaction=search&bUseEditor=&IncludeHTML=&gpn=&gpv=&keyword=quit&match=and&catID1=4&catID2=&catID3=&catID4=&sortmethod=rel+desc%2C+datemodified&submitbutton=Go]("http://turbine.fuzeqna.com/lotro.support/consumer/kbdetail.asp?kbid=1276&SearchType=advanced&referrer=&CustID=&rfield=&usertype=&formaction=search&bUseEditor=&IncludeHTML=&gpn=&gpv=&keyword=quit&match=and&catID1=4&catID2=&catID3=&catID4=&sortmethod=rel+desc%2C+datemodified&submitbutton=Go")

It says that there might be some problems with the firewall after patching, for example. I suppose this is because the executable(s) of the game has changed. If you are behind some kind of firewall, be sure to someway "refresh" the permission rules, or restart the firewall.
If that shouldn't work.. maybe I can suggest a game reinstall/repatch, depending of the starting version you have.. Good luck! :)

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-03
> Should I be asking a stupid question that has been answered 100 times over I apologize, and would not be upset to be pointed to a helpful howto or the like.

From the [LOTRO AppDB page]("http://appdb.winehq.org/appview.php?iAppId=4891")

*"*NOTE: In some newer distros like Ubuntu 7.10 this command line script fails as newer versions of wget need a "--no-check-certificate" switch after every wget command to work properly, please uncomment (remove the leading #) the following line on lotrolauncher.script: #wgetNoCheckCertOption="--no-check-certificate""*

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-08
**LotROLinux-0.7.4**
Various changes:
- No longer crashes if authentication server down
- the .deb file now depends on the mono base lib package (before it didn't so you in effect not be able to run it without manually installing something else)
- I've switch to using the 2.0 framework as it made looking after the realm and language lists easier, also it made detecting the wrong version of patchclient a bit more easier (ie doesn't look like an awful hack now), so if you build your own you might have to install mono-gmcs if you haven't already got it
- The launcher now deals with two versions of each game (and the switch game option has changed to reflect that), so if you have the test client you can set that up to work with the launcher now
- Rewrote the makefile as the one monodevelop created was awkward when adding new files (had to edit in two places)

Any problems let me know, the repository should have the new version on now (I had a problem while updating it).

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-11
> **Ferrat said:**
> It works like a wrapper, yukon starts then it launches the program, will take a look at the LotROLinux file
If it is a wrapper i.e. ran yukon gamename gameparams then try this mod to StartGame.cs

Find
```
game.StartInfo.FileName = wineProgram;
```
Change to
```
game.StartInfo.FileName = "yukon.exe";[/quote]
You might need to add the entire patch name to yukon {i.e. if it is in c:\Program Files\Yukon\yukon.exe set it to "c:\Program Files\Yukon\yukon.exe you will probably have to exact the the case of the names, I'm not too certain)

Find
[code]game.StartInfo.Arguments = String.Format("{0} {1} {2}", wineParams, appName, gameParams);
```
Change to
```
game.StartInfo.Arguments = String.Format("{0} {1} {2} {3}", wineProgram, wineParams, appName, gameParams);
```
If you need to pass any parameters to yukon add them before the {0}, i.e if you need to pass -n the quoted bit will start "-n {0}....".

It might work and it might not.

### Post by Fator dee on 2008-02-13
So, can you update to Book 12 in linux or do you need a windows installation to do it? Does the Updater_0.0_1.06.exe work with wine? How-To would be much appreciated.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-13
> **Fator dee said:**
> So, can you update to Book 12 in linux or do you need a windows installation to do it? Does the Updater_0.0_1.06.exe work with wine? How-To would be much appreciated.
If you installed LOTRO using wine then just run the update executable (i.e. wine Updater_0.0_1.06.exe) and it sets everything up for either of the launchers to patch when it goes live.

If you copied an installation from a windows box you might have trouble if the game is not installed in the default directory as the registry entry that tells the update executable where to put the files doesn't exist. Again just run the update executable using wine, it will either work or not work, if it doesn't you need to update via windows.

---

```
BOOTING GRE
