### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-14
> **badrunner said:**
> Hi ajcackson, I'm getting the following error when trying to do an update on hardy:

Any ideas what it might be? I dont know if the error is printed by mono or wine, but i know that the patch process definately used to work on this setup before upgrading to hardy (although i had only used it to to update the advertising image).
It is coming out of Mono, I tried to set the patching process to a slightly higher priority but it doesn't seem to work so I'm removing that in the version I'm currently tweaking.

If you've compiled yourself you just remove the line
```
patch.PriorityClass = ProcessPriorityClass.AboveNormal;
```
from PatchWindow.cs.

The actual exception might be being caused because hardy is still work in progress, I don't know what version of mono they are using or if it is getting the updates, it could be a bug in mono.

---

### Post by badrunner on 2008-02-14
Thanks that does fix it. 

FYI you really shouldnt be setting the process priority higher, especially for a completely io bound process that has no need for even soft realtime, all your doing is stepping on the toes of other processes, for no gain at all.

> **ajackson said:**
> It is coming out of Mono, I tried to set the patching process to a slightly higher priority but it doesn't seem to work so I'm removing that in the version I'm currently tweaking.

If you've compiled yourself you just remove the line
```
patch.PriorityClass = ProcessPriorityClass.AboveNormal;
```from PatchWindow.cs.

The actual exception might be being caused because hardy is still work in progress, I don't know what version of mono they are using or if it is getting the updates, it could be a bug in mono.

---

> **badrunner said:**
> FYI you really shouldnt be setting the process priority higher, especially for a completely io bound process that has no need for even soft realtime, all your doing is stepping on the toes of other processes, for no gain at all.
Yeah not one of my better ideas, I thought it would help with the strange lag in the text that the patching process throws out but it seems it has no impact whatsoever on anything (might not be implemented yet for all I know).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-14
**LotROLinux-0.7.5**
All changes are patch related. I've tried to get it parsing the output from the US version of patchclient better, it is slightly better but there is definite lag between what it does and what it spits out (the text I use for parsing).

With that in mind there is an additional option in with the rest of the options that lets you choose either a complex patch window (the one that mirrors the original launcher) or a simple one which basically just spits out the stuff into a status box. The difference is purely cosmetic as the underlying patching is the same.


I have done some tests with the US version and it still does not patch the hi-res graphics files, I would assume the EU version will also have that problem (if they ever get the servers back up :))

---

### Post by badrunner on 2008-02-14
> **ajackson said:**
> Yeah not one of my better ideas, I thought it would help with the strange lag in the text that the patching process throws out but it seems it has no impact whatsoever on anything (might not be implemented yet for all I know).

Yeah, i think it wasnt implement in mono at all until recently (hence not a problem on gutsy), and i would assume it relies on the pthreads call of the same, which in turn relies on root privilleges ;)

You might want to look at how you are getting the text, the lag is far more likely to be caused by some buffering going on that waits much longer than you want. If you are just capturing the stdout of a process, you probably want to look at flushing that buffer when you need to read from it.

---

Trouble is you can't flush a stream reader. ### Post by Ferrat on 2008-02-16
Patching at least DDO takes ages since it applys every updated file once it has downloaded it like this 

download 1 file
apply that file
download another
apply the new one 
and so on 

this prevents from actually gain much speed when downloading, perk is that it's more secure since if you crash at 95% your previously downloaded files are allready applied and can't become corrupt as if you downloaded a 1GB patch file and it got corrupted when almost finished. 

but yeah I got a 24Mbit connection and updating DDO for me in windows as well as linux takes hours but 50 / 2000 in 3-4 hours sound really slow even for their patch system, my DDO update was about 2000 and that took a total of 5-6 hours I think

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-21
> **drummingpariah said:**
> I wasn't aware that you had a .deb for it already.  Do you have a repo set up as well (automatic updates would be awfully shiny)?

I can't remember what page they are buried on in this thread but you can get the info via the link in my signature but I'll repost it here.

Use one of:

```
deb http://www.lotrolinux.com/ubuntu gutsy main
deb http://www.lotrolinux.com/ubuntu dapper main
```
If you use any other debian based system try the gutsy one, if that doesn't work try the dapper one as that should work on most systems.

There are two versions of the packages: lotrolinux-gecko uses the mozilla renderer for the web and lotrolinux uses the non-mozilla renderer.

>   I haven't checked in on anything LOTRO in awhile, and to be honest this project interests me more than the game.
I must admit I feel the same way, LOTRO could have been so much better, it deserves a lot better, maybe Turbine just wasn't the right developer. Not that I'm saying the game is bad just poorly implemented (and CMs servers are a joke).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-25
> **Vaitya said:**
> I have read the majority of this forum and have gotten tantalizingly close to making LOTRO work on my Ubuntu install. However, when I try to launch the game it appears to crash. I am not sure why, but I have ot assume there's a problem with one or more of the following: 1) graphics settings, though I have altered the UserPreferences.ini as indicated in the HOw to's. 2) there's a problem with the graphics card drivers I have - I have an ATI Radeon 2400Pro. I used a link posted in this forum to acquire a program call Envy which auto installed my drivers. or 3) I'm not installing LOTRO correctly to start with.
My guess would be driver problems and/or configuration.

If you edit the script that you run you'll find a line near the bottom that starts WINEDEBUG, insert a # before that (ie comment out that line) and try again. You should see a few more things on the screen (all fixme's) but sometimes they give a clue as to what is upsetting wine.

The other thing to try is running the script with --HighResOutOfDate (ie bash lotrolauncher.script --HighResOutOfDate) to see if it is that causing the problem.

A third possibility is that if the UserPrefs file is copied from a windows set up to rename it and allow the game to generate a new one (it will crash first time because of the AllowFakeFullScreen setting). But you might have already done this one.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-02-27
There has been one interesting development regarding wine and .NET 1.1, it is now possible (using the git version of wine) to install the base .NET 1.1 package.

I'm currently trying to install LOTRO on top of that to see what happens but getting .NET 1.1 installing is a good first step as now we can file proper bugs about the original launcher.

Mono 1.9 is also out in testing at the moment so I'm going to see if I have any joy with that on the original launcher as well.

Edit: with mono 1.9 it complains about the version of .NET but that maybe a configuration issue.

With .NET 1.1 it can't update to service pack 1 due to a stack overflow problem. But I guess that is the next problem for the devs to look into.

### Post by Vaitya on 2008-02-29
OK, I Googled around a little bit and found a similar issue someone had for StarCraft. This was the solution by amunimanghi:

____
I am your saviour today. Open up a console and type 'regedit'. Then go to
Code:

HKEY_current_user > software > wine > X11 driver

Right click in the white area and select "New > String Value". Type in "UseXRandR" without the quotes and hit enter. Then double click what you just made and type 'N'. Quit regedit and start your game up. It should work in full screen.
_____

This did actually change the output from the failed start - 

Connecting to world login queue for realm Landroval...
WARNING: Certificate verification error for launcher.turbine.com: unable to get local issuer certificate
18:58:49 URL:[https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php](https://launcher.turbine.com/login_queue.php) [325/325] -> ".launcher/WorldQueue.config" [1]
Ready. Now starting the client...
err:advapi:service_control_dispatcher pipe connect failed
fixme:win:EnumDisplayDevicesW ((null),0,0x34f818,0x00000000), stub!
err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered
err:ole:CoGetClassObject no class object {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} could be created for context 0x1
err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered

### Post by Vaitya on 2008-02-29
Found a fix for the following error - doesn't have any impact on anything though - seems superficial.

"err:advapi:service_control_dispatcher pipe connect failed"

Just open winecfg and select graphics tabs and uncheck "Allow the window manager to control the windows"

The killer seems to be related to:

"fixme:d3d:IWineD3DImpl_CheckDeviceMultiSampleType Quality levels unsupported at present"

Can't seem to find a solution to this.

### Post by Vaitya on 2008-03-02
Success!  

Thanks all for your suggestions. 

There must be an issue with the ATI graphics drivers running in the 64-bit vers of Ubuntu. I installed the 32-bit version of 7.10, used the ubuntu package of Wine, installed the ATI drivers with Envy, and followed basically all of the other steps outlines in the guide (which I now have mostly memorized). Once I updated my game it all worked like a charm.

On a performance note - I was able to change the in-game graphics settings (only at the char selection screen) and can run everything on the highest levels. The only setting I cannot turn on is the post processing thing. When I do my avatar and some other elements in the game appear inverted/upside down. There were 2 small things that I did notice running around on ultra high. These were that every once in a while I'd be running along and get "teleported" backwards five - ten steps. This quite often occurred when running up to loot a corpse. The other problem I noticed occurred when I switched characters. The world did not load up properly...much of the landscape was not drawn, or shaded in with what appeared to be contour lines rather than grass etc. When I tried to set the graphics levels down while logged into the char the game crashed.

Anyway, my next step is to attempt to get ajackson's graphical launcher up and running. 

Thanks to all of you - I'm now probably about 95% windows free!

Vaitya

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-04
> **Vaitya said:**
> Another odd thing is that both my linux box and my xp desk-top are not showing the intro movie - and the xp box is now operating without sound period. I'll  have to read the forums I guess and see if it was a patch etc that broke me.
I don't know about the sound problem but with book 12 once you have seen the intro movie once it doesn't play it again, due mainly to players demanding that change.

> Thank you. I was able to get it going with little trouble. Not sure, but does the launcher have a script that pulls updates built into it, or do I need to run sny's script and get them?
At the moment it doesn't do an auto update but you can get it to update for you, select Tools -> Patch then click the apply button, it might look like it freezes but that is down to the gui needed the output text from Turbines patch routine to update the status texts and for some reason that text gets buffered. But it does work though it can't update the hi-res graphics file but that seems to be a problem with patch function Turbine exposed for us to use.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-05
> **artmedia said:**
> Hi ,
i just upgraded my wine to 0.9.56 and installed the newest drivers nvidia through envy and now i get an error about dinput.dll in the log file when the game should start up.
any ideas?

That looks like a corrupted file to me. To repair it you need to run the repair utility.

If you installed via wine just run lotrosetup.exe using wine, if it is a copy from windows you could try recopying in case the problem happened during copy or run lotrosetup.exe under windows.

If that doesn't fix it then it could be a memory problem, run memtest86 for a few hours to see if that shows any problems.

---

### Post by artmedia on 2008-03-05
yep that did the trick ... after playing once i did get the same error ...strange...but this time i made a backup of the files at least

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-13
> **Zeion said:**
> my only problem for now is missing driver support from nvidia, my laptops 7300 nvidia geforce card cannot be supported easily
I'd have thought the nVidia drivers can handle a 7300. On the previous page there is a link for envy, that is very good at getting nvidia drivers and ubuntu working happily together.

> i signed the native lotro linux client partition, lets all of us join the dev chat, last wednesday of the month, maybe they will get their eyes open if they get "mass invated" by linux uses asking for a native client, so they can see what a massive amount of players they are ruleing out of the game. what do you think of that?.
A completely native linux version will not happen, the game is written to use directx, to recode it to be based on opengl would be time consuming and cost a lot of money. The game works fine under wine, the only thing they can do to help is make their launcher more wine/linux friendly but people are actively working on getting .NET working under wine, when they do wine should have no problems with Turbines launcher.

Of course in the meantime it would be nice if their patching routine patched the high-res files for us (or they gave some more hints about how to make it do it if it does already). But just exposing that patch function was a huge gesture towards the linux users who play LOTRO so you can't knock them for that.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-14
> **Vaitya said:**
> I've been playing in high res for most of the time since I've got this game working on Unbutu (Thanks to everyone's help here). It is possible to play in High Res without having fie updates for High Res?

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-16

> Anyway, does this just sound like a server problem? That I'm not really downloading or even checking for patches? I've been trying it for maybe 30 mins now but I guess it could just mean that the patch servers are down or something. Any ideas? Thanks.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-03-16
> **ajackson said:**
> 
Did you install via wine or copy a windows setup? I'm just wondering if this newer message (client version) is down to missing registry entries.

### Post by ct_traveller on 2008-03-16
> **ajackson said:**
> When you say you have multiple accounts do you mean two (or more) of the same game (ie LOTRO)? I didn't even know that was possible, how does the original launcher handle that (might give me a clue of how to get it working)?

In the windows launcher, after choosing world and loggin in, I get a list of the GAME accounts that I have available to log into.

It goes something like this....  You have BILLING accounts, and in those billing accounts you have game accounts...

Right now what happens is that after logging in to the BILLING account, I am taken straight to the GAME account that was last logged in.

So, right now, if I want to log in to a different GAME account, I have to log in through a windows computer (or reboot my laptop into Vi$ta.. yech) and log into the GAME account I want, then go back to the Ubuntu box and log into the Billing account that is the umbrella for the GAME account I just logged in Winblows...  

So I guess there is another list that needs to be queried?  Am going to try various iterations of BILLNGACCOUNTNAME (  :  ;  .  -) or some other list seperator followed by GAMEACCOUNTNAME when I get time to go back to game.

Sorry .. to elaborate..

When you buy multiple copies and set the account up, you use the individual numbers of each boxed set to register the game/account.  Once that is done, you can play any account from ONE installation of the game with the appropriate log in details that are linked to each purchased/registered copy.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-17
> **ct_traveller said:**
> bleh where does Lotrolinux get installed to when using synaptic?  Yeah having a brain fart and I can't find it.
The launching script is in /usr/bin, the actual program is /usr/lib/LotROLinux and the config files/logs/etc are in ~/.LotROLinux (ie your home directory in a hidden folder called .LotROLinux).

---

### Post by ct_traveller on 2008-03-17
Yes, GlsAuthServer.config has that type of structure.

I would post but it has personal info in it.  The game account names have their own sections with <name> in the <gamesubscription> sections.  

The <name> though are not clear text, but coded (hex?)  although <description> has the clear text name of the game accounts NOT (gamename) ie Game 1, is the game account name not LOTRO or DDO   ( I Don't play DDO)

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-18
**LotROLinux-0.7.6**
Only one change, if a user account has multiple game accounts you will now get a window pop up asking you to choose which game account to use.

Also the debs are no longer housed at [www.lotrolinux.com](www.lotrolinux.com), I have a ppa set up over at Ubuntu as it makes my life easier for maintaining the non-compile side of the releases.

The new locations are:
```

deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/ajackson-bcs/ubuntu hardy main
deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/ajackson-bcs/ubuntu gutsy main
deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/ajackson-bcs/ubuntu dapper main

```

Obviously only use one (non-Ubuntu debian users can try each in turn until they find one that works).

Two things I've noticed on hardy, when you compile you get a warning about using an obsolete function but it still works (I'll see if I can fix it when hardy is released) and when you run the gecko version you might see some messages on the console regarding mozilla, it works on my system despite those messages but if you have problems let me know.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-20
> **duncanbell said:**
> 
```
WARNING: Certificate verification error for launcher.turbine.com: unable to get local issuer certificate
```

If you are getting an error like that then check to see if you have the
```
wgetNoCheckCertOption="--no-check-certificate"
```
line uncommented in the script. If that doesn't fix it then it could be a driver issue, what graphics card do you have?

---

Anyway: here's what I've done:

- Installed ATI's fgrlx driver through Envy (this is where the problem lies, I'm sure...ATI). This works, the fgl_gears etc. whatever work, fglrxinfo gives the right info etc.
- Installed wine and Lotro from the DVD according to WineDB instructions (incl. regedit settings).
- Copied over the whole working patched lotro directory from my Gutsy machine to the laptop overwriting the installation.
- Installed graphical lotrolauncher from the repo
- patched (no patches needed)

and now when launching, it loads the start screen and then drops me to the desktop. I get this in the log file. 

```
000000005.531: ----CRASH REPORT START----

000000005.531: Program fault: ACCESS_VIOLATION (0xc0000005) trying to read from 0x0000000C

000000005.531: Detailed report:

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-21
The error is a memory related problem, double check your VideoMemorySize option in regedit, maybe try reducing it if it is ok (don't laptops used shared memory or something to give the video card more?).

My guess however is a driver problem, you could try searching the official forums to see if anyone mentions your graphics card (ie is there a known problem with that card and LOTRO). I know that the Hardy version of Envy was recently updated because of a bug with the ATI driver, don't know if it affected the non-Hardy version though but I'd double-check that you have the latest Envy just in case.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-03-25

> Actually i tested it a bit more yesterday and seems that when i am using cable this issue is far less frequent didnt happen at all. So i would guess my problem is with the wireless drivers or with my thompson router.
The stuttering is a general LOTRO problem, it seems related to packet loss and from what I can tell some wireless connections under linux suffer more than a wired connection under linux (probably a driver issue) but the same connection under windows would also likely suffer. Turbine/CM seem to want to ignore the problem or blame peoples internet connections even though they were working fine before the book 12 (or was it 11) update.

I have tried to do everything posted off this 100+ page thread including all the UserPreferences.ini and regedit hacks. No joy! I launch the Linux Launcher GUI, enter in password and server, the logo screen comes up, and then it drops and nothing.  Anyone know the fix for this?
That is the exact same problem I have when trying to run LOTRO on Gentoo (described [here](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=4202424)). My "fix" is Ubuntu which works just fine.

If you come up with a solution I'd love to hear about it (hint: PM me), as I hate having to reboot to play LOTRO, since all my other stuff is on Gentoo.

> **tramvaika said:**
> Have been running now LOTRO for 2 days on Ubuntu. All seems fine except one thing - my character quite often simply freezes in place - i cannot move or do anything meaniful besides turning around. It can last up to few minutes!
I get a lot of "back stepping", where I'm frequently thrown a few steps backwards. It might be related to what you're experiencing. Only I'm on a 25 Mbit (up and down) connection, so if it's network related, it's gotta be my router/gateway.
> **tramvaika said:**
> I also get occasionally an error in launcher script terminal window:
err:ole:CoGetClassObject class {4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24} not registered
This error is due to a missing dll. I found a fix for it on a french forum (long live google translate). Here's the google translation:
> You should create a value chain (4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24) in HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT \ CLSID \ and put "WBEM Locator" in
default
Then create HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT \ CLSID \ (4590f811-1d3a-11d0-891f-00aa004b2e24) \ InProcServer32 and put "wbemprox.dll" in default.

Assure yourself that you have good wbemprox.dll in .wine/drive_c/windows/system32

Otherwise you can download it HERE: [http://www.driverskit.com/dll/wbemprox.dll/3860.html](http://www.driverskit.com/dll/wbemprox.dll/3860.html) 

I found that I had to download another wbem-something dll as well. It did indeed remove the error. Unfortunately it didn't make LOTRO work on Gentoo. :(
It works fine on Ubuntu without this fix.

I do however experience a whole different problem, that I would very much like some help with. After playing for some time (sometimes a few minutes other times several hours), I loose the ability to click on anything. I can move around using the keyboard just fine, but I can't select or interact with anything. It seems to happen quicker if I try to ALT+TAB out of LOTRO, which doesn't work for me.
I don't know if it's related to Wine's graphics options, but I've tried almost all possible combinations on the "Graphics" tab in winecfg. What's the recommend settings here?

**LotROLinux-9.7.7**
Made a DDO specific change, you can now specify the name of the DLL file to use instead of patchclient.dll when patching.

Disclaimer: I accept no responsibility for any damage caused to your system should you not use the version of patchclient.dll supplied by the game.


I also made a slight change that may make the whole patch window more responsive when patching, it might also make no difference whatsoever :)

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-03-27
> **Vaitya said:**
> I use an ATI card and it does not handle the post-processing at all. The display is very dark and some things appear upside down.

For me (Nvidia), this exact thing happens with shadow higher than low and PP ON. Have you tried turning post-processing ON and then turning shadows LOW or even OFF? Give it a shot. For me, the upside down trees and dark spots disappear when shadows are adjusted down.

Post-processing (water effect, really) is what makes the game pretty, I think, so I messed witn the options quite a bit to find how I could leave it on.

> I do however experience a whole different problem, that I would very much like some help with. After playing for some time (sometimes a few minutes other times several hours), I loose the ability to click on anything. I can move around using the keyboard just fine, but I can't select or interact with anything. It seems to happen quicker if I try to ALT+TAB out of LOTRO, which doesn't work for me.
I don't know if it's related to Wine's graphics options, but I've tried almost all possible combinations on the "Graphics" tab in winecfg. What's the recommend settings here?
I enabled the WINEDEBUG and got the following error in the terminal after experiencing the above described problem:
```
fixme:d3d:handleStreams Tangent and binormal bump mapping is only valid with vertex shaders
```

This is with wine 0.9.55, a 7950GT and nvidia-driver 100.14.19.

I'll try upgrading wine to 0.9.58 and see if that helps. If not it's probably some LOTRO setting, I just need to figure out which one... As a last resort, I'll try and upgrade the nvidia-driver.

*EDIT: Nevermind, I googled and got a hit (on this very thread, page 26) and found the solution: I had StencilShadows=medium and LandscapeStaticObjectShadows=medium. They should both be disabled. Perhaps they work on "medium" in 0.9.58?*

*EDIT2: Hmmm... disabling those two Shadow options didn't make the fixmes go away :( But at least I haven't experienced the loss of mouse input, yet.*

### Post by SNy on 2008-04-10
Hey, I've just put up a new version of the script (0.9.9c) onto my page.
A few things have changed:
 - it now asks if you want to patch (default is no)
 - it does transform the language code to lower-case (first two letters)
   -> that makes patching the splash screen image work
 - it does check for the number of active subscriptions for the account
 - it lets you choose from your active subscriptions if more than one is found

Please test and report back.
Thanks.

SNy

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-04-22
> **Dimath said:**
> I played LOTRO on the same PC with Ubuntu Gutsy and different wine version, and different ATI driver and it worked fine, but I am not sure if I had any settings different. Should I try older ATI driver/older wine version? What would be your suggestions?
I'm not up to speed on ATI driver version so bear with me.

I remember reading a thread (it might even have been one of the stickies) in the Hardy Development thread where they were talking about the new ATI drivers and how good they were, I don't know if that is the driver you are using or not.

But your problem does look driver related to me, mind you with wine adding to it's directx9 with each release it could be that using something like wine 0.9.58 (59 had a few bugs) might get better results, i.e. wine isn't showing up the short comings in the ATI drivers as much.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-04-24
> **Dimath said:**
> Thanks guys.
I installed wine-0.9.58 (compiled from sources, without warnings) but it is less success now. I am getting just console messages and the game is not starting. I am using latest ATI drivers, i guess. Not sure what to do...

Sorry I gave you a bit of bad advice there, it seems that with kernel 2.6.24-16 only wine 0.9.60 will work due to that kernel defining an area of memory that can not be remapped (or something like that).

The problem is graphics driver related, hopefully now that Hardy is released (today is the day) there will be a new set of guides around getting ATI cards working smoothly (since those drivers have improved a lot).

The only other advice I can give is try setting LOTRO to the minimum graphics (for everything) and slowly try new settings to see if it is certain settings causing the problems.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-04-24
**The future for LotROLinux**
Since the new LTS version of Ubuntu is being released (Hardy) pre-built debs for Dapper will no longer be created after the .1 release (about a month for now). You should still be able to compile it from source but any modifications will be aimed at Mono version 1.2, Dapper does not have this version but I doubt there will be any need for changes as it seems to do the job fine at the moment (well patching is dodgy).

The Dapper repository will remain up beyond Hardy's .1 release only if that version is the same as on the Hardy and Gutsy repositories.

The Gutsy version may also cease to be pre-built as most people will move onto Hardy in the next couple of months.

The Hardy version will keep being built until the next LTS version of Ubuntu and hopefully by that time wine will be able to handle the original launcher.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-04-27
> **Savakker said:**
> Ive deleted vista by mistake
Having had the misfortune to use Vista I wouldn't class deleting it as a mistake :)

> Things like this...
First download [COLOR="Red"]fakeNET11.reg[/COLOR] and [COLOR="Red"]run using regedit fakeNET11.reg[/COLOR], this fools the installer into believing that .NET 1.1 is already installed, without this the installer will attempt to install .NET and fail

mean nothing to me at all in the sense of how do I do it???
First up make sure you have wine installed, it is best if you use the version from the winehq site, do that open up a terminal and type these two commands:
```

wget -q http://wine.budgetdedicated.com/apt/387EE263.gpg -O- | sudo apt-key add -
sudo wget http://wine.budgetdedicated.com/apt/sources.list.d/hardy.list -O /etc/apt/sources.list.d/winehq.list

```
That adds winehq to the list of repositories (software stores) and the first line accepts the repos secured key (so that you know the software is genuine).

While in the terminal do
```

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install wine

```
That refreshes your computers list of available software and then installs wine.

Type
```
winecfg
```
Set the windows version to 2000 (that one works best).

The fakeNet11 is a link to a file so right click on it and select save as and save it on your computer. Then in your open terminal go to that directory and type
```
regedit fakeNET11.reg
```
That loads the information in that file into the fake windows registry (all it does is prevent LOTROs installer from doing a stage that fails at the moment).

Go to where you have the base program for LOTRO (i.e. the main installer), run that using
```
wine lotrosetup.exe
```
I normally choose custom setup so that I can stop it creating it's own launcher on my desktop as that one doesn't work.

Go find something to do for about 20 minutes :)

When you come back it should have installed and it probably is asking if you wish to launch the game, untick it (it won't start anyway).

Install LotROLinux (if it isn't already installed), set the correct game directory in options, start it up. First run should crashe (the userprefs problem), go edit userprefs and try again, this time it should work.

Hi folks,

I've tried to get LOTRO running a number of times in the last few weeks with 7.10 and now with 8.04. Here's details on what I've done so far with 8.04.

I have a fresh, vanilla, Ubuntu 8.04 system. I've installed WINE 0.9.59 through add/remove programs.
I installed the Restricted driver for my NV8800GT using the Restricted Driver Management built into Ubuntu. Can't seem to spot what version of the driver is running but it's whatever NVidia driver is part of 8.04
I installed the registry kludge to allow the installer to think .NET is there.
I installed LOTRO using WINE and then copied my working, patched, LOTRO Book 13 program folder from my Windows machine to save having to patch up the new install.
I installed the GUI launcher via the .deb package from Alan Jackson's repository
I added the two recommended Direct3D reg entries per the instructions at lotrolinux
I configured WINE to run lotroclient.exe as Windows 2000
When I run the GUI client it logs me in and goes to flash up the game.
There's a flick of the screen as it flips to fullscreen mode and then it ends and I'm looking at my desktop again.
If I run in Windowed mode, a window flips up briefly and then it ends.

The lotro launcher run log doesn't show anything particularly unusual.
There's no log in the lotro folder that has user prefs

So basically, it's not happening and I've no idea where to look! Any advice is appreciated!

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-03
> **srodden said:**
> I have a fresh, vanilla, Ubuntu 8.04 system. I've installed WINE 0.9.59 through add/remove programs.
Although the wine in the hardy reps is a modded version of 0.9.59 it still has issues, if you pop over to winehq you should find instructions for adding their repository, wine 0.9.60 seems pretty good (0.9.61 came out yesterday so should hit wines repos is a day or two).

> I installed the Restricted driver for my NV8800GT using the Restricted Driver Management built into Ubuntu. Can't seem to spot what version of the driver is running but it's whatever NVidia driver is part of 8.04
Should be 169.09, have a look at Applications -> System Tools, there may or may not be NVidia X Settings in there.

> I configured WINE to run lotroclient.exe as Windows 2000
When I run the GUI client it logs me in and goes to flash up the game.
There's a flick of the screen as it flips to fullscreen mode and then it ends and I'm looking at my desktop again.
If I run in Windowed mode, a window flips up briefly and then it ends.
I think that is one of the problems in wine 0.9.59, there was something wrong with it changing resolution (even when it doesn't need to), I've had a few things hang or crash because of that, wine 0.9.60 (or 61) seem to handle things better.

### Post by srodden on 2008-05-04
> **ajackson said:**
> I think that is one of the problems in wine 0.9.59, there was something wrong with it changing resolution (even when it doesn't need to), I've had a few things hang or crash because of that, wine 0.9.60 (or 61) seem to handle things better.
That did the trick, thank you. I was getting a "hash sum mismatch" on updating the repository but I dug into the tree and installed the .deb directly and it's working a treat now.

Incidentally, I had a problem with floaty names not being visible until really close to the mob and even then there were parts being obstructed from view if you were facing the mob directly. I found that turning off Post Processing Effects fixes that one. 

Thank you very much for your efforts, SNy and Alan.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-04
> **srodden said:**
> 
Incidentally, I had a problem with floaty names not being visible until really close to the mob and even then there were parts being obstructed from view if you were facing the mob directly. I found that turning off Post Processing Effects fixes that one. 
Press 'N' twice, once turns floaty names off, the second back on again, tends to fix that problem for me (though touch wood I haven't had it since 0.9.61 came out).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-06
**LotROLinux 0.9**
Before anyone asks I realised I'd jumped 0.8 when it was too late to sort out.

The big change is that the launcher now works with Crossover Games (and hopefully Crossover Office but I haven't tested that).

In the options window you can select Wine or one of the Crossovers, when a Crossover is selected the WINEPREFIX gets replaced with Bottle Name, just enter the name of the bottle you installed LOTRO into (remember case sensitive).

If your version of Crossover Games is installed somewhere other than /opt/cxgames or /home/username/cxgames give the full path to the wine program, otherwise it might try to use the wine version or just not find it.

The default place for the directory containing userprefs on my system was ~/Documents rather than ~/.

I've only got the demo version of CXGames but I got it to run ok but I didn't select a character and log in (the launcher has done it's bit by then). Patching works as well as ever (or as bad as ever depending on your point of view).

DEB versions are in the process of being built.

---

### Post by schtufbox on 2008-05-07
Have you edited the UserPreferences.ini  file? 
Specifically these lines:

AllowFakeFullScreen=False

FullScreen=True

If you ran LOTRO that file should have been autocreated in wherever your wine looks for My Documents. In default wine, it adds it in a Lord of the rings folder in my home folder. In crossover games, it adds it in Home/myusername/Documents.

### Post by buntunub on 2008-05-07
Ok, figured it out. Here is the answer and DONT say I didnt warn ya -

[PHP]sudo sysctl -w vm.mmap_min_addr=0[/PHP]

Sorry if this has been posted already. There are now numerous threads about this. Here is the problem:

> # protect bottom 64k of memory from mmap to prevent NULL-dereference
# attacks against potential future kernel security vulnerabilities.
# (Added in kernel 2.6.23.)
vm.mmap_min_addr = 65536

That, off a $cat /etc/sysctl.conf. It currently wreaks havoc on almost all games played via wine. It was a good idea for Linus to do that, but poor poor wine hasn't caught up yet. If your having issues with games in wine, give that a shot, and DONT FORGET to re enable it when your done!!!!

[PHP]sudo sysctl -w vm.mmap_min_addr=65536[/PHP]

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-08
> **buntunub said:**
> Ok, figured it out. Here is the answer and DONT say I didnt warn ya -

[PHP]sudo sysctl -w vm.mmap_min_addr=0[/PHP]
I knew that was an issue but I didn't know it could stop LOTRO from starting. Funnily enough on my computer it doesn't stop LOTRO starting, wonder why it does on some systems but not others?

But good spot and I'm glad it works for you.

---

### Post by buntunub on 2008-05-08
That prevented LOTRO from starting at all on my system, along with EQ2, and EVE Premium. As soon as I changed that value, it all worked flawlessly. When I reset it back to the old value, nothing launches in wine anymore. This is a touch and go thing, because literally, nothing else is impacted by this - just wine apps, and even then, just wine apps that are resource hungry, like LOTRO. That says alot about the state of Windows and Security. That "fix" was put in to prevent some extremely malicious exploits that could compromise your kernel - very remote chance of that happening like this - but still. Currently, I dont see any serious harm in setting the value to zero, but just dont keep it like that for an extended period.

There are a few references for this exploit, but [this]("http://osvdb.org/26839") one is the best. The others I searched through are all the usual boring geek speak. Notice the line -

> Currently, there are no known upgrades, patches, or workarounds available to correct this issue.

Wrong answer, thanks to Linus. Nothing quite like Microsoft Security though eh?

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-09
**wine 0.9.61 late find**
I know it's been out over a week but I found out that today you no longer need to set AllowFakeFullScreen to False in UserPrefs. I was looking at a way for the launcher to create a dummy UserPrefs to prevent the initial crash and found it was no longer a problem.

Now the only major problems with the game are the help system, the native launcher and installing without spoofing .NETs registry entries. The only game breaking one is the help system. Not bad considering this time last year we were struggling to get anything working.

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-05-11
For those who have followed my efforts on Gentoo and contributed with their highly valued efforts:

Success! I finally managed to get LOTRO working on Gentoo :D :D :D

It turned out it was a faulty hostname configuration. I had an entry in /etc/hosts ala:
```
127.0.0.1 blablabla localhost
```
This caused a winsock error in Wine and thus crashed LOTRO. Removing the hostname ("blablabla" in the above example) made LOTRO finally work.

Man have I been looking in all the wrong places...  :roll:

Thanks to all who have contributed to this thread and **ajackson** and **SNy** in particular for their highly valued efforts.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-12
> **evilviper77 said:**
> I would like to bring up something that just now reared it's ugly head. I installed LOTRO (archlinux distro) and it ran fine using the script launcher. But the server I play in is Brandywine which is now having population problems and the script doesn't work when you have to get into the queue to get in.

Is there a quick fix for this or am I just gonna have to wait till a good Samaritan comes out with a patch or my server doesn't have a queue?
That's going to need a change to the script or my launcher, I can see where to get the correct information from regarding the queue, it's just working out how to test it.

Basically in WorldQueue.config you have a QueueNumber (ie your position in the queue) and a NowServingNumber. If the serving number is not your number then you have to wait. But to find out when it is time you need to check the server.config (ie keep getting the server details) that has a nowservingqueuenumber, when that matches (or is greater than?) your number the client can start.

So the process is fairly simple to implement but testing could be a problem.

---

You have to take read through it all and follow the instructions, this is not really a click-n-play kind of thing. I've set this up on different computers / installations now a bunch of times, and it really only takes a few minutes to do the steps, but you do have to do it all and of course it gets easier once you've done a few times.

Here are the areas that I don't get:

regedit settings  [COLOR="Red"]THESE KEYS DON'T EXIST IN REGEDIT[/COLOR]

 HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/UseGLSL = enabled    **** wine-0.9.48 or less ****
 HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize = < amount of video memory ie 256 >*

UserPreferences.ini settings [COLOR="Red"]I HAVE NO USERPREFERENCES.INI FILE[/COLOR]

Note this file is found in the equivilant of your "My Documents\The Lord of the Rings" folder which defaults to ~/The Lord of the Rings

If you do not currently have a UserPreferences.ini file run the game using the script or gui it will crash with an error but will create a default UserPreferences.ini file

 AllowFakeFullScreen=False   **** wine-0.9.60 or less ****
 FullScreen=True   **** wine-0.9.51 or less ****

Those settings will enable you to start the game and log in as a character. If you change any settings via the graphics or advanced graphics tabs ensure that those setting are correct (ie choosing a new level of graphics via the graphics tab may alter some of these values)

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-12
> **mckinneycm said:**
> Here are the areas that I don't get:

regedit settings  [COLOR="Red"]THESE KEYS DON'T EXIST IN REGEDIT[/COLOR]

 HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/UseGLSL = enabled    **** wine-0.9.48 or less ****
 HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize = < amount of video memory ie 256 >*

UserPreferences.ini settings [COLOR="Red"]I HAVE NO USERPREFERENCES.INI FILE[/COLOR]


Yes, those keys don't exist, you need to create them. Go in regidit to the right spot, right click and select "new key". Create the Direct3D key there and then create those values under it.

The UserPreferences.ini file should be created in your home directory the first time you run the game. So ~/The Lord of the Rings. (e.g. /home/mckinneycm/The Lord of the Rings/ etc. whatever).

### Post by SNy on 2008-05-13
> **mckinneycm said:**
> rm: cannot remove `.launcher/GLSDataCenter.config.LOTRO': Permission denied

You apparently started the script as root at least once and now try to start it as normal user. Since it creates a directory for storing all the files from the official servers, it needs to have write access to it.

Just delete the .launcher directory as root like so:
```
sudo rm -rf "~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online/.launcher"
```

Then start the script again as normal user, it will recreate the directory with you as owner and subsequently will be able to read and write from/to there.

HTH,
SNy

---

Just delete the .launcher directory as root like so:
```
sudo rm -rf "~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online/.launcher"
```

Then start the script again as normal user, it will recreate the directory with you as owner and subsequently will be able to read and write from/to there.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-13
Based on the "WineD3D" part of the error, I'll take a wild guess that it's a 3D issue. I'm sure Ajackson and/or SNy can say more, but in the mean time, can you post some more information that'll be needed anyway:

- what version of Ubuntu are you using?
- what version of wine are you using?
- what is your display card?
- what version of the display driver are you using / how was it installed? Or have you installed the restricted drivers at all?

If you don't know the card / driver versions, just type "lspci" and "glxinfo" in the terminal, look through the output and post the relevant parts. Actually, "lspci |grep VGA" and "glxinfo |grep OpenGL" output everything needed at least for me, not sure if all cards will output stuff with the same formatting. Anyway, try to find the name of your video card and its driver version from that info and post those. You need working 3D acceleration to play the game.

---

### Post by evilviper77 on 2008-05-14
> **ajackson said:**
> That's going to need a change to the script or my launcher, I can see where to get the correct information from regarding the queue, it's just working out how to test it.

Basically in WorldQueue.config you have a QueueNumber (ie your position in the queue) and a NowServingNumber. If the serving number is not your number then you have to wait. But to find out when it is time you need to check the server.config (ie keep getting the server details) that has a nowservingqueuenumber, when that matches (or is greater than?) your number the client can start.

So the process is fairly simple to implement but testing could be a problem.

So, does the GUI launcher not have this problem? I've tried to install it before but I always run into a problem getting gtkhtml2-sharp (use archlinux) so I decided to just use the script, but if the GUI doesn't have this problem I'll try to get the GUI working.

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-14
> **mckinneycm said:**
> What would be a working card for a decent price?

Well, that depends on what you consider a decent price :). Seriously, to me 200 - 300 € is a fair price for a good card I know I'll use for several years but I know many people who refuse to pay that much.

Most likely any pretty recent Nvidia card will do fine. I have two machines with Nvidia cards and they both are very playable. One is a quad core with 8800GT (works great, everything on max except shadows that cause problems for me and no hi-res texture since they cause crashed for some reason) and the other one is an older AMD 2400XP or something like that with an AGP Nvidia 7600GT. That works OK too, although with a lot of stuff turned down. I also had a PCI-express Nvidia 7950GT in the quad core for a while, and it worked great too. This is all with Ubuntu. My laptop has an integrated ATI card (mobility radeon X700) and I haven't been able to get that to work, so as long as you pick Nvidia, you're probably fine. Ask for opinions here once you find something that fits your budget, perhaps somebody else is already using a similar setup and can tell you what to expect.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-14
> **mckinneycm said:**
> I found a 8600GT 512MB for $82 USD.  Will that work for me?

That'll probably work totally fine but don't be fooled too much by the big "8600" number, there's similar/better performing cards in the 7000 series. The difference between the 8600 and 8800 is big. Anyway, probably a fine card for Lotro, but my point is that if there's similarly priced 7000 series cards, you might wanna investigate which one is actually faster before buying.

Here is a site that I've often turned to when comparing cards:

[http://www.tomshardware.com/charts/graphics-cards/3dmark06-v1-0-2-hdr-sm3-0-score,538.html?modelx=33&model1=710&model2=720&chart=275](http://www.tomshardware.com/charts/graphics-cards/3dmark06-v1-0-2-hdr-sm3-0-score,538.html?modelx=33&model1=710&model2=720&chart=275)

It's a chart of a lot of cards and their benchmarks under whatever benchmarking program they used this time. Of course it's not Lotro-specific, but I'm sure you can get a pretty good idea how card A ranks compared to card B etc. Note that "SLI" means two cards being used at once, so don't be fooled by those.

How come now it doesn't work? Or I guess a better question would be is there a way to update High res textures?

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-16
> **evilviper77 said:**
> Thanks you two, that did the trick, although I must ask; I used to be able to run it with High Res on (I downloaded the book 11 downloader, downloaded it, then updated it, since then i've had it update a decent amount once more upon which I came upon these problems) and could run it fine. How come now it doesn't work? Or I guess a better question would be is there a way to update High res textures?
You need to give the script a tweak, find the lines
```
wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --filesonly --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game
    wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --filesonly --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game
    wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --dataonly  --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game

```
At the end stick --highres at the end of each of them (probably only needed for the dataonly line but better safe than sorry).

That should patch the hires files for you, it was left out because prior to book 13 it didn't work right.

---

### Post by evilviper77 on 2008-05-17
> **ajackson said:**
> You need to give the script a tweak, find the lines
```
wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --filesonly --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game
    wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --filesonly --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game
    wine rundll32.exe PatchClient,Patch $patchServer_ADR --dataonly  --language ${languages[$selectedLanguage]} --productcode $game

```
At the end stick --highres at the end of each of them (probably only needed for the dataonly line but better safe than sorry).

That should patch the hires files for you, it was left out because prior to book 13 it didn't work right.

Yup, that fixed it. Thank you once again!

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-17
> **nytefall said:**
> For some odd reason, every time I use the Lotro Launcher for Linux it doesn't get past the character selection screen before causing my IP address to change somehow, disconnecting me from the internet.

It only happens when I do this so I do believe something in the actual Turbine client is causing this.
If you are hitting the character select screen then both launchers have done their job, so it's not down to either of them.

If it only happens when playing LOTRO my guess is either a problem with LOTRO itself or a problem with wine (do you use any other app/game via wine that uses the internet?).

The alternative is a network setup problem but then you would see similar drops when just doing other things like surfing the net, etc.

You could try posting a question in the official LOTRO forums (don't mention Linux though) see if there is a known issue with satellite connections (I wouldn't have thought so though) and/or ask in the network forum here (I think there is a network forum at ubuntuforums) to see again if there are any known issues.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-19
**LotROLinux 0.9.1.1**
Fairly major changes, though if you are a wine user you won't notice them.

Implemented something to hopefully deal with the problem of when you are in the queue, it's not been tested so it will either work or not.

Removed the old conversion routine from when the config was stored in the game directory. Your current config does get a mini conversion (to separate wine settings per game) but you won't see any difference apart from being able to have LOTRO, DDO and the test versions in different WINEPREFIX/bottles.

Major improvement on the Crossover support, made selecting bottle and game directory a bit easier when using cx.

Support for Macs has been added, this involved losing the GTKHtml browser for a less fancy one to get around Mac config problems, the gecko browser is still there though.

DEBs are being built (slowly) but the tarball is available for download.

**LotROLinux web site**
It looks ugly but I've added pages that allow you to enter your configs and userprefs, see the list and view individual configurations. Hopefully when it gets a few more entries it will help out those who are having problems since they can find a working userprefs.

Please only post working userprefs.

---

OpenGL renderer string: Mesa DRI Intel(R) 965GM 4.1.3002 x86/MMX/SSE2
OpenGL version string: 1.4 Mesa 7.0.3-rc2
OpenGL extensions:

lspci |grep VGA: 
00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation Mobile GM965/GL960 Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 03)

I installed lotro in wine, after that i copied the complete lotro map from a windows machine. I also installes Direct X in wine box just to trie it out.

hope I gave enough information now

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-21
> **ardawen said:**
> 
I installed lotro in wine, after that i copied the complete lotro map from a windows machine. I also installes Direct X in wine box just to trie it out.

hope I gave enough information now

You're using an Intel graphics chip, and I have no idea how well those work or if they need some extra 3D configuration or not. But for installing the game, yes, you seem to be missing the reg entries. The instructions are on ajackson's page, here are the links:

[http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php)

You probably need to follow the instructions starting from "winecfg settings". To lauch winecfg, type "winecfg" in the terminal. Change version to win2000 and close it. To launch regedit, type "regedit". You will have to create new keys so, to do this:

HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/Direct3D/VideoMemorySize 

You go to HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Wine/, and then right-click and create the key "Direct3D". Then below that, you create VideoMemorySize and set it to whatever your card has. (128, 256, whatever it is)

After that's done, add the repo for the lotrolinux GUI launcher:

[http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php)

The easiest way to add the repository is to start up Synaptic, add the repository from that page, reload listings, look for lotro in the list of installable programs and install it. Then try starting the lotrolinux launcher and running the game again, and let us know if you get further.

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-25
This is just something I thought people might find amusing...about the benefits of playing Lotro on Linux instead of Windows:

I play with my wife, we have two accounts for the game...and occasionally we have items to trade to each other etc. that would normally of course call for using two computers. On Windows XP I've actually had problems even just tabbing out to a browser so I'm pretty sure this wouldn't work there: here's a picture of me running two Lotro's on the same computer, trading stuff from one account's character to another's. This is with Compiz, I just pressed Win+e and took a screenshot. The amazing thing is that the game is actually quite playable like this as well, I've sometimes had to run long distances while one character waits in another workspace. Pretty cool, eh? :)

[IMG]http://pics.livejournal.com/ladycoreopsis/pic/0019kg19[/IMG]

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-25

I was at work and my wife was home and happened to need some ores or whatever from my character - and she didn't feel like starting up the laptop to do a trade and asked me over a chat if she could launch another game on this machine to trade stuff. No language files found."

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-27
> **psykoroman said:**
> I've tried installing using the GUI launcher twice now, and have both times received the error message in the launcher that says: 

"Initialising, please wait...
No language files found." 

Is this a problem with my installation of the launcher, my installation of the game, or something else? 
Just a configuration problem. In the Tools -> Options check that the Game Directory is pointing to the directory where you installed the game, the launcher uses that directory as a base when it is checking for language files and the games configuration file.

---

### Post by psykoroman on 2008-05-27

> Just a configuration problem. In the Tools -> Options check that the Game Directory is pointing to the directory where you installed the game, the launcher uses that directory as a base when it is checking for language files and the games configuration file.

Thank you very much. Easy fix, great launcher, as I'm sure you've heard before. Haven't played this game in months, and now I can again. Gracias.

Best thing to do is to switch to the simple patch windows (under tools -> options) that shows you what the process spews out. The complex window used to work well but something changed in Turbines patching module and it is now very hit and miss (usually miss).

> 
Edit: I'm also downloading the Fileplanet .exe patches for 11-->12 and 12-->13. Are there any tricks to getting them to work, or is just a execute and follow the instructions sort of thing?
Download them, run them one after the other, fire up LotROLinux, hit patch go watch TV for a bit. If you are certain it has hung you can kill it and restart (might have to manually delete ~/.LotROLinux/patch.log if it complains about it being locked).

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-05-28
> **psykoroman said:**
> 
I know that my video card can run the game (I was running it fine in Vista for months before I made the switch), so I know that the card is capable of doing it. I'm guessing, then, that it's a driver issue. But I don't know how to resolve it. Any help?

Did you set the wine options in regedit? If this is a driver / wine settings issue, you can probably solve it by grabbing a cup of coffee and reading the past few months of post on this list for a while. Wine settings and driver issues have come up a lot, so I suggest you take a look at the previous posts first, most likely it has already been answered. Let us know if you don't know what to do but please do take a look at the previous posts first, there's a lot of information there.

There's a post about how to find your video card and driver information a few pages back on this thread - post that info along with information on everything you've done so far, if you can't get it to work.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-28
> **jbradshw said:**
> When I launch the game I get this error:

Game Error [128]
The game encountered a fatal DirectX issue while attempting to start. Try a different screen resolution or bit depth. If that doesn't work, try new video drivers. [128]
What version of wine are you using?

See if a Lord of the Rings folder has been created (it will be in either ~/ or ~/Documents), if it is there look for a file called UserPreferences.ini, if that exists edit it and find AllowFakeFullScreen, set it to False. That should solve that problem but the issue that causes it was fixed in wine a while back (unless it is because you have a Mesa card).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-05-31
> **psykoroman said:**
> And what's the issue with Mesa cards? I think I have one (at least, the EVE Online configuration reads it as one - Mesa DRI Intel(R) 945GM 20061017 x86/MMX/SSE2), so it sounds as if that's the problem. Is there a fix?
It's down to graphics drivers, LOTRO uses the high end of a graphics cards capabilities, unfortunately that means that the drivers for those cards have to correctly expose those functions.
NVidia drivers do, ATI drivers are getting there (slowly) and Mesa drivers I must admit I don't know but it sounds like they are lagging behind.

The only real fix is to make sure when you spend your hard earned dosh on hardware that you buy brands who support linux, on a graphics front at the moment that is only NVidia with ATI getting an honourable mention.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-06-02
> **ninjasonfire said:**
> 1) Error 205: invalid command line parameters (this is with the gui launcher)
2) Connection to the server has been lost (quit button) (this is with the cli launcher)

So, I've tried getting my install as up to date as possible, and I can't even patch with the gui, as it gives me the first error even when I try to update it. Is there anything I can do, or do I just need to give up on wine for this game?
Well the lotroclient.exe gives out the 205 error and that isn't called by the GUI when patching so how are you patching using the GUI? Patching via the GUI is done by selecting Tools -> Patch then clicking Apply (change to the simple patch window under options as the complex one no longer works properly).

With the CLI it doesn't update the hi-res graphics at the moment, I posted a small modification for it a couple of weeks ago so trawl backwards through this thread and you should find it.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-02
Wow, Success with ATI! I tried my laptop again with this latest ATI driver:

[http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html](http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html)

and now the game actually works after disabling Post-processing effects. PP will turn everything black so that has to be off.

This is with X700 mobility Radeon 128MB (Asus A6 laptop). The performance is quite bad, on the native resolution of 1280x800 with pretty much everything turned down, it seems to be playable outside of cities (hunted a few wargs in the North Downs, no problem) but even a quiet city like Esteldin will make everything lag bad enough that the reboot into XP sounds good after a while. For comparison, the game runs very smoothly on the native resolution with higher graphics settings (and post-processing) under XP so for me, it'll still be dual boot unless I can figure out some nice settings to speed it up. Even the intro movie (the spinning logo that comes up when loading after the first load page) lags. That's how slow it is, my guess is that this is just a matter of the ATI drivers being pretty immature still (clearly getting there now, though!). Anyway, this is a pretty low-end ATI mobile graphics card, the laptop is about 3 and a half years old. I'd suggest owners of newer and better cards load up these new drivers and see how it works for them.

A note about installing the drivers: in addition to installing the driver after unchecking the restricted driver I had to add "fgrlx" to the restricted driver's blacklist to make it stop trying to load the restricted one. I did this by adding this line:

```
DISABLED_MODULES="fglrx"
```

to this file:

/etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common

I hadn't done this before, so my earlier note about it not working was actually incorrect, I wasn't really using it then, apparently. This little change will make sure the restricted driver's "fglrx" module is not loaded.

Edit: Config posted to Ajackson's site. Also, I added this to xorg.conf's Device section:

```
Option		"VideoOverlay" "on"
```

It was missing for some reason, I suggest everybody take a look at xorg.conf after installing the driver, ATI's installer doesn't really seem to do anything good to it. Seems faster now. (That option is actually required for xv video playback - this is also a myth frontend - but it seemed to make the game considerably faster too.) I'm not very familiar with xorg.conf's ATI options, but I'll keep looking if I can find more options.

### Post by HegonBadde on 2008-06-02
Ok got an odd problem.

I followed the directions at [http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php]("http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php")
with a couple of minor changes.


I skipped the part about FakeNet1.1
I copied the entire installed game from a windows box.(and the User folder)

I used the Prebuilt with gecko laucher from [http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php]("http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php")

And then I followed the User Preferences and Wine config and wine Registry edits

For the most part it works great but I've noticed one problem.
I can play for hours and not have any lag, but if some one else on the network starts playing from a windows box, Both of us start lagging, (a halt followed by a skip, ok for a bit then halts, and then skips to catch up again, etc repeat). 

3 windows boxes at the same time have no issues, 
1 linux alone has no issues, 
windows + linux at the same time, and we both get (Halt skip) lag.
2windows + linux at the same time, and we all 3 get (Halt skip) lag.

If I shutdown the Windows or wine client, it takes about 2 minutes for the lag problem to disappear completely for the others.

I figure it's got to have something to do with the networking.
My initial guess was that the Windows boxes were passing plug and play rules to the router, and that wine + Ubuntu 8.04 wasn't quite passing the same.

I tried manually setting up rules for port forwarding to the linux box, and nothing changed. Windows boxes were still fine until I started wine + LOTRO, and wine + LOTRO was fine until one of the windows boxes Started LOTRO.

Any advice on where to start looking to track this issue down?

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-03
> **HegonBadde said:**
> I figure it's got to have something to do with the networking.
My initial guess was that the Windows boxes were passing plug and play rules to the router, and that wine + Ubuntu 8.04 wasn't quite passing the same.

I tried manually setting up rules for port forwarding to the linux box, and nothing changed. Windows boxes were still fine until I started wine + LOTRO, and wine + LOTRO was fine until one of the windows boxes Started LOTRO.

Any advice on where to start looking to track this issue down?

I play regularly with Windows+Linux or Linux+Linux and the lag is not that bad either way, I haven't noticed a difference, really...yes, we have some, but it's very playable and the lag is not constant. I've always thought it's normal network lag (and sometimes I'm downloading something as well, so that might do it).

Anyway, before you start tweaking your iptables etc: do you have another router you could try? I've noticed these common ADSL or cable modem / router combinations are not often high quality so if you're using something like that, I might start troubleshooting by seeing if another router makes a difference. One of my old cable modems actually rebooted when the load was too high...quite funny, really: two machines bittorrenting, boom - router reboot. So if it's a low-quality router, see if you can loan another one for testing. Also, Lotro itself has some network speed setting in the menus. No idea what it does but maybe worth a try to try to limit traffic? Clearly there's some differences between how Linux and Windows deal with the traffic if two Windowses work fine, but the router might make a difference (wouldn't run out to buy a new one, though, this is just a hunch).

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-09
> **ziete said:**
> Something more if it can help you, when I try to start with the command mono ./LotROLinux.run it tells me that: "Unhandled Exception: System.DllNotFoundException: glibsharpglue-2
...
Is that giving you any other informations guys?

I think somebody with more technical knowledge of mono etc. should comment on that (if that's a mono problem), but it seems to me like you're trying to run it the wrong way now...why are you launching it with "mono lotrolinux.run"? Just run "LotroLinux". The repo should install the program and it should lauch from anywhere by doing that. Open a terminal and type "LotRoLinux" (not at my computer now, hope that's the correct capitalization). Ajackson's repo should install it so that you jsut type that or select the menu entry.

Also, installing .net framework under wine is not required - and I frankly didn't even think it was possible. You have to use the linux launcher, don't bother clicking the windows files, they won't launch. Use the launcher or script. The whole point of the launcher is that the real launcher doesn't work because of .Net framework.

In addition to ~/The Lord of the Rings, there's the ~/.lotrolinux directory too or something like that, the one with the launcher setup files. Have you tried deleting that and reinstalling the launcher? And sorry about repeating this, but are you ABSOLUTELY sure you have the reg entry right? No typos? Key in the right spot? Right amount of memory? And have you done some wine or lotro configuration/copying as root? Could this be a permissions issue? What are the permissions of ~.wine? and the "The Lord of the Rings" directory underneath it? Are you using the latest wine?

If you can't get it to work otherwise, I'd suggest a BIG cup of coffee and redoing it all. Backup the ~/.wine/drive_c/..../The Lord of the Rings, so you don't have to patch again, and completely remove wine + lotrolauncher (including ~/.wine and ~/.lotrolauncher etc. whatever) and reinstall them and the game. For patching, just copy the backup dir. back there. Use ajackson's repo for launcher and winehq repo for latest wine (rc4 I think). Follow the installation instructions, there's the fakeNET.reg part about getting the installer to think you have .net framework. That's enough for .net stuff. Do all the settings and try again. Don't install any extra stuff and don't run / install anything as root.

I've installed this several times with Nvidia cards (7600 and 8800 series) with no problems ever so I would imagine it should work for you as well...good luck!

Edit: Oh, and since you said you're running Wow as well - is there some special Wine settings that World of Warcraft requires? If so, post them, maybe they could be interfering with something.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-06-09
Both complex and simple windows work (as in the game patches) they just don't update the progress very well.

Disconnect from server is almost always data files out of date, start the patcher and when (if) it finishes you should be up to date.

**LotROLinux 0.9.3**
The improvements to the simple patch window are in place, there is no longer a complex patch window as I can't work out how to get that updating using my new method.

It might hang after doing the data files patch on a big update, I can't work out why (well I know why I don't know how to fix it) but it should show progress a lot better than it used to.

Source tarball up, DEBs on the way. Standalone version (for Macs) also up on SourceForge.

Set texture filtering to trilinear and see if that fixes blockiness (restart game between changing this). Start with lower setting and work you way up to ones that work well for you.

### Post by SNy on 2008-06-13
Epohax:
To investigate this problem, you should start with the X.org log file. Check there if the fglrx driver is properly configured and reports direct rendering as active (in the log).

If it does, the next step is to check why the programs you tried are unable to use it. This is most likely a problem with libraries. If you previously used an NVidia card, there might be leftovers preventing apps to function with the new driver correctly.

You can tell the GL library to inform you about problems like so:
```
LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose glxinfo
```

For a more detailed description you might also want to take a look at [http://wiki.linuxquestions.org/wiki/OpenGL](http://wiki.linuxquestions.org/wiki/OpenGL).

HTH,
SNy

I don't know if envyng includes it now, it didn't then. The latest (released 20th of May or so) driver is the only one I have gotten to work.

In any case, make sure it's not the restricted fgrlx module loading (see what i wrote about blacklisting). I'm out of town now and for the next few weeks with no access to that ATI laptop so can't really say much more about that at the moment - it was too slow to play, anyway, I was happy to see it work but haven't really played the game with it more than a few nights to test settings. But I'd start with the blacklisting and then reinstall driver from ATI website.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-06-14
> **sofarsogood said:**
> Sorry if this post is in the 'wrong' place as it concerns LOTRO on a Mac and not on Ubuntu....but this forum seems to be the most active/helpful.

I've been trying to get LOTRO working on my Mac for a few days.

tried wine, added mono, added CXG, tried Alan Jackson's GUI, tried CLI, using fakenet....the book 11 pack installs under wine and CXG.

When it comes to running, mono gets the furthest. Installed...runs the launcher and authenticates with password and then....this...

omar-rahmans-imac:The Lord of the Rings Online rahmans$ mono LotROLinux.run
omar-rahmans-imac:The Lord of the Rings Online rahmans$ Argument 'LOTROEU' unknown

My installation is so messy now I'm not sure what is where.....anyone know where I should add a definition for 'LOTROEU'?
I'll do my best to help but I don't have a Mac so some of the names/advice might be a bit wrong.

On a Mac it seems that only CXG works well (something to do with it having it's own implementation of X11 as the one from Apple is a bit poor).

Easiest way is to create a bottle based on windows 2000 using CXG. Then download that FakeNET.reg file and use the "run windows command" to run regedit followed by the path to that reg file. That should allow you to install LOTRO. If the version of LOTRO that you are installing isn't at least book 11 (if it is download version it will be at least book 11, if it is the retail version maybe not), if you aren't too sure get the patchclient.dll from the site in my signature.

You then need to install Mono for Mac OSX, not for Windows. You already have a copy of LotROLinux.run so no need to download that again.

This is the bit where I go purely on what one of my fellow advocates at CXG has told me, using CXG you can open a shell for a bottle, I think it is an option on the "run windows command" option but I'm not certain.

That will open two terminals, one fairly empty the other with various things in it (gibberish is the best description I've heard so far). In the gibberish window type mono and then you can apparantly drag and drop LotROLinux.run into it.

You will have to set the option (CXG, bottle name, game directory) and then you should be OK to patch the client up. The patcher is a lot more responsive now but you may find it hangs at the end. If you are certain patching has finished and LotROLinux has hung just kill it and restart it (a quick click on patch should confirm that it has nothing left to do).

Select server, enter username and password and it should launch fine. Any problems post them here or over on the CXG forums for LOTRO as that has a Mac advocate who might give more accurate help.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-06-16
An interesting suggestion for ATI users.

I've been following [bug 12929]("http://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=12929") about fglrx and wined3d not getting on.

One of the devs posted:

> Just a guess, but try disabling GL_ATI_separate_stencil and
GL_ATI_envmap_bumpmap by commenting them out at line 57 and 60 of
dlls/wined3d/directx.c
Someone tried just the GL_ATI_envmap_bumpmap and that worked, might be something worth try to see if it helps with the LOTRO situation.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-22
> **Epohax said:**
> 
BatPenguin: You suggest putting fglrx in the module blacklist, but then again, fglrx is being used in my xorg.conf? I don't understand why that is.

If there is anymore info I need to post, please let me know!

Yes, it's a bit confusing: both the ATI driver and the restricted driver are called fglrx. When you blacklist the restricted one like I mentioned, it actually makes it _possible_ for the ATI module to be loaded by preventing the loading of Ubuntu's "restricted ATI driver" module. Otherwise the restricted module gets loaded no matter what. I know this sounds strange but that's just the way it is. So just try the same blacklisting that I mentioned (sorry, still not back at that computer, gotta keep quoting myself) after installing the ATI driver. Xorg.conf needs to have fgrlx just like you have. Try it and reboot. If you have problems, just open a terminal, remove blacklisting and reboot. This is the only way I've gotten the ATI driver from the web site working.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-23
> **Epohax said:**
> Thanks ajackson and BatPenguin, these are my files:

First of all, why do I have this section twice? Maybe by some automatic configuration script? 

Yes, probably. Just remove one of them. And, yes, glx probably needs to load - so remove comment. The blacklisting seems fine, pretty sure I have it exactly like that. I'll be back home on Sunday so I can post my xorg.conf then if you don't have any luck until then, so sorry, my responses are only as good as my memory now :). But now it sounds like you're doing it pretty much like I was when I got it running. Reboot and try again with the glx module loaded.

Also: now that the restricted module is disabled and new ATI loaded (I suppose?), look at the fgrlxinfo (or something like that) command plus ATI control panel output and make sure they're all good, right versions and all. I think fgrlxgears command will show silly spinning gears, just double-check everything is fine. The install of ATI driver went fine and you got no errors when it compiled the module, right?

There's got to be a "redo xorg.conf" command for the ATI driver as well, so you might just want to read through the driver README, backup your xorg.conf and let ATI's command recreate one for you.
