I think by *"There's got to be a "redo xorg.conf" command for the ATI driver as well"*, you mean the "aticonfig --initial -f" command, which rewrites your xorg.conf. 

When the command is run without parameters, these are excerpts of the help file:

> 
ATI Initial Configuration:
  --initial
        Generate a default ATI device section in the configuration file which
        is capable of loading the fglrx driver.


> Examples:
  1. Setting up fglrx for the first time.
       Single head :    aticonfig --initial --input=/etc/X11/xorg.conf
       Dual head   :    aticonfig --initial=dual-head --screen-layout=above
                        This command will generate a dual head configuration
                        file with the second screen located above the first
                        screen.
  2. Setting up big desktop to horizontal and set overlay on secondary display.
                        aticonfig --dtop=horizontal --overlay-on=1
  3. Setting up modes for primary display.
                        aticonfig --resolution=0,1600x1200,1280x1024,1024x768

### Post by SNy on 2008-06-23
Epohax, as I said earlier, you need to check two different things.

First, make sure the kernel and X themselves are loading the drivers properly and report hardware rendering in the logs.

Then, if you are certain the driver has been loaded correctly (ie. the X log says direct rendering is enabled) and it still doesn't work, check that the GL libraries are correctly setup.

The debian packages for graphics drivers are (or at least have been) using diversions for the libGL.so and other libraries and that has caused problems if not properly un- and reinstalled.

Also, if you are using a 64bit system, you need to check that the 32bit GL/glx libraries also work correctly or you will no be able to play any 32bit games (including, but not limited to anything running under wine).

HTH,
SNy

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-06-23
Epohax, have you looked at this:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI)

About mid-way through the page, instructions for manual install from ATI web site.

Also, since mesa driver being used is a sign of driver installation not working according to that page, you should probably try looking for and, if needed, asking for more installation help from ATI-specific forum threads, if you can't get it working with those instructions. I use 32-bit, so I don't know about how to check the 64-bit stuff SNy mentioned, for example, so I'm sure you can find better help from a threaad devoted to ATI installation problems. I would imagine there's a few threads about that here  :)

What did you set VideoMemorySize to? If it contains any non numeric characters it probably defaults to 0. Wine does a fairly good job of recognising graphics cards now so the need to set VideoMemorySize isn't as big as it used to be.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-03
At the top of the lotrolauncher script is the following line
```
#wgetNoCheckCertOption="--no-check-certificate"
```
Try removing the leading # see if it is a certificate problem being reported in a strange way.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-05
> **pvella said:**
> I still get that error.  I don't seem to be able to get it to work very often.  Most of the time I still get that error.  I read somewhere in the forum someone recommending using an older version of the kernel.  Is that worth trying ?

Is there anything I can do to make it work more consistently ?
The lag problem is something server side but linux users seem to suffer more than windows users (there is an open bug about it).

As for the access violations they tend to be memory related, double check things like the VideoMemorySize option in regedit (make sure the case of that and Direct3D are correct, wine should recognise your card but it doesn't hurt to give it a hint).

It could be that for your card & drivers there are certain settings that upset LOTRO and cause the crashes, unfortunately finding them is trial and error, there are a few known working configurations on my website (see sig for link) but your card might not be one of the ones with a submitted config.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-07-06
Regarding these crashes, in case this might help troubleshooting: for as long as I've played under Linux (this is with Nvidia 8800GT) I haven't been able to keep high-res textures on. I have to set them to "high" instead of "very high" or the game will eventually crash (or actually it hangs, not crashes, the sounds go on but the picture is either black or freezed). Sometimes it takes half an hour before this, sometimes a much shorter time, but eventually it will crash for me under very high textures. The "High" textures or medium etc. settings don't crash at all.

So anyway, in case these crashes you are experiencing are coming when high-res textures are in use, I suggest you try lowering the texture setting and keeping everything else high (well, not shadows, some of those cause graphics glitches at least for me).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-06
> **CSWScrooge said:**
> I had this error too for some time. I was able to track it down to my power supply, which is a bit old. Took me quite some time though.
Not really current graphics cards pull more juice when they have to work harder (energy conservation and all that), if your PSU is near it's limit that extra bit of juice can cause random malfunctions.

As for crashes when there is a sudden change in what is to be rendered the problem does tend to get reported but as a fix-me rather than an error and the default for both launchers is to suppress the fix-me's.

### Post by pvella on 2008-07-06
I will keep trying.  I really don't want to use windoze any more.  I am happy to even sacrifice the ultra-high setting that I can run in.  But I have to keep going back to windoze because I am annoying people by crashing too often.  I guess I just need to find a setup that works for this machine.  I will keep trying lots of different things until it works.  I wish Turbine would officially support Linux, we need to put more pressure on them to do this.

From winehq I found the following fix for a similar problem with WoW;

```
Open a terminal window, (konsole/terminal/x terminal etc..), type regedit and press enter. This will start the Wine equivalent of the windows registry editor. If you are familiar with using the registry editor under windows then this is pretty much the same.

   1. Find HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\
   2. Highlight the wine folder in the left hand pane by left clicking on it. The icon should change to an open folder.
   3. Click right on the wine folder and select [NEW] then [KEY].
   4. Replace the text "New Key #1" with OpenGL (CaSe Sensitive).
   5. Right click in the right hand pane and select [NEW] then [String Value].
   6. Replace "New Value #1" with "DisabledExtensions" (CaSe sensitive).
   7. Then double click anywhere on the line, a dialog box will open.
   8. In the value field type "GL_ARB_vertex_buffer_object" (without the quotes).

```

Tried it, but it made no difference.  Also seems that people are having more problems with 0.9.59, but 0.9.60 is currently available for download.  If I feel adventurous I might install that today...

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-07-07
> **pvella said:**
> 
Tried it, but it made no difference.  Also seems that people are having more problems with 0.9.59, but 0.9.60 is currently available for download.  If I feel adventurous I might install that today...

How are you installing Wine? That's not the latest version available, it's actually up to 1.0-rc-something now. Or, umm, actually I take that back: seems to be a real "version 1.0" now. I didn't even know it's up to a proper 1.0 yet, actually, since I haven't been playing for a while - at this latitude, you gotta enjoy the summer the brief time it's warm outside, hehe :). But anyway, the WineHQ site has instructions for adding the repo:

[http://www.winehq.org/site/download-deb](http://www.winehq.org/site/download-deb)

I suggest you give the latest version a try. 

Also: since it's a laptop, do you have some way of monitoring its temperatures? Could this be a temp issue? Is the laptop's fan running as loud as it does under Windows? I've had problems with my laptop (Asus) running really, really hot, so I'm not so sure that Linux does a good job of watching temperature of all laptops when running graphics-intensive stuff. So if this sounds like it could be an issue and it crashes because of overheating, you could test this by pointing a table fan at the laptop and trying playing then. I've done that while monitoring temperatures and it actually helps a lot, just position the fan correctly and you should see temperatures drop. Maybe that'll make it more stable, worth a try at least.

### Post by thom_raindog on 2008-07-08
> **Faud said:**

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-09
> **thom_raindog said:**
> I have wine debug fixme-all in the options dialog set.. is that what you talk about? In this case, I had it like that ever since I started playing using the gui launcher..
Yeah, when we first got LOTRO working the amount of fixme's it spewed out was having a detrimental effect on game speed so we suppressed them, how much gets spewed out now I don't know but the crashes due to a sudden surge in rendering used to trigger a mass of fixme's but no errors (this was around a year ago though).

> My wife used my PC a few times to play WoW and it "stuttered, i.e. kind of froze once every few seconds then moved on.. might that be AT ALL related
The LOTRO stutter is caused by the client sending huge amounts of duplicate UDP packages in less than a second (someone clocked close to 4000, each takes time to process). The WoW stutter has a bug report somewhere as a few people have noticed it though I think that one tends to hit wireless users more than wired.

As for benchmarking, I don't really know if there are decent applications around to gauge system performance, I know 3DMark (I think that's it name) should work under wine.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-17
Well it looks like the problem with the terrible lag spikes has been found (see [here]("http://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=12302")). Interestingly enough it is lotroclient being silly in it's processing **but** getting away with it on Windows because of an undocumented feature. Luckily it can be fixed in wine (if they can prove that Windows is doing what they think it is) but another fine example of sloppy programming from the Turbine guys (no offence but if a function says it didn't fail, why then try to get the error?)

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-07-17
I just downloaded the dll file from the thread and copied it to the Lotro directory under wine, and then used regedit to add this key for it:

```

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\AppDefaults\lotroclient.exe\DllOverrides] "ws2_32"="native"
```

It seemed to me from the thread that that's all that was needed. I hope I did it right.

I gave the game a couple very quick tries in the past hour or two (just a quick run through Bree and a run out of Bree, going in and out of buildings etc.), and I actually didn't see any of the rubberband type lag during this quick test, so that's promising. Normally I get some lag always running there. Anyway, this really proves nothing, very quick little tests, no hard benchmarks or anything...just a gut (or placebo =) feeling that "seems better".

The problem was never that bad for me anyway so somebody who used to have bad lag should give it a try.

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-07-18
> **thom_raindog said:**
> I get a strange thing happening here:
Sometime during my playing, sometimes withing 3 minutes, sometimes after an hour or more, the sound dies. The game just goes all quiet.
No errors show up in run.log.. any ideas?

Do you have other programs running? For me, that happens if I have Skype on and somebody I know logs in. Skype just grabs the sound for itself and I have to restart LotRO to get sound back.

So I'd just check if you have something else that uses the sound runnin. It might be grabbing the sound every once in a while...don't know if system sounds could be doing the same (I don't have them on). I generally stay clear of sound settings, I've managed to mess up my sounds more than once trying to get Dolby surround output work and such...let's just say that ALSA and I are not best buddies.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-07-18
> **GeeBee58 said:**
> Thanks for the reply Bat Penguin, really appreciate your time. I have an ATI HD2600XT card and the game is copied from my XP install and is fully patched. Iv'e also run the patch from the GUI launcher. The video driver is the one that popped up when I first ran Ubuntu that said there is a usupported or not confirmedcor something driver from ATI and asked me if I wanted to use it. I have dowloaded athe latest driver( .tgz I think) but dont know how to install that. I am very new to linux but have 25 or so years experience with PC's useing from dos 3 to Vista so can usually work it out eventually-lol

Thanks again

OK - the ATI card/driver is then probably the problem. It won't work with the default driver. I made a post some time ago (a month or two ago, something like that) here about how I got my ATI laptop to work (well, very slowly, but the game runs). You have to install the latest ATI driver from their web site, which I guess you have downloaded done now.

So I suggest you start by reading my post and everything we've talked about the ATI driver since that, this should give you an idea of what to do/expect. Then find a "installing the ATI driver from their website" thread here on the forums somewhere and install the latest driver. There's instructions somewhere, I remember seeing a how-to. Pretty much you just drop out of X and run the installation script but look up the how-to, it's good to do it like that. Backup current xorg.conf and any other files you edit too, just in case.

Pay attention to the part in my post about blacklisting the "restricted" fgrlx driver. This is in my post and should be in the how-to as well, but I'll just mention it again since it's pretty confusing. Read up on what we talked about it here on this thread and just do it, it works :).

Disable post-processing (didn't work for me) and start with a really basic setup if and when you get it working. Let us know how it goes.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-21
**LotROLinux 0.9.5**
A few bug fixes
- you can now have spaces in the bottle name (CrossOver)
- Username/password error should now be reported as such rather than a server error
- Warning about incorrect patchclient version works again under CrossOver
- Another tweak to the mini-HTML browser

Now that there is a dedicated version for the Mac which uses the native Mac interface the mini-HTML browser will be replaced with the old GtkHTML browser that used to be there (it only got removed because it didn't work right under OS X).

DEBs are being built, source and the Mac version already up on SourceForge.

---

### Post by thom_raindog on 2008-07-23
Book 14 is out as a standalone patch.
How would I use that? Simply calling wine with the patchfile results in it telling me that it can  not find a previously installed copy of the game (I copied an installation over from a friends windows machine..)

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-23
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Book 14 is out as a standalone patch.
How would I use that? Simply calling wine with the patchfile results in it telling me that it can  not find a previously installed copy of the game (I copied an installation over from a friends windows machine..)
If you have the EU version try the following (copied from the EU forums)

```
Make a reg file with:

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Curr entVersion\Uninstall\4f6dcc3b-179d-4b1b-80f0-b6083a0b3ce6_is1]
"Inno Setup: App Path"="D:\\Games\\Codemasters\\The Lord of the Rings Online"

Where D:\\Games\\Codemasters\\The Lord of the Rings Online is your current instalation folder.

You can do this on Notepad and save it as "whatever.REG", doubleclick it and it should work.
Or go to Windows>Run> tape regedit > search for that path and create the key.
```
Obviously use a text editor of your choice and just run regedit from a terminal, click registry -> import and select the file you created.

---

### Post by SNy on 2008-07-23
The patchers have always done a very poor job for me wrt. finding the installation directory.

What you definitely need to do is to have a bunch of registry keys. I don't rememeber the exact details and can't check right now, but IIRC it needed and "Uninstall" key. Perhaps someone here has posted or can post a .reg file containing the necessary keys.

Other than that it worked best for me to just symlink the real location to the default location (C:/The Lord of the Rings Online/ or something along those lines).
The installer, once satisfied with the registry keys, will tell you where it thinks the installation resides. Just create the appropriate symlink. Easier than windows where you cannot change it and need to manually copy it over in case it's not correct.

Menthol: you should be able to patch with the patch option normally, no worries. It's just probably a pretty large patch, a new book.

mad_golfer: there's good instructions for getting this running on ajackson's web page: 

[http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php) (GUI Launcher)

[http://bmx-chemnitz.de/~mfr/LOTRO/](http://bmx-chemnitz.de/~mfr/LOTRO/) (command line launcher)

If you've understood and followed all instructions and still can't get it to run, post here with information about your system (graphics card, driver version, wine version etc.) and details about what you've done, and somebody can probably help you. If you ask for help, please list all the steps you've done. If you get an error message, use this thread's search function and look it up before asking. Somebody has probably already had the same problem before :)

### Post by SNy on 2008-07-24
> **Menthol said:**
> sorry for the stupid question, but do I take it that the "patch" option in the Linux launcher wont install this latest book 14 patch then?

Oh, it will, but the book patches are quite big, hence Turbine/Codemasters publish them as archives a few days before they go live.
You can download this archive (I think Book 14 is around 580MB in size) and install it (also prior to the server updates). Upon patch day it will then need to get only a small portion from the servers and copy the rest from the pre-patch installation.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-25
> **Menthol said:**
> Hmm, I wasn't sure if it completed when I first installed it and patched up the game, I noticed since this latest patch there are now more sound effects for fishing, however I couldnt find any notes about this in the latest update, so I was wondering if they had always been there but werent downloaded before.
If you get into the game then your are up to date as it does a check on the data files, if the checksums are wrong then you get the connection to server was lost message. It's part of their anti-hacking protection to stop you altering game files, doubles up as a nice "am I up to date" checker.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-07-27
> **Calredon said:**
> Hi, i have a very "special" problem here: The pregame ad (the screen with new game informations ..) didn´t update - when the game starts i still see the thanksgiving ad from november! I use always the actual wine version, my system is ubuntu 8.04. My wifes system with ubuntu 7.10 has the same problem. All the other updates work as designed - just this ad-screen.... 

Has someone a similar problem, or ist this "normal" when the game is started with wine and linux?
I don't think it is normal but I have a rough idea about why it could be happening.

Linux is case sensitive while windows isn't, it could be that the name of the directory where the pre-game add lives is in the wrong case or you have two (one in right case the other not) and wine is picking the wrong one.

The directory to look in in raw under that I have en and en_gb (not sure what the US version has, presumably just en), under (in my case) en_gb I have logo and a file called lotro_ad_pregame_en_gb.jpg (again the US might have a different name). If you have multiple directories you should be able to see which image is in use and rename directories to something else to see about getting the correct image displayed.

But it is purely cosmetic and doesn't affect gameplay but I can understand wanting to see what is coming up.

### Post by SNy on 2008-07-28
I'd say you forgot to remove the DllOverride for Daniel Santos' workaround.

> 
[/home/daniel/proj/lotro/wine-1.1.0/dlls/ws2_32/socket.c:2690]


Start winecfg, remove the DllOverride and make sure you have the .dll for wine-1.1.2 in place.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-04
> **BrowncoatElite said:**
> Hey, new to Ubuntu and Linux - but I absolutely love it so far!  No more Windows!

Any-hoo, been trying to get Lotro to run - and I'm so close I can taste it!  I'm using the CLI script from [www.lotrolauncher.com](www.lotrolauncher.com) and it loads beautifully - that is, until I get passed the very first "Shadows of Angmar" screen with the witch king and all and then the screen goes black and it says "error, cannot patch".   Any hints? 

Thanks a ton!
My guess is that the hi-res graphics file is out of date, I'm not sure if the CLI patches it automatically at the moment. The easiest thing to do is edit that script, find the lines that contain rundll32 and append **--highres** to the end of them (it's only really needed on the datafiles line but it doesn't hurt to be on all of them).

---

### Post by BrowncoatElite on 2008-08-04
> **ajackson said:**
> My guess is that the hi-res graphics file is out of date, I'm not sure if the CLI patches it automatically at the moment. The easiest thing to do is edit that script, find the lines that contain rundll32 and append **--highres** to the end of them (it's only really needed on the datafiles line but it doesn't hurt to be on all of them).

That got rid of the "cannot patch" error, but now I've got a new one! (Yippee!)   It says "Connection Failed: You do not have the current version of the client installed."

I should add that I'm running this through Crossover Games on a 64-bit Ubuntu system.  Don't know if that messes it up...

Thanks so much!

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-04
> **BrowncoatElite said:**
> That got rid of the "cannot patch" error, but now I've got a new one! (Yippee!)   It says "Connection Failed: You do not have the current version of the client installed."
That normally means that one (or more) of the game files (the ones called client_*.dat) is out of date, run it through the patch again see if that makes it go.

> I should add that I'm running this through Crossover Games on a 64-bit Ubuntu system.  Don't know if that messes it up...
I use CXG so it's not likely to be that, though the script was designed with vanilla wine in mind. Make sure if you have both wine and cxg installed that the correct wine is being used by the script (the vanilla wine ends up on the path but cxg doesn't. CXG also runs the first file called lotroclient.exe unless you specify one directly so it might be running the one in the backup directory by mistake (try renaming/deleting that one).

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-05
> **BrowncoatElite said:**
> It indeed needed another update, so I let it and now we're back to the "error, cannot patch" madness.  I had to take the "--highres" off so that it could patch, and now that it's back it seems it's magic is gone. :confused:
I'm still guessing that it is because you are using cxg, when launching the game the GUI uses the following command:
```
/opt/cxgames/bin/wine --bottle "LOTRO" --workdir "path to LOTRO directory" -- lotroclient.exe arguments for lotroclient
```

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-05
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Checking run.log I find errors about sound. So I test a few things and remember I had changed to OSS during the time of my sound issues. Back to Alsa and those errors are all gone.. 
BUT.. still no luck in the playing departement. Still crashes after the first screen.
That is odd, in the directory where your user prefs should be look for a file called lotroclient.log, that (as the name suggests) is the clients log, there may be errors in that.

Another thing to try is running LotROLinux via a terminal see if the launcher is crashing for some reason, could be for some reason your config for LotROLinux has got corrupted so it is failing to launch wine.

Finally remove the fixme-all from WINEDEBUG and see what extra info that is spewing out, shouldn't be anything to make it crash but fixme's can sometimes give hints as to where a problem is.

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-08-05
> **thom_raindog said:**
> That's one mighty bummer. I can't play anymore!

I am rather clueless as to what to do..?
Have you updated anything on your comp. that can have changed your network settings? I had a network problem that resulted in the same LOTRO behaviour. It turned out to be a faulty entry in /etc/hosts. I've posted the solution a couple of pages back in this thread.

Your problem sounds similar, but it may be unrelated.

To save you the trouble of digging back, here's a [link](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=4937078&postcount=1189).

### Post by thom_raindog on 2008-08-05
> **cthulhu666 said:**
> Have you updated anything on your comp. that can have changed your network settings? I had a network problem that resulted in the same LOTRO behaviour. It turned out to be a faulty entry in /etc/hosts. I've posted the solution a couple of pages back in this thread.

Your problem sounds similar, but it may be unrelated.

To save you the trouble of digging back, here's a [link](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=4937078&postcount=1189).


Good gravy, that was it!
I had completely forgotten (as usually happens in a situation like that) what I had changed. I wrecked my brain unable to come up with anything.
Indeed I had changed my hostname from something_something to something-something and that caused the crash.
Why in the world does it do that?
Especially when my wifes PC running gentoo just as well has her hostname setup exactly the same patter as the one crashing LotrO for me but could play fine..?

Is it my AMD64 machine with a 64bit gentoo on it?

---

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-08-05
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Good gravy, that was it!
Hey, I'm not the only one to stumble unto to that problem ;)
Glad it worked for you.
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Why in the world does it do that?

Is it my AMD64 machine with a 64bit gentoo on it?
It's a Wine thing. It looks for your machines hostname, but cannot find it, since it (in my case, at least) isn't a fully quialified hostname (as in peters.home.com, as opposed to a non-fully qualified, as fx. peters.home).

I don't think it has anything to do with AMD64, since I'm running 32-bit on a Intel Core 2 Duo (IA64).

It's been a while, so I can't remember the exact details, but it's some variant of what I described.

---

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-08-06
> **thom_raindog said:**
> That leaves the question why it works on my wifes PC with the same wine-version and LotrO and a hostname that contains - which throws mine for a loop... weird.
I don't think it's the '-', that causes the trouble. It's the fact that Wine can't lookup your hostname, since it's probably not fully qualified or somehow otherwise invalid.

Without further knowledge of your network (hostnames, DNS etc.), I can't give you the exact reason.

I'm just glad we found a solution. ;)

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-07
> **BrowncoatElite said:**
> 
/usr/bin$ bash LotROLinux 
Exception in Gtk# callback delegate
  Note: Applications can use GLib.ExceptionManager.UnhandledException to handle the exception.
System.InvalidOperationException: File name has not been set
  at System.Diagnostics.Process.Start_common (System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo startInfo, 
You don't need the bash in front of LotROLinux, just LotROLinux will work (not that the bash is harming it in any way).

If I'm right then it is trying to run wine but not finding it. If you are using CXG then you need to tell it (Tools -> Options) and if your CXG is installed anywhere other that /opt/cxgames or ~/cxgames then you need to replace the wine in the program box with the full path to the wine script (including wine on the end) as by default CXG doesn't add itself to the path.

---

> You don't need the bash in front of LotROLinux, just LotROLinux will work (not that the bash is harming it in any way).

If I'm right then it is trying to run wine but not finding it. If you are using CXG then you need to tell it (Tools -> Options) and if your CXG is installed anywhere other that /opt/cxgames or ~/cxgames then you need to replace the wine in the program box with the full path to the wine script (including wine on the end) as by default CXG doesn't add itself to the path.

YAY! :):):) It loaded and got past the "error, cannot patch" screen!  For the record, I needed to change the wine location in the "program" tab and patch the game from the GUI.

Now the game loads to the character screen, but none of the buttons work.  If this has been discussed in a previous thread, excuse me - I'm still reading through it.  Looks like you had a similar problem back on page 9 of this thread.   Did you ever figure it out?

### Post by ObiWan2001 on 2008-08-10
Btw is there any plan to support different language news, ajackson?
Just asking because the german ones differs from the english and french ones atm.

launcher.config entry:
```
<add key="URL.NewsFeed" value="http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_{lang}.xml" />
```

The original launcher uses *en-gb* for english, *de* for german and *fr* for french instead of *{lang}*.

English: [http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_en-gb.xml](http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_en-gb.xml)
German: [http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_de.xml](http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_de.xml)
French: [http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_fr.xml](http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_fr.xml)

[http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_{lang}.xml](http://lotroeugls.com/News/cache_{lang}.xml) works for the english news too, but it looks like some kind of fallback.

---

### Post by thom_raindog on 2008-08-12
Adressing the stuttering and lagging problem that quite a few windows users complain about on the LotrO-Community and that hits a fair few wine users as well:

Did anyone ever try different file systems for the ~ partition to see if anything gets better (or worse)? It is rumored that that stuttering is LotrO trying to load textures off the harddrive so I wondered..

Another question: Someone mentioned turing off multiprocessor usage for LotrO as a solution. I use an AMD64 on gentoo 64 bit. What would I need to do to make wine run LotrO on just one core, or, does it do that by default anyway, in which case: How do I set it to use both to test if that helps?

And lastly: How can I (re-)nice wine to -15 or even -19? I would need to be root to do that, and since I can not alt-tab out of Lotro AT ALL I would have to go to a console, which works but is a bit of a workaround. Can I start it as niced somehow?

Thanks :)

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-12
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Did anyone ever try different file systems for the ~ partition to see if anything gets better (or worse)? It is rumored that that stuttering is LotrO trying to load textures off the harddrive so I wondered..
It's probably a product of poor coding (since it only really started after book 14) and maybe the hard drive being a bottleneck. The wine devs used to recommend not using ntfs partitions but that was largely due to the writing of them being problematic but I would still avoid using an ntfs partition.

> Another question: Someone mentioned turing off multiprocessor usage for LotrO as a solution. I use an AMD64 on gentoo 64 bit. What would I need to do to make wine run LotrO on just one core, or, does it do that by default anyway, in which case: How do I set it to use both to test if that helps?
I think it will use both if they are available but I'm not 100% certain you could try asking on the winehq forums.

> And lastly: How can I (re-)nice wine to -15 or even -19? I would need to be root to do that, and since I can not alt-tab out of Lotro AT ALL I would have to go to a console, which works but is a bit of a workaround. Can I start it as niced somehow
If all processes have the same nice value as their parent process you could try running LotROLinux and changing it's nice value. Failing that you should be able to switch to another desktop (ie not alt-tabbing) to change nice values.

### Post by SNy on 2008-08-15
That's probably because the window is set to be 'always on top'.
I am not sure about the way wine has been setting up fullscreen windows with mouse capture before, but when I start the game, its window happens to be 'always on top' so I need to switch that off to be able to ALT + TAB out.
For gnome, you reach the wm-menu via ALT + right click, dunno about KDE.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-08-18
OK, I'm home and I remembered this. :)

In winecfg I have:
[IMG]http://www.outergods.dk/winecfg.png[/IMG]

And the DISPLAY section of my UserPreferences.ini:
```
[Display]
SyncToRefresh=False
AllowWindowResizing=True
WindowedResolution=1024x768
ConfineFullScreenMouseCursor=True
AllowFakeFullScreen=False
FullScreen=True
RefreshRate=Auto
TripleBuffering=True
AllowDesktopCompositing=False
Antialiasing=Disabled
Resolution=1680x1050
ForceFakeFullScreen=False
```

I hope this is sufficient to make your ALT+TAB experience a more pleasentful one. :p

---

### Post by Beren Camlost on 2008-08-18
I´m on KDE and can´t Alt - Tab out. My workaround was to put LotRO on my second desktop and Shift - Tab out instead. Worked a charm for me.

### Post by not4us on 2008-08-18
Hi all,

I've been following this thread from way back when I was beta-testing LOTRO, however I've always ran it on my Windows gaming-desktop even though I have a few Linux boxes.
Now I've recently got an Acer Aspire One netbook that's running Linus Lite. I installed XP SP3 on it just to see if LOTRO would run on the Intel x3100 and I'm surprised to say that it runs pretty well on low/medium settings (I've added 512mb of RAM tot a total of 1gb and the game is installed on a 8gb SDHC card).

After all my rumbling, my question is if anybody has successfully run this on an Intel on-board graphics adapter like the X3100 on Wine?
I've searched this thread but all I could find was people that tried it long time ago (2007) and on older versions of Wine.

I'd love to put Ubuntu on the Aspire One and be able to run LOTRO on it, otherwise I'd have to stick with Windows :(

### Post by cthulhu666 on 2008-08-21
Hmmm... this game never fails to disappoint and frustrate me every now and then.

So I've just started doing my mount quests. After riding that bay steed up to Othrikar, I decided to hand in a few quests in north eastern North Downs. On the way I had an encounter with Bleakwind (nasty elite, just south of Angmar) which turned out quite bad. I was kicked all the way down to the farm west of Estledin. Why on earth did I have to be thrown a 10 minute walk away?!? ](*,)

Anyway, I decided to run down to continue the mount quest (to save me the 10 minute walk next time), so I started walking back to the mount dude in northern Bree-lands. I headed by Lin Giliath and south along the shore of the lake, but as soon as I entered Bree-lands (on the map, it's near the ruins just south west of the lake), I started rubbing-banding like mad. I'm running Wine 1.1.2, so I should have the network fix. But no matter what I did, I was trapped in a small square on the map with absolutely no chance to leave. I exited the game hoping that the rubber-banding would go away, instead I couldn't log back in for 5-10 minutes. When I finally got in, I was still trapped in that little square.
It was working just fine 2 days ago, when I did a full Garth Argawen run, but today it just sucks bull's balls.

Thanks a bunch Turbine Games! You really don't deserve €13 a month for this pile of crap. :evil:

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-08-22
> **cthulhu666 said:**
> I started rubbing-banding like mad. I'm running Wine 1.1.2, so I should have the network fix. But no matter what I did, I was trapped in a small square on the map with absolutely no chance to leave. I exited the game hoping that the rubber-banding would go away, instead I couldn't log back in for 5-10 minutes. When I finally got in, I was still trapped in that little square.
That is a server side problem, the zones automatically unload if there is no-one in them (to save processor time), it was a very good idea but sometimes the zone doesn't reload properly (by zone I mean mini-zones not area) so you can't enter it and do the rubberband dance on the border.

I am looking forward to the day that wine gets the original launcher working as I had enough with this game months ago, I was stupid enough to get a lifetime subscription so I don't mind keeping it up to date with the free patches and don't mind maintaining my launcher but Turbine are a joke and Codemasters are just incompetent so I won't be touching any more games that either are involved with.

---

### Post by SNy on 2008-09-09
I fiddled with that for a while as well after upgrading my hardware. I am using an older SB Live! card, so it might not fully reflect your setup.

Anyway, if you hear yourself, you have the monitor source activated which results in the mic input being echoed onto the output. While that doesn't hurt with the capturing itself, I find it quite annoying.

As for the capturing to work, if you're able to use sound recorder, you should be fine within the game. However, I found it to interfere with other applications (I can only use the ingame chat if I start the game prior to anything else [eg TeamSpeak] and then I can only use the mic in the game, not in TS [and vice-versa]). Maybe you have that same problem and some other application that tries to access the capture channel is running, preventing you from using the mic.
The game displays a list of available capturing devices in the audio tab, see which entries you have there. If it's not what you expect, compare to what sound recorder displays and also compare with the winecfg audio tab.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-09-14
> **amyg said:**
> My husband is begging me to try out the 14 day trial of this as he recently decided to take a break from WoW.  I use Wine and have seen some mention of being unable to use a launcher, etc.

As silly as this sounds (I'm still a n00b at linux), how do I go about getting it installed?  When I tried to install the trial through the Wine setup, it's come up with a screen that's saying it's trying to download Microsoft.Net framework.

Most of my issues are always user error, so I thought I'd ask before I messed things up.

What, you mean you didn't feel like reading the whole thread? :)

First of all, what type of graphics card do you have? If you have an Nvidia card, make sure you have the restricted driver enabled. If you have some other card, let us know what you have or search the forum and prepare to be disappointed, sorry. I do actually have it working on an ATI card on my laptop, but it's very slow compared to running it under Windows on the same machine. With an Nvidia card, performance is great.

Also, before you install the game, make sure you have the latest version of wine ([http://www.winehq.org/site/download-deb](http://www.winehq.org/site/download-deb)). For the install itself, Ajackson's page has very nice instructions for installing the game under Ubuntu: [http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/how-to.php) . In the "Laucnher script" part of the instructions, you can select either a command-line launcher script or the GUI. For you, I suggest you just install the GUI launcher, it's probably a bit more newbie-friendly looking.

If you're unsure how to do some part of the instructions, search the forums here or ask more. A few tips: to enter wineconfig, type "winecfg" in the terminal, and to edit the wine registry, type "regedit", browse to the correct location and right click to add keys. 

The installation is not hard to do under Linux, you just need to follow all the steps of the instructions. Good luck :)

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-09-14
So...just to clarify: the game works great under Linux but the launcher doesn't. So that's why you need to use either a command-line launcher script created by SNy or a Linux GUI launcher made by Ajackson. Without these heroes, we couldn't play the game under Linux at all.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-09-14
> **amyg said:**
> I can open one of the launchers, it comes up to where I can put in my account name and my password.  Below it says : 

This "no language files found" is with the GUI launcher I guess? I remember reading about someone having that problem before, and I think it might be as simple as making sure the game directory is set correctly under "Tools -> Options" in the GUI launcher. The game is installed under "~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Codemasters/The Lord of the Rings Online". Another common installation problem is installing something as root. So if you did, change the owner of the game directory back to yourself.

If you can play WOW or any other 3D game under linux, your graphics card drivers are fine, no problem. And that card should run the game well.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-09-14
> **amyg said:**
> I'm not sure where to find information on the GUI launcher to get to the options, but I'll look and see if I can find it =)


If you added Ajackson's repository (see [http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php)), you should be able to just install the lotrolinux launcher with synaptic or "sudo apt-get install lotrolinux". After this, you can launch it from the terminal (if you don't see a shortcut anywhere) by just typing "LotROLinux". Then from the Tools menu, select options and under "Game Directory" change it to point to your directory. See if that helps.

# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

I'm running Ubuntu 8.04 2.6.24-19-generic on a Lenovo 3000 N200.
Thanks!

### Post by SNy on 2008-09-15
> **amyg said:**
> Now I have this error:

"Could not patch Turbinelauncher.exe error 32. Sharing violation"

Hm. Depending on the way you installed the game in the first place (or a patch attemp as root), this could hint to a permission problem. Check that the user you are starting the launcher as (ie you) actually has all the rights on the files and directories in the LOTRO installation folder.
Should you not be able to make out any error, you could try to use the script launcher from my site (see signature) which has been said to be a bit more helpful with patching problems.

SNy

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-09-18

I've never played the game on Vista/DX10 but since you said your husband runs Vista, my guess is that this "dullness" of graphics is actually the difference between the DX9 mode (that wine uses) and the DX10 graphics. There's supposed to be a difference. You can test this by asking your husband to test this by changing Lotro's UserPreferences.ini file's DirectX 10 option (forget what it's called, but it's there) to "disabled" / "off" whatever, so that the Vista machine will use DX9 too for a test. If it looks the same then on both machines, that's it. Unfortunately there's nothing you can do to enable that in Linux, wine doesn't support it. I've never seen it, so I don't know what I'm missing, thankfully :)

### Post by SNy on 2008-09-18
Apart from DX10, it might also be the "Postprocessing Effects" setting.
If you activate this, you will have proper of water surfaces and also some other visual effects like some sort of blur/fog near campfires and such. This might also contribute to a different visual appearance, depending on the effect being enabled and the value set if enabled.

### Post by SNy on 2008-09-19
Yeah, that sounds exactly as if you used an older wine version.
It might not be firefox using 100% CPU, but the faulty game code, that tries to send the same data over and over again in some border condition. It is what causes your glitchy experience, as well. See [http://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=12302](http://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=12302) for details

Run ```
# wine --version
``` from the terminal and see what the result is. The fix has been in place since... 1.1.2 I think. Latest wine from the winehq repository is 1.1.4, with their two-week release cycle maybe 1.1.5 by the end of the day.

HTH,
SNy

---

Hey there, it's been a while. I have reacently had to replace an HDD in my PC, which as forced me to try to reinstall, and get Lotro working in linux again.
Some of the biggest problems I had was 1) I run and ATI card. This used to be address nicely by Envy drivers, but they just totally messed up the display when I started up lotro. This was fixed due to a nice post by Batpenguin on page 126 (roughly), 
With the display issues fixed, I was able to start the game up and go through the first couple of default screens and go to the point whre I got the "Error, cannot update!" message. I then proceeded to get Ajackson's loader working (for the first time), and ran the patching tool. It spent a very long time updating the hires dat files, then it appear to hang up. I let it sit for an hour or so with no further activity, then I cancelled the patch process.
Now, what baffles me is that I am now getting the "Game Error [128]" message now when I try to run Lotro. The program doesn't even go as far as it used to in the loading process. 
I have edited the regedit stuff and added both keys. I have edited the ini file. I have also completely replaced my lotro directory with the one from my native windows xp install. ### Post by ajackson on 2008-10-19
> **Vaitya said:**
> A line from glxinfo interests me a little:
"direct rendering: No (If you want to find out why, try setting LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose)"

not sure what it is referring to about libgl_debug=verbose though.

That is the problem, the drivers you are using don't allow the full functionality that the game needs.

The LIBGL_DEBUG stuff is a way of getting additional information out of glxinfo, what extra info it provides I don't know and chances are wouldn't understand it anyway :)

Getting the ATI drivers working right is the key to solving your problem but I don't have an ATI card to experiment with so the best I can do is provide encouragement.

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-10-19
> **Vaitya said:**
> 
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.4 (2.1 Mesa 7.0.3-rc2)

A line from glxinfo interests me a little:
"direct rendering: No (If you want to find out why, try setting LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose)"

Any thoughts would be most appreciated :)

Thanks!

Looks like the ATI driver is not installed or enabled, that should say "ATI Technologies" and Direct Rendering should be Yes.

This could be as simple as xorg.conf not having the "fglrx" driver set - see if it says "radeon", if it does, change it to fglrx, and don't forget to do the module disabling trick I mentioned. Starting the ATI Catalyst control center is an easy way to see everything's working too, there should be a link in your menu. Anyway, see my previous post for general stuff (which you mentioned reading, too):

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=5100027&postcount=1280](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=5100027&postcount=1280)

and here's the latest ATI driver:

[http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html](http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html)

Looks like it's just been updated. I actually haven't played with that ATI laptop in Ubuntu for months now, I think I'll update myself too and see if performance is better (it used to be slow enough that I just booted to Windows...the ATI card I have is really slow, though, yours sounds much newer).

### Post by ajackson on 2008-10-19
> **Vaitya said:**
> 2) When I use Ajackson's graphical launcher I am fforced into the character creation mode which I cannot exit. I assume that for some reason that launcher is not reading the character data or some such properly. however, Sny's script works flawlessly.
All the script and GUI do is get the login queue for the specified server, add your request to get a ticket number and fire off the client with that as one of the many parameters.

Double-check that you are specifying the same server on both the GUI and CLI. If the same server is being used for both then I'll have to do a bit of digging (if I can persuade the GUI to work on Intrepid that is).

> **Vaitya said:**
> 
1) During one of my successful launches I was playing with the game settings and crashed the game (surprise there). Now, my desktop is stuck in 800x600 mode (from when lotro changes resolutions). I cannot seem to change it back by either the resolution settings on the system menu, nor by opening up lotro and closing it successfully.

I haven't had that problem, but I play at the display's native resolution anyway, so there's no need for it to change resolutions.

Do CTRL+ALT+PLUS and CTRL+ALT+MINUS work for you? Those are or at least used to be (doesn't seem to do anything for me now, hmm) shortcuts for cycling resolutions up and down. If nothing else works, you can avoid a reboot by doing CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE (restart X, login again). Disabling Compiz-Fusion (desktop effects) might be a good idea too. I keep them on when I play and I haven't noticed problems with my Nvidia card, but there could be issues with the ATI driver and using that, dunno. Worth a try disabling them, if you use them (search for compizswitcher in the forums for a cool script that disables and enables effects when launching programs).

Anyway, how's the performance otherwise in the game with your card? Comparable to Windows or clearly slower?

### Post by Vaitya on 2008-10-19
Ajackson - Rather embarassing, but I was pointed to the wrong server for some reason. I changed it back to my usual one, and all works great again.

Batpenguin - I'll play around with those options for reloading X. The ATI catalyst centre works fine for changing the resolutions, just the reboot that skews everything. I suspect there's a setting in a boot file that has been borked by the crash, but I'll investigate a bit after I satisfy my lotro addiction for a bit.
As far as how the game works - When I load up I have to alt+enter into windowed mode, then again back to ful screen. If I don't do this, the game flickers like mad and is basically unplayable. I noticed the same thing actually happens with playing movie files, have to flip back and forth to full screen a couple of times before things settle down.
Other than that, I'm a very happy camper. My linux box is a whole lot more powerful than my old acer laptop, so I can get out of the minimum settings now. I basically run lotro in max settings, with a few exceptions - I turn of most of the shadowing, some of the lighting settings, and I try to avoid ansiotropic settings (my card chugs hard when I use this). Also, no post-processing effects due to the ATI card (will probably replace this someday). Over all, the colours are much richer on my linux box, the system handles all the high settings very well, and I never had to slow down due to overheating (I installed a liquid cooling system).
One minor thing that bugs me is that for some reason I cannot get my mic to work in lotro, or even using native linux apps. Think I saw a post or thread on this somewhere, but was more interested in getting the game up first. It's something I can live with for a while anyway.

Thanks to all of your efforts and quick responses - I never expected to get up and running this week-end at all :)

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-10-20
> **Vaitya said:**
> Actually...just noticed that while grouped with more than 1-2 people I get incredible lag. Makes it hard to Rift, will have ot break out the laptop for rifting for now I guess.

Do you have the latest wine version installed from WineHQ and not the Ubuntu repository version? There's the major lag problem with older versions so if you're using an older version, install the WineHQ repository and the latest Wine.

Also: if you're using the desktop effects, I've noticed that the option "Unredirect fullscreen windows" under System -> Preferences -> Advanced Desktop Effect Settings -> General can do nasty fullscreen flickering things with programs (at least XBMC for me) if enabled. So uncheck that. I keep talking about the Compiz desktop effects stuff, but I actually don't know if anybody else uses them. Oh well.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-10-23
It's been brought to my attention via the CodeWeavers forum for LotRO that the login process seems to have changed.

So far my testing has only shown problems with Gilrain and Snowbourn (EU), one of the German servers worked OK.

The problem seems be that for some servers you no longer have to join the world queue before starting the game (ie login process is identical to DDO).

I have a modified piece of code that seems to get around the problem but I'd like some people to test it (especially US users as I can't test against the US client).

In the file MainWindow.cs, find
```
private void GetRealmDetails()
```

Replace the bit that reads:
```

if (settings.UsingDND())
{
	StartClient();
} else {
	EnterWorldQueue(realm.QueueURL);
}

```
With:
```

if (settings.UsingDND())
{
	StartClient();
} else {
	if (realm.QueueURL == "")
	{
		StartClient();
	} else {
		EnterWorldQueue(realm.QueueURL);
	}
}

```

The change basically checks if the queue URL contains a value, if it does then login the normal way, if not skip the world queue bit.

It works for me using various EU servers but I'd like confirmation from other EU users and US users before I commit the change.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-10-26
> **Bad-Taste said:**
> I have one problem with the lotrolauncher.script from SNy.

When I type in my name and password I am getting this.

```
Your choice (enter the number on the left)? 10
2008-10-25 22:04:15 URL:http://94.75.194.7/status/cache_morthond.xml [877/877] -> ".launcher/server.config" [1]

Connecting to world login queue for realm [DE] Morthond...
http://195.33.152.250/login/login_queue.php:
2008-10-25 22:04:16 FEHLER 404: Not Found.

Error: World login queue response indicates failure.

```

It seems something has chanced with the login-server...

Can you help me please? 
That is the same problem the GUI was having, a rather ugly hack fix is find the following in the script
```

echo -e "\nConnecting to world login queue for realm ${serverNames[$selectedServer]}...";
echo -e "\n[${worldQueue_URL}]\n";
wget $wgetNoCheckCertOption -nv --post-data="${worldQueue_ARGS}" "${worldQueue_URL}" -O .launcher/WorldQueue.config
if ! [ -r .launcher/WorldQueue.config ] ; then
    echo -e "\nError: World login queue request failed.\n"
    cd "$oldDir"
    exit
fi

# check the result, should be HRESULT 0x00000000, indicating success (Windows API madness)
hresult=`grep -h "<HResult>" .launcher/WorldQueue.config | sed -e "s/^.*<HResult>//;s/<\/HResult>.*$//"`
if [ "$hresult" != "0x00000000" ] ; then
    echo -e "\nError: World login queue response indicates failure."
    cd "$oldDir"
    exit
fi

```
and change it to
```

echo -e "\nConnecting to world login queue for realm ${serverNames[$selectedServer]}...";
echo -e "\n[${worldQueue_URL}]\n";
####wget $wgetNoCheckCertOption -nv --post-data="${worldQueue_ARGS}" "${worldQueue_URL}" -O .launcher/WorldQueue.config
####if ! [ -r .launcher/WorldQueue.config ] ; then
####    echo -e "\nError: World login queue request failed.\n"
####    cd "$oldDir"
####    exit
####fi

# check the result, should be HRESULT 0x00000000, indicating success (Windows API madness)
####hresult=`grep -h "<HResult>" .launcher/WorldQueue.config | sed -e "s/^.*<HResult>//;s/<\/HResult>.*$//"`
####if [ "$hresult" != "0x00000000" ] ; then
####    echo -e "\nError: World login queue response indicates failure."
####    cd "$oldDir"
####    exit
####fi

```
You only need one # rather than all 4 but it sticks out where you made the mod with 4. Of course if you play on multiple servers there is no guarantee that both need that fix but I'm sure the next time SNy reads this thread he'll mod his script in a less hacky way than my mod.

### Post by SNy on 2008-11-06
For those of you who suddenly (as of today) have problems logging in with the script...
If you happened to not need the --no-check-certificate option before, you might need it now, because they changed servers around, the GLS auth config now points to an IP address, yet the server certificate still indicates lotroeugls.com. This will result wget to fail without the option set.

SNy

### Post by enigma83 on 2008-11-12
Anyone gotten the Mines of Moria update working in Wine, or is this yet another fallback to Windows?   I've got the 2.7GB downloaded, however the update won't install.  
On startup, I get a message box saying "Access violation at address 617EB1DE. Write of address 0000091C".
Pressing OK moves onto the main window fine. Pressing "Next" brings up the EULA page, but where the EULA should be, I get a blank box, and a pop up saying "External exception 80000101".  The console at this stage prints out 
```
dater_0.0_2.02.exe.tmp: wrap.c:617: ME_WrapMarkedParagraphs: Assertion `endRowPara->member.para.nFlags & 0x10' failed.
```
At this point, pressing ok just spawns another dialog box and repeats that message in the console.

Guess it's time to go back to my Windows install.

Edit:
Even more fun.  The update won't install on a fat32 drive (file system limitation).  See [http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=172648&page=2](http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=172648&page=2)

Am now creating a new vmware image drive, will format to NTFS, copy game over, modify registry, update and copy back to see if that works.

Fun fact number 3.  FAT32 cannot handle the number of files in the bindat folder in MoM.  Hurrah.  Going to go with networked share on my file server to store the game for now.

Final results:
MoM cannot be run from a FAT32 partition.  The MoM updater does not run under wine (that I could tell).
My solution:  Update to MoM inside Windows (2000 for me).  Then copy it over SMB to an ext2 partition (any other FS should work here.  It probably also works to just mount the share as a drive, and then tell Windows to look at that.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-11-15
> **ShadowKidd said:**
> Also after upgrading and using the 8.04 package, the GUI launcher reports "Erroring accessing GLS data centre."  If I go to Tools->Switch Game, then select Lord of the Rings and click Ok, it will then be able to contact the GLS and get the world queue.
Are you using 0.9.7? Because the initial problem was being caused by the connection to the GLS data centre timing out so I removed anything that would set a timeout time on the connects. I'm assuming there is a bug in intrepids mono causing the problem as the same code works fine everywhere else.

Also check that you re-enable the repository for LotROLinux (if you used it of course) as the update procedure disables all 3rd party ones. There probably won't be an intrepid rep as the hardy one works fine still.

### Post by enigma83 on 2008-11-18
Servers are fixed.  Those who paid attention would notice that the launcher installs VC++ libraries.  You now need VC2005 libraries.

Get winetricks from [http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks](http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks)

Download the script.
Run it with
bash /path/to/winetricks vcrun2005
bash /path/to/winetricks vcrun2005sp1

The first installs the libraries, second installs SP1 for them.

Once that is done, I got in to the game fine :)

---

Get winetricks from [http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks](http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks)

Download the script.
Run it with

The first installs the libraries, second installs SP1 for them.

### Post by lexpenrose on 2008-11-18
For people that don't get this to work , here's a mini howto.

** Don't forget to get the LotROLinux-0.9.7 version ** 
for people who have the old version 0.9.6 , it does not work anymore.
Get it from Sourceforge

I couldn't get this to work in any way with my old wine and actually had to reinstall wine !

*copy your Codemasters directory to a safe location first !!

sudo apt-get autoremove wine
sudo rm -r ~/.wine
*make sure everything of wine is removed !
sudo apt-get install wine
*wait for it to complete and make the ~/.wine directory
*copy back the Codemasters directory to ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/
Then use enigma's tip : 

Get winetricks from [http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks](http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks)

Download the script.
Run it with
bash /path/to/winetricks vcrun2005
bash /path/to/winetricks vcrun2005sp1


Then the game should run. ( I get logon server busy though, but that's normal apparently )

Some info I gathered while trying to get it to work under my old wine ( maybe it helps someone ) :

Prerequisite : Having a working LOTRO.14 install

Download offline MoM patches 1 ( 2+gig ) and 2 ( 500+mb ) from [www.lotro-europe.com](www.lotro-europe.com)
Extract Mines_of_Moria_Standalone_Patch1.zip and Mines_of_Moria_Standalone_Patch2.zip
run Mines_of_Moria_Standalone_Patch1.exe which extracts itself 
Then run Updater_0.0_2.01.exe which updates LOTRO.14 to MoM.1
Then run Mines_of_Moria_Standalone_Patch2.exe which doens't extract but just patches.
The patching took about 1 hour on my laptop.

Start Lotrolauncher.script and say Yes to the updates question.
It will update. They changed the updater dll so now you can actually see the progress. I like it.

go to synaptic package manager and 
- install Cabextract

then
go to your LOTRO directory ( ./wine/drive_c/ etc etc )
wget [http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks](http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks)
chmod +x ./winetricks
./winetricks

btw, I'm still downloading patches now... sigh. After having patched the game with the 2 predownloadable offline patches, it still takes about 4 hours to get the patches installed.

Don't know what's going on, but I'm getting it too.  The patch notes mentioned a FakeFullScreen thing, so I'm going to play with that see if I get something.

Maybe not real useful, but I found a partial fix.  Well, it works for me at least.
For the record, I use XFCE.
Change workspaces and change back to have LotRO go funny.
Alt tab to another window in that workspace, and alt tab back.  Now LotRO is back to normal for me.

 (image) [http://i6.photobucket.com/albums/y244/dnal/Screenshot-5.png](http://i6.photobucket.com/albums/y244/dnal/Screenshot-5.png)

### Post by magpa on 2008-11-19
> **amyg said:**
> Alright, so I uninstalled the lotro launcher via synaptic, and then reinstalled.  It is now virtually an empty box, there are no realm choices, no option to patch, nothing.

 (image) [http://i6.photobucket.com/albums/y244/dnal/Screenshot-5.png](http://i6.photobucket.com/albums/y244/dnal/Screenshot-5.png)

My LotROLinux folder has nothing but LotROLinux.config in it now.

What did I do wrong during the reinstall?  I don't recall having this issue before.  I know I had several and SNy and Batpenguin (I believe) were awesomely patient with me).

My husband is really getting antsy for me to try out MoM but I'm farther away than when I started it seems.  Where do I go from here?

I really don't want to have to uninstall everything and start over.

Did you download and install the launcher from [**here**](https://sourceforge.net/project/showfiles.php?group_id=200627)?
Download and unpack the file LotROLinux-0.9.7.tar.bz2
then
```

cd /path/to/LotROLinux-0.9.7/
./configure
make
sudo make install
```
That compiles and installs the launcher.

If the game is patched to version 2.02 (MoM) open the launcher and go to Tools->Options and make sure the game directory is pointing to the game folder (something like ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Codemasters/The Lord of the Rings Online). I'm not sure if that's necessary but I usually do that.

Then either use the winetricks-guide by enigma83 and lexpenrose or do what I ended up doing, downloading the files needed from Microsoft Download (google vcredist_x86.exe and it should be the first hit, don't forget to also download the sp1-upgrade aswell) and installing them via wine. After that run the patch again to apply the upgrade, output will be something like ```
installing <file>
applying <file>
...
```

[http://www.lotrolinux.com](http://www.lotrolinux.com) is a good place for further help installing the lotro-launcher if this didn't make any sense (It's 5.30 am here so I'm a bit tired ;)).

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-11-20
After reading all these problems people are having, I just wanted to say that at least for me, it went without any problems without downloading any standalone patches: I downloaded winetricks and installed the new required stuff (vcrun options), and then just used the GUI launcher's patch function all the way. It hang a few times, and I had to restart the patching, but it always resumed just fine after restarting the GUI launcher. The next morning it was done, seems to work with no problems. This is with Hardy and the latest GUI launcher version.

For those having problems, make sure you're not running something as root (sudo) and messing up your permissions. For me getting the game patched really didn't seem to require anything more than the winetricks install, so I'm surprised there's so many people with problems. I always update to latest wine and latest GUI launcher versions once they're available, other than that I haven't done anything special.

I recall seeing it look like that once before, and it probably had to do with either the game directory being set incorrectly or wrong game selected. So I'd try setting the directory to point to the correct wine directory and selecting Lord of the Rings again in the game selection screen, if you haven't tried that before.

### Post by SNy on 2008-11-20
I am with BatPenguin here, it looks as if the game directory was not properly set ('no language files found' is a rather strong indicator).
Check the options in the launcher (game type and installation path) and see if that gives you any progress.

For the record, I patched using the two standalone patches. I already mentioned the only quirk I came by (patchclient.dll update removed it, manual copy needed), otherwise it worked fine (using my script so I see what's going on while patching).

The game itself runs OK as before with one very annoying exception: if it looses focus (changing desktop/app or even after having the screensaver activated), it will just stop responding and needs to be killed. This certainly needs some investigation.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-11-21
> **cameran said:**
> hi,

i've reinstalled lotro on intrepid to play the new mines of moria expansion.  i keep getting the following error:

"Error getting world queue configuration"

when using the graphical launcher.  I have played lots of lotro with the graphical launcher up until last week and had no problems.  anyone know what the new issue is?

cameran

I think two weeks ago or so the GUI launcher was updated to deal with some changes to the login procedure, so make sure you have the repository enabled after the 8.10 update (update disables 3rd party repos) and that the GUI launcher is the latest version. If you really played up to last week then you probably should have the latest version, but still worth checking.

Also (and this goes for others having GUI launcher problems, like Amyg), try using the command line launcher and see if that works better for you.

### Post by cameran on 2008-11-21
hi,

thanks for the reply.  i fixed it by going to "switch game" and then pressing OK.  that clears the problem and i can log in again.  i have to do this each time i want to log in.  what a weird problem but at least there is a simple solution.

cameran

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-11-21
> **cameran said:**
> thanks for the reply.  i fixed it by going to "switch game" and then pressing OK.  that clears the problem and i can log in again.  i have to do this each time i want to log in.  what a weird problem but at least there is a simple solution.

Sounds like the problem I (hopefully) fixed in 0.9.7. Basically for reasons known only to intrepid's mono the initial call to the GLS data server took an age and timed out. In 0.9.7 I modded it with an almost infinite timeout (could make the app hang if you genuinely don't have a connect). For some reason the switch game trick solves the timeout issue. Since that mod on my intepid it connected (eventually) everytime but YMMV.

PS Thanks for all the thanks and well wishes, I'm still reading the thread and will chip in from time to time but as I said well-being before, well everything else really :)

---

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-11-22
> **amyg said:**
> 

Do you want to check for updates (y/N)? y
Checking for updates...
wine: /home/mom/.wine is not owned by you
wine: /home/mom/.wine is not owned by you
wine: /home/mom/.wine is not owned by you


Based on that, your problems with all of these launchers/updates are probably due to permissions set incorrectly. I think it's been suggested earlier, I might've said myself too that "make sure you're not running anything as root/sudo" or something like that (if I didn't, I should've), this is pretty common although it really shouldn't be. Aside from actually installing wine with apt, you don't need to be using root permissions for anything with Lotro, just use your own account.

Well, in any case, try making yourself the owner of the whole .wine directory and see what happens. Your username is apparently "mom", so open up a terminal and do this:

```
cd /home/mom
sudo chown mom .wine -R

```

If your username is not "mom" then use whatever it is. Let me know how it goes.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-11-22
> **amyg said:**
> Thanks for responding ajackson.  You're right it's amy instead of mom.

Anywho, I typed it in and it accepted the code with amy instead of mom.  However, when I retried the launcher script, it still required a password, etc.  

=/

..and actually, I just remembered that Lotro uses two other directories in addition to the .wine directory. These are, in your case, "/home/amy/.LotROLinux" and "/home/amy/The Lord of the Rings Online". So these might be owned by the wrong user as well, so open a terminal and do:

```
sudo chown amy .LotROLinux
sudo chown amy "The Lord of the Rings Online"

```

So try that too.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2008-11-23
> **amyg said:**
> no change.  my poor emo husband is hinting that i should go back to windows hah.  i can't blame him when it's obviously this hard for me and so easy for others.


Well, hopefully we can get this sorted out before such nuclear options become necessary :). You must've had other reasons to pick Ubuntu for your OS, so if it works otherwise, hopefully this won't be what forces you to stop using it. This is a bit off-topic but related I suppose, I do suggest you read up a bit about using the terminal and about permissions. It will make your life a lot easier if you go over stuff like this so you better understand our suggestions:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UsingTheTerminal](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UsingTheTerminal)

and this:

[http://www.linuxcommand.org/](http://www.linuxcommand.org/)

In general, getting this to run requires basic knowledge of the command line so it's a good idea to actually go looking for basic tutorials, if the help people give here doesn't seem clear. Of course we'll explain if you ask, but there's probably much better resources available on the forums for properly explaining the commands. All these commands (chown, running scripts etc.) are quite basic things, an evening of tutorials should cover it and then it'll make much more sense when somebody asks you to check permissions of files.

Anyway, getting back to the problem, you said there's no change. Actually, there **must **be some change to the output of the commands now that the permissions issues are dealt with, so please post whatever the command line spits out when you try to use the script.

Also, post what the GUI launcher does when you try to use it. It should work, I just suggested the command line script as an alternative so keep trying these both. So, in the GUI launcher: Can you patch it now by selecting Tools-Patch. Do you see your account name, can you log in? What happens? And what does it save in:

/home/mom/.LotROLinux/patch.log

and

home/mom/.LotROLinux/run.log

In general, all commands will give you output that can either be a bit useful ("error 194") or very useful ("directory not owned by you"), so please always post the output when a command doesn't work, it's the only clue we have for trying to troubleshoot these things. 

I'm going to offer a few quick suggestions again:

```
sudo chown amy /home/amy/.winetrickscache -R
```

Just in case your error 194 is caused by the winetricks directory having wrong permissions, this will change the owner of the winetricks cache. Then rerun winetricks after this - no sudo - by typing 

```
./winetricks
```

in the directory where you downloaded it and selecting the vcrun2005 and vcrun2005sp1. If it keeps failing, then I'm out of ideas about winetricks. If somebody know what files to check in wine's system directories to see the vcrun stuff was installed, please post.

Also post the output of this:

```
df -ha
```

This will show you free disk space. Check that you have space remaining on the drive or post the output so we can see.

And while we're at it, post the output of these as well:

```
wine --version
```

```
uname -rv
```

For wine and kernel versions. And check the version number of the GUI launcher from Help-About. Please give a detailed description of what happens when you use the GUI launcher now.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2008-11-23
I think a lot of the problems are because for some reason you are using /home/mom/.wine rather than /home/<your username>/.wine. Wine doesn't like using other peoples wine directoroes (without the use of WINEPREFIX anyway).

Do you have a .wine in **your** home directory (ie the one it defaults to when you open a terminal)? If so is LOTRO installed in there?

Wine's default without using WINEPREFIX is to look for stuff in /home/username/.wine.

wine --version

### Post by amyg on 2008-11-23
> **ajackson said:**
> I think a lot of the problems are because for some reason you are using /home/mom/.wine rather than /home/<your username>/.wine. Wine doesn't like using other peoples wine directoroes (without the use of WINEPREFIX anyway).

Do you have a .wine in **your** home directory (ie the one it defaults to when you open a terminal)? If so is LOTRO installed in there?

Wine's default without using WINEPREFIX is to look for stuff in /home/username/.wine.

yes, my default, though named 'mom' is my home directory which has the .wine folder.  when i open terminal the default is - amy@mom-desktop:~$ - in which if i then type cd .wine it goes to the wine folder.

like i've said, a good friend helped me set all of this up, and of course i haven't been able to reach him to see if he could help me get this figured out - so i very much appreciate all of the patience that you, batpenguin, and anyone else has had for me.  i apologize, because i'm sure it's very frustrating from your end when it probably seems very simple to all of you.

### Post by ajackson on 2008-11-23
> **amyg said:**
> yes, my default, though named 'mom' is my home directory which has the .wine folder.  when i open terminal the default is - amy@mom-desktop:~$ - in which if i then type cd .wine it goes to the wine folder.
OK I'm slightly confused, open a terminal and type
```
whoami
```
```
pwd
```

The first shows your username, the second what directory you are currently in (this should be your home directory).

If you are certain that LOTRO is installed in /home/mom/.wine, then you have two choices.

1) Reinstall in your .wine directory
2) replace your .wine directory with the one from /home/mom

The first is fairly fool-proof but takes an age, the second would be my prefered option but you may still find some files with the wrong owner but that can be fixed.

To do number 2 type the following **from /home/amy**
```
rm -rf .wine
mv /home/mom/.wine .
```
the first line removes (forcible) the existing .wine directory, the second line move the .wine from /home/mom to the current directory.

In the GUI you'll probably have to alter the game directory to it's new location, after that it should be able to find all the files it needs (which I think is what was causing the game to not start after clicking login).

> so i very much appreciate all of the patience that you, batpenguin, and anyone else has had for me.  i apologize, because i'm sure it's very frustrating from your end when it probably seems very simple to all of you.
We all had to learn linux at some stage and trust me I've made more than a few mistakes while learning.
