### Post by Duskao on 2009-05-25
Alright, this is how I got it going on my compy. 

First go here [http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X](http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X)

Go to the OS/X part. I'm not kidding... I know, I know, it's weird. But bear with me. 

There is no need to download crossover games, you can use wine, but I would recommend a new prefix for it either way. 

Choose standard Res for download cause the high res doesn't seem to work properly/if at all. It's about 5 or 6 gigs, can't remember. Huge anyway. 

Hint: after downloading, put the huge folder in a second partition or usb flash drive, or burn it to disc so you don't have to download it again if you need to reinstall ubuntu or if anything happens, you'll have it.

Go here [http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php)

Download it. I would recommend the source files because for some reason I never got the repository to work.... Odd eh. If you get it to work, let me know how. But the source is quite easy to use, the instructions are good so just follow them. They are in the README in the new folder after extracing the turball. 

Once your standard res download is complete then install it in through wine. ie. MoriaSetup.exe. Once that is complete then open up pylotro and then you will have to "search" for the game installation. Don't worry it finds it automatically, you don't need to search through folders and junk. Once that is done you will have to "patch" your installation. Also done automatically, but it takes a while.

Ok, we are getting to the end of all this work, but first a couple wine registry entries. Relax, it's not that hard.

First, go to a folder. Doesn't matter which one. "home" is fine. Then click the "Edit" tab up near the top, by the "File" tab. Then "Prefrences" at the bottom of the column. In the "View" tab make sure the "Show hidden and backup files" box is checked. Close that window. 
Now find the .wine folder. Should be in your "home folder". Then double click the "drive_c" folder, then double click the "windows" folder. Now open up "regedit.exe" with wine. Click expand "HKEY_CURRENT_USER" then "software". Now right click "Wine" and choose new > Key. Make the new Key "Direct3D" no quotes, no periods, so spaces. Ensure the capitals are where they are supposed to be. Now right click "Direct3D" and choose new > string. Make the new string "OffscreenRenderingMode" no quotes. Now double click "OffscreenRenderingMode" and put "fbo" in there. Right click "Direct3D" and make a new string again, this time "VideoMemorySize", remember no quotes. Then double click that and put in the amount of RAM your video card has. I have a Radeon 4850 and it has 512 megs of RAM so I put in "512". If you have 256 megs of ram put in "256". 
If you need a bit of a referance go here 
[http://wiki.winehq.org/UsefulRegistryKeys](http://wiki.winehq.org/UsefulRegistryKeys)

Now, you should be able to play Lotro in linux. Don't expect the same performance as in windows, you won't get it at this time. Maybe in the future when wine matures a bit more. But for me it is easily playable. I have heard that it works better with nvidia cards, but it works fine with my Radeon 4850. 

One last thing. If you want to make a shortcut for the game on your bar/panel at the top you can. Right click the bar/panel then choose "add to Panel..." Chooes "Custom Application Launcher" then "add" or double click it. 

Type - Application in Terminal
Name - LOTRO (or whatever you want it to be)
Command - pylotro
Comment - Lord of the Rings Online (or whatever you want it to be)

You can chance the icon for it by clicking the icon button to the left of Type -. Try google to find the real icon.

click "Ok"

Now you have a new icon on your panel to start up LOTRO with one click. 

Hope all this works, it works very well for me. 
Best of luck.

---

In regards to ajackson. 
I'm still new to ubuntu so that was how I got LOTRO up and running.

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-03
> **ubuntuman001 said:**
> installing VC++
I'm pretty certain that is what is causing the problem. If you install the game from a MoM download (or the MoM retail disks) it should take care of installing the VC++ for you. If you then install VC++ on top yourself it seems to upset things (why I don't know).

Steps you can take to correct it:

If we assume that you used /home/username/.wine-lotro as your WINEPREFIX

Rename that prefix
```
mv $HOME/.wine-lotro $HOME/.wine-temp
```

Create a new empty prefix for LotRO and install VC++ into it (yes this time you need to as you aren't letting the installer do it for you).

Navigate to the Program Files directory inside this new prefix and copy/move the relevant (Codemasters or Turbine) directory from your original prefix into this one)
```
mv $HOME/.wine-temp/drive_c/Prog*/Turbine .
```

Set the windows version to 2000 and give the patching another whirl (if you used the standalone pylotro you need to install that inside that prefix as well).

The other way is to get rid of your existing wine prefix with lotro in it and sit through the install process again.

The golden rule when thinking about installing the VC++ run times is use the config checker to see if they are installed, if not install them.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-03
> **superdif said:**
> It attempts it for 20 times, but then it says:
Well it could be that the login server is down (not lotroeugls.com, that is the initial stage) or more likely one of the ports lotro uses is being blocked. By who is another matter, it could be by your linux installation, it could be your router or it could be by your ISP. If it is the latter all you can do is ask them to reopen them (but expect no as an answer).

There is was a thread on both official sites saying which ports need to be opened but I don't have the links to hand.

### Post by superdif on 2009-06-05
> **ajackson said:**

There is was a thread on both official sites saying which ports need to be opened but I don't have the links to hand.

Ajackson,
thank for the answer and sorry if I didn't followed up, but in the last few days I have been busy (both with the LOTRO attempts and other stuff).

Wednesday I had been able to install LOTRO on an old Win machine and I noticed that I had the same problem (*"Searching for logon server...."* up to 20/20) so I concluded that the problem was not due to the Linux machine itself.

Then I tried to look on the router, and more specifically I tried to look around for the ports I needed to open (if it does matter, I have a D-Link DIR-120 as router and it is connected to the DSL modem provided by my ISP), but I found some discording sources (some post reporting more ports than others):
[http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=87737]("http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=87737");
[http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=248309]("http://forums.lotro.com/showthread.php?t=248309");
[http://www.wilderssecurity.com/showthread.php?t=167321]("http://www.wilderssecurity.com/showthread.php?t=167321").

Eventually I opened the following ports:
2900-2916 (both TCP and UDP);
8081 (UDP);
9000-9013 (both TCP and UDP);
5015 (both TCP and UDP).

I opened them on both "Port Forwarding" (even if in theory it should not be necessary as it function for incoming connections) and "Application Rules" (as it seems to function for outgoing connection as explained on the page).

Yesterday I done some test with such configuration and ... a the beginning I could not connect (same problem); than I have done an apparently "unnecessary" step: I tried to disconnect and than reconnect the DSL connection so I would change the public IP and ... FINALLY I can login to the game.

So far so good? Well ... after few minutes I started to have huge lag problem: basically I could not interact with any NPC (*"NPC is already in use"* or something like that, I forgot to write it down) and any mobs shows an empty health bar (and if I attempted to attack it, nothing would happen) - if I attempted to log off, I would get to the character screen, but I get a message that says *"waiting to logoff to complete"* (or something like that, even here I forgot to write down).

The strange thing was that if I closed the game (normally - no kill necessary) and than log back in, I started to have again the original problem (*"Searching for logon server...."* up to 20/20) and the only way to solve it was to change again the public IP. After several attempt I noticed something strange: the lag spikes kept showing up only when I tried to add some spell to the action bars - if I kept playing without adding new spells to the action bar I could play without problems. Now, it is very strange so maybe it was just a bad coincidence, but I had (I will explain in the next paragraph why I am using past tense here) a theory: when I make a change to the action bars the client should sync it with my server profile, but for some unknown bug (who know if it was Wine, LOTRO client, my Linux network config, or the router) during the sync the connection to the server stall (explaining the huge lag and the *"waiting to logoff to complete"* error message at the character screen), but for some reason the server does not close it until I try to log back in with a different IP (because probably then the server would forcefully terminate the previous connection - I know that the World of Warcraft serves do that).

Now, why I used the past tense on my theory? Because this evening I have been able to play for about 1.5 hrs without problems, even by changing my action bars. 8-[

After this post I will going to play a little bit more and I will see whatever I had bad luck yesterday (with the lag problems) or good luck early this evening (with no problems).

A side note (about side problems that I could tolerate on the short-middle terms): I occasionally get some graphic artiphacts:
- sometime the terrain textures at mid-far distance do not render correctly, but instead they are totally black. If I get close, they are re-paint correctly;
- the icons above the npc sometime have the same problem;
- sometime the text above the mobs (their names to clarify) is partially invisible.

Tomorrow (now it is late where I live, so on the short session I have after this post I will just enjoy the game), I will attempt to do some experiment with the graphic settings, but if someone already know the solution it could save me several minutes (i.e. a way to make the game use OpenGL instead of DX?!?!). :p


P.S. thanks a lot for PyLOTRO, it is a bless. :-)

---

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-06

That one is the game itself, it takes 24 seconds (I think), if you choose to drop back to the character screen you still have to wait for the logout to finish before selecting another char or logging back in.

> **superdif said:**
> the only way to solve it was to change again the public IP. After several attempt I noticed something strange: the lag spikes kept showing up only when I tried to add some spell to the action bars - if I kept playing without adding new spells to the action bar I could play without problems. Now, it is very strange so maybe it was just a bad coincidence, but I had (I will explain in the next paragraph why I am using past tense here) a theory: when I make a change to the action bars the client should sync it with my server profile, but for some unknown bug (who know if it was Wine, LOTRO client, my Linux network config, or the router) during the sync the connection to the server stall (explaining the huge lag and the *"waiting to logoff to complete"* error message at the character screen), but for some reason the server does not close it until I try to log back in with a different IP (because probably then the server would forcefully terminate the previous connection - I know that the World of Warcraft serves do that).
Does it do the same via Windows, just to rule out further connection problems?

> **superdif said:**
> A side note (about side problems that I could tolerate on the short-middle terms): I occasionally get some graphic artiphacts:
- sometime the terrain textures at mid-far distance do not render correctly, but instead they are totally black. If I get close, they are re-paint correctly;
- the icons above the npc sometime have the same problem;
That is a known [bug]("http://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=16997")

> - sometime the text above the mobs (their names to clarify) is partially invisible.
Another known problem with a known workaround, there is an option called floating names on top (or something similar), make sure it is ticked (pressing N toggles it as well).

Just for future reference can you post your wine version and what graphics card/drivers you are using.

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-06
**Wine 1.1.23**
Due to a change to the default off-screen rendering method some people may notice that the game screen is slightly darker (stencil shadows seems to be the game option causing the problem).

If you wish to switch back to the previous off-screen rendering method, copy the following into a file and import it using regedit (or enter it manually if you wish)

```
REGEDIT4

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\Direct3D]
"OffscreenRenderingMode"="backbuffer"
```

---

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-09
> **smckenzie said:**
> PS I have never gotten pass this stage, so as you can imagine this is quite irritating, I don't want to give up
The last line holds the answer, you need to get a copy of d3dx9_36.dll (google for it if you don't have a proper windows install), stick it in your game directory (you can stick it in windows/system32 but I tend to place replacement dlls in the app/game directory) and give it another go.

If you've done a bottle-shuffle (ie created a new bottle and moved your LotRO directory over) then it is using the Wine version of d3dx9_36.dll which hasn't got that function working yet, if your LotRO is sraight from install then directx was installed by the installer so that dll is the windows version.

---

### Post by smckenzie on 2009-06-09

"I got everything installed correctly" is clearly not true if it doesn't run :), so please write a detailed description of what you have done. Hopefully we can help you get it to run after that.

Will do, and thanks BTW. I wrote up pretty much a step by step instruction on how I installed or attempted to install Lotro, per your request. So here goes: 



1. Installed wine 1.1.23

2. Configured wine regedit 
HKCU>Software>Wine>Direct3D>VideoMemorySize= 256
(double checked it in the prefix check built into PyLotro) 

3. Changed Windows version to 2K

4. Issued the following commands in terminal: 

> (wget [http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks](http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks)
bash winetricks vcrun2005
bash winetricks vcrun2005sp1)

5. Downloaded and compiled PyLotro

6. Downloaded and installed High Res client for LOTRO via WINE

7. used the Game finder service built into PyLotro to find directory and found it, then patched.

8. entered credentials at the login screen, selected realm "Meneldor", also saw that the server news was showing up correctly. 

9. Clicked "login", screen goes blank the I receive the error that was attached on my previous post.

PS. I am still at work so I don't have access to my terminal at home, once I do i will give you the detailed specs.

### Post by ajackson on 2009-06-11
> **BatPenguin said:**
> That sounds right, you can have it in the game folder or in the system32 folder I think.
My preference is to put it in the game directory, so that if I need to shift the install to a new wine bottle I don't have to remember that I needed the dll but system32 is also a good enough place.

If the error message is still the same (ie complaining about that dll) then try sticking it in the game directory as it doesn't seem to be finding it.

> - the patching: did it take forever and a day (=are you sure it is properly patched)? If not, look up patching problems here, there was some install sources that caused the patching not to work (a missing patchclient.dll or something or other).
Even an unpatched version will get further before displaying an error about not being the right version, so my guess is it is still that dll.

One thing I did notice was the installation of the VC++ run-times via winetricks. I've said it several times now but if you are using the downloaded version (or the MoM retail disks) you **do not** need to do that. If you have a LotRO install that won't start, use the config checker, if it says VC++ isn't installed then it is safe to install it. I think that installing it then running the LotRO installer might have prevented the normal problem installing it gives (as the LotRO install probably overwrote the winetricks version) but it could be causing a problem.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2009-06-12
> **smckenzie said:**
> At the risk of annoying everyone, I should just come out like a noob and ask. Where is the bottle? I am not using CXG I am merely installing this one thing via wine.


Is this the same directory as the one in which your wizard finds LOTRO? ie. **/home/sean/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online**.....is this where I drop the .dll?.....I am sorry for being so confusing, I am honestly trying to take everyones advice.

Yes that's it. Try that and ignore the rest of this if it works ;)

Anyway, presuming it doesn't: 

If you're just using plain wine, your "bottle" is the normal ~/.wine . Bottle is a crossover term for prefixes. See: [http://wiki.jswindle.com/index.php/Wine_Prefixes](http://wiki.jswindle.com/index.php/Wine_Prefixes) . 

Prefixes are for keeping wine setting separate for various wine programs. Using them is a very good idea. If you have installed other wine programs, they may be causing problems for you, so actually (supposing inserting the dll doesn't work, I guess you've tried copying it to various places already) what you should try next is either try using crossover games (I think they have a free trial) or do this with wine to ensure that your problems are not due to a somehow broken wine install (maybe due to the winetricks you did):

- create a fresh wineprefix:  (your username seemed to be sean, I'll use that here)
```

mkdir /home/sean/.wine-lotro
export WINEPREFIX=/home/sean/.wine-lotro/
winecfg
regedit 
```

With Regedit, just do the videocard memory part. DO NOT CLOSE THE TERMINAL DURING ALL OF THIS, the export command only works during the terminal session. This now created a new wine "bottle" called ~/.wine-lotro. You want to move lotro to this one and always use this bottle for running it. Its settings are separate from whatever else wine stuff you've installed.

After this, move (or copy if you've got plenty of hard drive room) your Lotro folder over to the new place. (cp /home/sean/.wine/drive/...whatever.../Codemasters /home/sean/.wine-lotro/blahblahblah/Program Files/ -R 

Then you can try running Lotro, set the prefix to the correct one in Pylotro's settings and see what error message it spits out. My guess is that after this you get an error that you can search from this forum and get the "install vncrun2005+sp1" recommendation, but do it one step at a time, remember to set the prefix in Pylotro and do not run anything as sudo during any of this. Good luck.


EDIT: oops, essed up the path on the copy. You course you want to copy it to the NEW ~/.wine-lotro path, not the old. Sorry, fixed it.

What graphics card & drivers are you using?

I have a Nvidia GeForce 8800GTX with drivers 185.18.14 (downloaded directly from the Nvidia site).

BTW the problem **seems** to be auto*magically* solved with a system reinstall (due to other issues) --- WARNING: I am saying "*seems*" because I had just few minutes to open the game.

Anyway, the problem is indeed related to "Landscape Shadow": in the last few days I noticed that if I set it to "High" I would get the black textures even on the short distance (even below the character), while setting it on "Medium" I would only get it on mid-far distance (and as I was approaching that distance the textures would repaint normally). Today, after re-installing the system, the problem *apparently* disappeared on "Medium" setting (on "High" is still present).

Thank you very much for your help. :-)


P.S. the game is now running fine, but as "icing on the cake" I would like to know if anyone as an answer for two little issues:
- The "Antialiasing" option has no options other than "Disabled"; the overall graphic is still very good, but some texture would still benefit from some "Antialiasing" (see first attachment);
- For "Texture Filtering" I can go up to "Very sharp" because with "Anisotropic" I actually get the opposite result (see second attachment); it is not much of an issues as the rendering is still great, but it cost nothing to check if there is any fix. :biggrin:

Are you sure it's post-processing? If half your screen is black, it could be the same type of shadow problems I think most of us have. Tryu turning off all shadows, stencil, environment etc. and see if it helps (with post-processing on). If it does, increase shadows one nudge at a time.

The black vendor icons I don't think can be fixed yet.

Many thanks again both of you.

No problem, glad we could help you :)

I don't know particularly about this patch, but as a quick tip on how to find info on doing it: go to WineHQ and see "compiling wine yourself". Make sure you install all the dependencies, I think it was just one command that pulls them all, the page there says it. Then download the sources, put the patch file in the same directory, patch the files and compile. I woudln't necessarily do the "make install" part right away though, I'd just keep the wine binary and its directory where it is and test with it (just run it with "/home/user/wherever/wine /path/toyourlotro/..."). For a quick how-to on how to patch the sources, see the Fallout3 appdb page. It requires patching, so that page has instructions that you can probably mimic to do this...I'd imagine the process is the same.

### Post by funghi on 2009-07-08
Firstly, I need to say that I did not run the latest trial installer from the LOTRO website for the reason that when I downloaded it and ran it, it would tell me that I did not have the latest version, and then attempt to redirect me to a page that would not work.  If someone can get me to a version of the vol 2.8 installer, I will give that a shot.

That said, I have the most current version of LOTRO on my virtual machine drive, and I was actually on LotroLinux until after I ran into the freezing problem, except it would happen about 40 min into the game, once while I was attempting Carn Dum.  

I start researching, and learn I should be on PyLOTRO, so my install steps are as follows:

1) Run cabextract, wine tricks etc.
2) Added fakeNET11 to my Wine registry
3) Checked Direct3d key in wine, it was already there - so I didnt bother,
4) Wine was set to Windows 2000 btw per previous instructions for LOTRO Linux.
5) Skipped trial install for reason explained above.
6) Installed pylotro gui via synaptic
7) Made sure LOTRO was properly mapped (it is under ./wine/drive_c/windows/program files/turbine/the lord of the rings online for me)
8) Ran pylotro - got msrvc80.dll error

Got that error at first, did more research, attempted to install with the following mods:

1) Deleted .wine folder, then ran wine to recreate it
2) Set Wine to Windows XP
3) Downloaded cabextract, wine tricks etc using bash -q filename
4) Set Wine to Windows 2000
5) Added direct3d key again.
6) Added fakenet11.reg again
7) Went to VM, made sure game was patched up to date, then proceeded to move files over from lotrofiles, where most updated game data is stored, to the proper wine folder, as mentioned earlier.
8) Mapped the drive for the pylotro client.
9) Ran pylotro - got black screen error
10) Grabbed the dll file indicated in the lorebook entry, and added
11) Start game, it says I dont have most current client data - so I proceed to patch in game. 
12)Ran pylotro again, now getting freeze errors, except now it happens right when I login or a few seconds afterwards

Also, I want to mention that after patching in game, my load screen talks about vol 2.7 instead of vol 2.8 as it did prior to LotroLinux or Wine 1.1.25.

### Post by ajackson on 2009-07-08
> **funghi said:**
> I start researching, and learn I should be on PyLOTRO
LotROLinux still work but I no longer maintain it, PyLotRO is in essence LotROLinux plus a few features but either will work for now.

> so my install steps are as follows:
<snip>
Installation has got a lot easier now that both Turbine and Codemasters have MoM downloader/installers, it is as easy as:

- run lotrosetup.exe or moriasetup.exe (I've seen both but never together)
- Wait while LotRO installs, if it displays an error while installing .NET you can ignore it
- use winecfg to switch wine to win2000 (it doesn't seem to affect the install being in winxp mode)
- Get/install PyLotRO
- Point PyLotRO at game directory
- Fire patching off
- (optional) read the LotR novel to kill time while patcher works away (not that I'm saying the patch process is slow or anything :))
- Login and go do whatever you want to do

> Also, I want to mention that after patching in game, my load screen talks about vol 2.7 instead of vol 2.8 as it did prior to LotroLinux or Wine 1.1.25.
The 2.8 means that they have released volume 2 book 8 between you last running and patching.

### Post by ajackson on 2009-07-08
> **funghi said:**
> I had the vol 2.8 load screen - whereas after patching via pylotro I get vol 2.7 load screen.
Ah I think I understand now, if you mean the screen that appears after the logo swish and movie (if the movie gets shown) then that's just an ad window and doesn't affect much. However because Linux is a case sensitive file system and Windows isn't it could be that you have two files in there with in essence the same file name but one having capitals or something similar and the wrong one is being shown. As I said though it's just a splash screen to give you something to look at so it won't matter too much if the wrong one shows. Of course they could have just sent the old one down in the patch, they've done odd things like that in the past.

> I am actually in the process of regression testing while researching how to install 1.1.24 (still a bit of a Linux/Ubuntu noob, even after 1.5 years....)
If you start up synaptic, do a search for wine and then select the wine package so that it is highlighted. Select package -> force version (or press ctrl-E) and you should be able to select 1.1.24

---

### Post by wylfing on 2009-07-16
> If you start up synaptic, do a search for wine and then select the wine package so that it is highlighted. Select package -> force version (or press ctrl-E) and you should be able to select 1.1.24

As an FYI, this doesn't appear to be the case. On both the Hardy and Jaunty machines I have here, force version only permits choosing between 1.1.25 and whatever is in the official Ubuntu repository (which for Hardy isn't even 1.0).

To get 1.1.24 back, I have to uninstall 1.1.25 and manually install 1.1.24 from the [winehq.org archive]("http://wine.budgetdedicated.com/archive/index.html").

Actual i have just two little problems:

This is ok for me, no need to switch to another desktop. Usually, but ... :)

When i turn compiz off (using compiz-switch), i can use ctrl+alt+arrow to change workspaces and go back to the game then, without any problem. BUT: I cant use the "buy amount" feature. When i press the left alt key, the buy button changes to "buy amount", but i cant click the button to bring up the menu where i can change the amount.

So i have deactivated the "windowmanager controls window" option in winecfg (graphic tab). Now i can use the buy amount option without problems, but when i switch to another desktop the game minimizes. When i go back to the game then, it runs fine but the sound is gone (without compiz)

How can i stop the game (i play  in fullscreen) from minimizing when i switch to another workspace or, another option, how can i get back sound?

Im using ubuntu jaunty jackalope, and wine 1.0.1-0. I used to kill pulseaudio with "pulseaudio --kill" in the console before i start the game, because with Pulseaudio in crashes after a few minutes. Maybe this is the reason why the sound is gone after maximizing the game.

Cal

### Post by ajackson on 2009-07-31
> **Calredon said:**
> Im using ubuntu jaunty jackalope, and wine 1.0.1-0.
Before trying anything else update to the latest version of wine [http://www.winehq.org](http://www.winehq.org) and see if that alters anything.

Second thing to try is setting the sound, via winecfg, to OSS. You'll lose the sound in the cut scenes but it seems more resiliant than ALSA is when it comes to desktop jumping.

When i play lotro with compiz/effects enabled, i cant alt+tab out or use ctrl+alt+arrow to switch workspaces (i know about this bug), but the game itself is working fine, especially the new "buy amount" feature at the vendor. This is ok for me, no need to switch to another desktop. Usually, but ... :)

When i turn compiz off (using compiz-switch), i can use ctrl+alt+arrow to change workspaces and go back to the game then, without any problem. BUT: I cant use the "buy amount" feature. When i press the left alt key, the buy button changes to "buy amount", but i cant click the button to bring up the menu where i can change the amount.


Problem solved: I changed the key for moving windows in ubuntu -> system -> preferences -> windows from alt to super, now the alt key is working as designed. Its the same problem people have with photoshops clone tool. It also works with the alt key ...

### Post by g0bez on 2009-08-02
After all i've been going through, i figured I'd add my experience to give a current update.

proc AMD64
ram 6gb
vid Nvidia GeForce 9600GT (driver v.180.44)
HDD 160 (ubuntu 9.04); 500gb (NAS backups); 1tb (NAS Media storage)
wine v1.1.26
game LotRO - MoM (dvd install)

I started (stupidly) at the first thread I found with instructions on how to run/install LotRO with wine. (namely [http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X](http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X) ) Following those steps to the letter, I kept getting errors (esp. when installing winetricks, and then trying to run LotRO). I've spent hours trying to get this to work... granted, much of that time has included several installs of the game from DVD.

After some more searching, I read that MoM dvd would take care of the vc++ install... which, as I've discovered, it didn't. But, it did lead me to what I hope is the correct way to install.

Here are the steps I took:

<erase all previous .wine bottles>
>>> rm -rvf ~/.wine/

<create a new .wine>
>>> wineboot

<install LotRO: MoM>
>>> Open the dvd --> run the installer --> choose the correct install path
>>>>>> ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/The Lord of the Rings Online/
>>> *my installer hung for 90 minutes when it reached .Net 
>>> *I killed the installer (terminal: lsof |grep setup   ... find the process ID and run: kill processID

<install PyLotRO>
>>> follow the instructions here:
>>> [http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php](http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php)
>>>>>>>>*add the repo and run the installer

<<<<<>> optional time-saver <<>>>>>>
    If you have the disk space, make a copy of ~/.wine/ -- that way if anything gets borked along the way, you have a backup of a fresh wine + MoM install. cp ~/.wine/ ~/.wine-lotro.bak/
<<<<<<<<>><<>>>>>>>>

<install vc++ redists>
[http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks?highlight=(wine)|(tricks](http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks?highlight=(wine)|(tricks))
>>> *wget -nv [http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks](http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks)
>>> *chmod 755 winetricks
>>> *winetricks vcrun2005
>>> *winetricks vcrun2005sp1
>>>>>>>> these should run without major errors -- see the following output:

```
jwinters@hp:~$ ./winetricks vcrun2005
Executing wget -O vcredist_x86.exe -nd -c --read-timeout=300 --retry-connrefused --header Accept-Encoding: gzip,deflate http://download.microsoft.com/download/d/3/4/d342efa6-3266-4157-a2ec-5174867be706/vcredist_x86.exe

<Run PyLotRO>

>>>>>>> if you've done everything correctly, this part should take a while to get/apply all the patches


Once the patch is applied, you should have a fully functional LotRO client running in Linux!

I'll update if anything breaks as I get into it some more.

### Post by mabdk on 2009-08-22
Hi all,

thought i might share my experience with installing LotRO on Ubuntu 9.04 x64

I installed wine
then i installed lotro via the MoM dvd's
My installation also hang at the .net installation, but i didn't wait 90 mins though just killed it after 10 mins. :)

Then installed PyLotR, ran pylotr setup game folder etc. did the patching. The patcher only updated the client and turbine launcher though, no graphics or anything was updated.

Then after installing Eve-Online and following these step, i tried to see if that had helped on LotRO, and miracuesly i worked now, pathing worked and game launched.

wget [http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks](http://www.kegel.com/wine/winetricks)
sh winetricks corefonts

sh winetricks vcrun2005sp1

i'm running wine 1.1.27

Also i had to download the d3dx9_36.dll from [www.dll-files.com](www.dll-files.com) and copy it to the systems32 folder in wine.

i have to say i'm a completely Linux newbie, but above did the trick for me, and i even don't have to set it to win2k i can run it with wine set to winxp

### Post by Posh on 2009-08-24
I thought I would post what I found with anti-aliasing for those who are trying to get it to work.  Hopefully this is new information as I couldn't find it anywhere in this thread.

It can be enabled but it wasn't obvious.

Here is what I'm using.. Sorry ATI users I'm going to be of no assistance.
Nvidia 185 series drivers with Wine 1.1.28.

1. Make sure you have a key in your registry to enable multisampling in wine.

The entry should be in HKCU/Software/Wine/Direct3D/Multisampling
You should have this key with a value of "enabled".

2. Open the nvidia-settings application.  Set anti-aliasing to Override Application and then set it to the AA you would like to use.  Do no close the application but leave it open.

3. Start up LOTRO and go to options.  Set Anti-aliasing to 2x. 

4. Enjoy LOTRO with antialiasing.

I'm not sure if changing the setting in LOTRO between 2x and 4x will change anything.  But feel free to experiment.  I haven't used this much yet.  And just now experimenting with it myself.  

Hopefully this will help out for those trying to get this to work.

Feel free to post back here any additional findings on this.

---

### Post by wylfing on 2009-08-25
I have been experimenting with this method of antialiasing too. I held off posting because there are a range of problems that I have been trying to pin down.

First off, I will confirm that this method does work. However...

Multisampling is disabled by default in wine because it can lead to conflicts with fbo offscreen rendering. In my case, this was definitely the case -- with multisampling enabled, I got nothing but black screen in the game (although curiously the character selection screen worked fine, which is how I knew AA was working in the first place). 

The 'workaround' is to switch the rendering method. Both pbuffer and backbuffer work. Switching to either one restored in-game graphics, complete with nice antialiasing. There was a downside, though: the framerate dropped horribly, down to about 12 FPS.

This was with 180-series drivers and wine 1.1.27, so things may be better upstream (i.e., no more fbo conflicts). I also have an 8600 GT, which may not be the greatest card, I don't even know anymore. I am still planning on getting everything up to the latest & greatest and trying again to see what I can get away with.

The game sure does look a lot better with AA enabled, that's for certain.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2009-09-13
> **YuccaNel said:**
> Hi folks and a big TY to all of you....
I must say an excellent well written guide is provided here and I managed to get LOTRO up and running first time....

I have an issue though now, I mucked up my system beyond repair and had to reinstall ubuntu 9.04 and I hope what was a good move will save me an issue of downloading patches... I made a copy of my previous .wine folder for LOTRO and would like to know how I can use this backup to reinstall the game... copying the folder and pasting is not good enough as I need to reinstall the wine symbolic links and possibly the VC run.

Would it be possible to create a new.wine and winecfg it and then just copy-paste the game folder in there?

The way I've done it a few times (when reinstalling my machine or installing it to a laptop etc.) is to make a backup of the patched .wine/drive_c/Program Files/Codemasters directory, install the game normally from DVD to the new machine and then copy the backed up folder over the freshly installed Codemasters folder. So basically just install normally, but instead of patching, copy your old directory over the unpatched one. That at least should work fine but of course you could just try installing wine and then copying the whole .wine directory over it and see if it works even without a reinstall from DVD. If it's the same machine and same wine version etc. I don't know, maybe it'd work. I probably would at least give it a try.

Remember to backup your ~/The Lord of the Rings Online/UserPreferences.ini as well, if you've done something creative with your settings.

---

### Post by ipx on 2009-10-07
I thought i'd share with you how I fix the alt+tab problem in LotRO through wine. This is a 'fix' that I also use successfully for warcraft3 and it's alt+tab issues. Actually, it seems to be usable in all fullscreen-applications running in wine that has a problem with alt+tab.

When I launch the game, I simply launch it with this command:
```
env WINEDEBUG=-all wine explorer /desktop=0,1680x1050 <CLIENT.exe>
```
Note: You have to change the resolution and the client-exe

To implement this in lotro, I have to edit the lotrolauncher.script and manually add this to the execution-command. Do this by replacing
```
wine ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} $@

```
with
```
env WINEDEBUG=-all wine explorer /desktop=0,1680x1050  ${gameClient_FILE} ${gameClient_ARGS} $@

```

Now, your game should be run into a virtual desktop in your desired resolution in fullscreen, and alt+tabbing should work as a charm.

---

I could never get the mic to work with the onboard sound (see sig below) but with two PCI cards I can get my head set working on one and the game sounds working on the other following [this]("http://www.wowwiki.com/Wine/Misc#In-Game_Voice_Chat_with_USB_Headsets").

If that helps.

---

### Post by wylfing on 2009-11-11
I don't have any experience with multiple PCI sound cards. However, I have an all-in-one USB Logitech webcam with microphone, and the linked instructions work for me for getting that to work with LoTRO voice chat. I replaced the label 'headset' with 'camera' for clarity, but otherwise followed those instructions.

For me, the options did not show up quite the same way in winecfg. All I got was "USB Audio" for the camera's audio input. Easy enough to see what is happening, though.

After setting up ALSA, in LoTRO I went to Options > Audio, scrolled to the bottom and changed dsnoop to USB Audio. Works!

---

### Post by wylfing on 2009-11-11
Graphics testing update: nVidia driver 190.42, wine 1.1.32, 8600 GT card.

1. Enabling multisampling (i.e., anti-aliasing) still produces a black screen. No joy there.

2. Changing the blur filter to High dramatically reduces the flickering from night-time lights for me, with no noticeable drop in performance. I think this is a much better solution than disabling glow mapping entirely, because glow mapping adds a lot of visual appeal to the game.

3. The icons above NPC heads appear to be rendering fine. Hooray!

---

You're using a very old wine version. Go here: 

[http://www.winehq.org/download/deb](http://www.winehq.org/download/deb)

Enable the wine repository, install latest wine and try again. I haven't played in a while so I don't actually know what issues there might be at the moment, but using latest wine is always a good place to start.

### Post by BatPenguin on 2009-11-28
> **Renzo-M said:**
> Thanks, apparently this was the problem, updating wine solved the problem.

Renzo-M

Edit: A problem I'm facing now is that all of a suddon my mouse cursor changes into a big black box ... any idea how this happens?

Also, I'm new to using wine so if the following question is a stupid one, I'm sorry...
- When I'm listening to music (Rythmbox) and I open LOTRO, I don't have any sound in LOTRO. How do I get both my wine to produce sound, and the rest of my ubuntu (using Karmic btw)

Hmm, mouse cursor I don't know, you might want to see if some of the graphics options might help. Things like turning down shadows affect these, so try by turning down all shadows and some other graphics options, restart the game and see if the problem is solved. If it is, start turning up graphics a bit at a time and see how far up you can go without the problem coming back. I had these sorts of issues before, half the screen black etc, and this is how I've solved them. Anistrophic filtering at least used to bring up problems if set too high, try that too. It could of course also be a known problem with Lotro+current wine,I haven't really been keeping up. Hopefully I'll get some time to play again during Christmas, now I don't even have my account active anymore.

Sound: Lotro/wine is picky about sound, I don't think you can easily get Pulseaudio to work properly and to get sound from multiple sources at once. If you can alt+tab out of the game without it crashing, try starting Lotro first and then your music player, but I don't think you'll have much luck,sorry. At least I havent, perhaps somebody else can suggest a solution if they have one. I consider it a major victory to even GET sound in Lotro...wasn't exactly simple for me to get that far :D

Edit: Do some googling about "wine alsa" and "wine pulseaudio" to see how support is coming along. The wine people at least used to be pretty hostile to pulseaudio so you might pretty easily find descriptions of why wine doesn't work with Ubuntu default sound system so well and why they might not be supporting it anytime soon either...again, I haven't been keeping up. But Wine people didn't use to like Pulse at all, a year or so ago.

### Post by SNy on 2009-12-04
Hey all,

short update about the Siege of Mirkwood expansion:
It works.

Update went smooth, installed prepatch, then ran my script and let it patch. Downloaded the new client and a few other files and then applied 3850 forward iterations (the prepatch).

Started and played normally. Crashed a few times, so it might have introduced new instabilities. Time will tell.

SNy

PS. Oh, the tip with the desktop setting was pretty neat, using this now and the strange alt-tab behaviour is gone. Thanks!

### Post by wylfing on 2010-01-05
**@Rokit_Armor** - I agree with huwbie that your problem seems to be a video card driver issue. I am not familiar with your hardware, but you might try choosing System > Administration > Hardware Drivers from the desktop menu and seeing if it offers you a proprietary video card driver.

UPDATE: After some searching it looks like the Ubuntu installer does not always pick the 'intel' driver for the X3100 chipset (which drives the video for the Inspiron 1525). It sometimes picks 'vesa' for whatever reason. You can confirm this by opening a terminal and running this command:
```
grep Driver /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

You should get a few lines printed out as a result, one of which should be [FONT="Courier New"][COLOR="DarkSlateGray"]Driver "intel"[/COLOR][/FONT]. If you don't see that, but instead see, for example, [FONT="Courier New"][COLOR="DarkSlateGray"]Driver "vesa"[/COLOR][/FONT] then we will know what the problem is.

**@huwbie** - It's not unusual to get errors in the Pylotro log window. Wine spews out a lot of debugging messages, and Pylotro just passes them right on through. It's nothing to worry about if things are working OK.

---

    --voicenetdelay : <int>: Specifies the voice network delay threshold in milliseconds.
    --voiceoff      : Disables the Voice chat system.

    --wfilelog      : <64-bitmask> : activates file logging for the specified weenie event types. Alternately, logtype enums seperated by ',' are enummapped and or'ed together.
    --wprintlog     : <64-bitmask> : activates print logging for the specified weenie event types. Alternately, logtype enums seperated by ',' are enummapped and or'ed together.

*** Finished ***[/INDENT]

### Post by wylfing on 2010-02-19
You may have 2 issues. First, I don't think fakeNET is needed anymore. Using it might actually mess things up. Maybe someone else can confirm/deny that, it's been a while since I installed. Second, you do need to use a full installer. Working installers have been linked to in this thread, I believe.

EDIT: This is the [installer link]("http://www.ausgamers.com/files/details/html/40665") I was thinking of. That's a Mines of Moria installer, so you'll be in for some good long patching time. A more up-to-date full installer might also work but I have no data.

You should try a fresh .wine (assuming you have nothing else in there except LotRO) and simply running the installer without running winetricks or anything. Only run winetricks later if things don't work "out of the box" with the installer.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-02-19
> **wylfing said:**
> You may have 2 issues. First, I don't think fakeNET is needed anymore. Using it might actually mess things up. Maybe someone else can confirm/deny that, it's been a while since I installed.
It isn't really needed any more as the current wine version installs .NET 1.1 without any errors (or did the last time I tried it) but using fakeNET won't break anything until wine is in a position to correctly run the real launcher (if that time ever arrives).

> **wylfing said:**
> You should try a fresh .wine (assuming you have nothing else in there except LotRO) and simply running the installer without running winetricks or anything. Only run winetricks later if things don't work "out of the box" with the installer.

This is the key, try with a clean wine prefix, don't do anything other than run the installer, if you then have problems you can start trying things such as winetricks but the most likely fixes needed first is dropping one of the directx dlls (I think it's d3dx9_36.dll, it's appears in this thread a few times) into the game directory and telling wine how much video memory you have.

Only when that starts to fail should you worry about manually adding the Visual C++ 2005 run-times.

```

Make sure you have downloaded one of the full installers, and not the "download manager" one.

### Post by wylfing on 2010-04-29
There's something about the Lucid environment that it doesn't like, and I haven't figured out what it is yet. It was working fine yesterday before I upgraded :P

I'll just do data collection until something turns up: (1) I tried wine 1.1.43 and 1.1.38 with the same results. (2) I am running the 195.36 series drivers from nvidia.com, although it looks like my particular build might be a few weeks out of date. (3) glxgears and glxinfo show what I expect them to show.

I am skeptical that driver versions and wine versions have anything to do with it, but I may monkey around with these to see what happens. I will also try totally disabling sound and see what happens there too.

EDIT: Hm, well, the short story is that I have it running again. I will sort out what I did and modify my post a bit later today (I have a bunch of meetings to attend right now, cursed work!).

EDIT: My current belief is that the problem is another conflict between wine's ALSA and Ubuntu's pulseaudio. It appears to be possible to go straight to pulseaudio in LotRO, and this allows the game to run again. Here is what restored me to a working setup after upgrading to Lucid:

1. I downgraded wine to 1.1.38. This is probably unnecessary. I downgraded during troubleshooting and have yet to upgrade again. Will test as time permits.

2. I regenerated a clean .wine directory. This is also probably unnecessary, but again it's something I did during troubleshooting. To do this:
a. Move your Turbine folder out of ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/ to some safe spot (like your desktop). Then delete ~/.wine. (WARNING: I am assuming you are have LotRO installed in .wine and that you have nothing else in there worth preserving. If this is not the case, deleting .wine may not be a smart thing to do.)
b. From a terminal run winecfg. This will automatically create a new .wine directory.
c. Use winetricks to install vcrun2005 and d3dx9_36.
d. Put the Turbine folder back in ~/.wine/drive_c/Program Files.

3. Use winecfg to **deselect all audio output options**. Normally you'd have, for example, ALSA or OSS selected as your audio output choice. But you want to clear all of the checkboxes under Audio.

4. Get rid of your LotRO settings by moving ~/The Lord of the Rings folder somewhere else.

5. Start the game.

You'll have to recreate all your preferences, but this is the cleanest way to proceed. In the game, under Audio Options, you'll see that the audio system selected is Pulseaudio.

I still need to test things to make sure I can (e.g.) make Skype calls while playing LotRO.

### Post by ajackson on 2010-05-10
> **Calredon said:**
> Now i have Gamesound, but no Sound in the movies- afaik a known problem, i played the last months without sound in the cutscenes - i dont need to, because my wife was also playing with an older ubuntu 9.04, so she has sound ingame AND in the movies. I could hear the sound on her machine.

But now were both on lucid, so is there a fix to have sound in the cutscenes to?
It could be how you have sound configured in winecfg. In Karmic and earlier releases if you set sound to ALSA you might have had problems with pulseaudio crashing, killing pulseaudio did solve that. If you had sound set to OSS you would get in game sounds and only cut scene sounds if you were very lucky (very much sound chip dependant).

Under Lucid I now get both in game and cut scene sound while using ALSA without having to kill pulseaudio. Every now and then I'll lose both sounds and I suspect it is pulse crashing but it is a pretty rare occurance.

### Post by wylfing on 2010-05-10
@peterofferman Why are you compiling pylotro from source? I have never needed to do that.

Also I think ajackson's comment is meaningful, i.e., you don't seem to have followed the instructions in the howto. For starters, LotRO is probably not going to coexist peacefully in the same wine prefix as WoW. I recommend starting over, keeping the following in mind:
[LIST]
[*]LotRO needs to be in its own wine "bottle". (Learn about this if you need to. It's not very hard to learn and it is very, very useful.) Make sure you create a nice clean bottle for LotRO to live in all by itself.
[*]Not every installer works for LotRO. Make sure you are using a "full" installer, not a "net" installer (that is, one of those that only downloads a tiny program which in turn downloads all the rest of the game -- these don't work for us).
[*]Don't make things more complicated than they need to be, such as compiling programs from source. That's too hard!
[/LIST]

Try again and report back if you have problems. Don't give up!

### Post by wylfing on 2010-05-12
> **peterofferman said:**

Yeah, you may well end up needing to experiment with several different installers. Only some of them work -- the rest are going to end up giving you errors down the road (e.g., 205). You should generally try to find the most recent "full" installer you can find. Besides, you don't really want to start with Angmar and patch all the way up to Mirkwood, that would take forever.

EDIT: @Calredon - Confirmed that esound in winecfg and pulseaudio in Lotro works. Sound quality seems the same.

### Post by wylfing on 2010-05-13
> **peterofferman said:**
> ok crap. when I start the game, it loads up a black screen, then crashes back down to the desktop, with a severely lower resolution. any ideas?

EDIT: I just found out it's common on lucid. meh

Just an FYI, it's better to edit your existing post than to double-post, unless you're changing the subject.

Anyhow - the black-screen-then-crash is the audio problem we've been talking about. Remove your UserPreferences.ini, clear all the audio checkboxes in winecfg, and launch the game. You won't get cutscene sounds but you'll get a game launch, and it will choose Pulseaudio as the audio device in the game. Then quit, re-run winecfg and select the esound checkbox. After that you should get cutscene sounds in the game as well.

It's still not perfect though. About 50% of the time that I exit the game, pulse seems to not release its hold on wine, and so the next time I run the game I don't get any sound at all. This happens whether winecfg is set to no options or to esound. Haven't figured out what the right solution to this is yet.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-05-13
> **wylfing said:**
> It's still not perfect though. About 50% of the time that I exit the game, pulse seems to not release its hold on wine, and so the next time I run the game I don't get any sound at all. This happens whether winecfg is set to no options or to esound. Haven't figured out what the right solution to this is yet.
I think the problem is very much audio card specific as all the way through alpha/beta testing Lucid the only times I had issues were when nothing in wine was working due to a nice bit of breakage (even poor old winecfg wouldn't start).

I've not really encountered wine not letting go of the audio but have you tried making a linux app make a noise (play a song or something), that might force the lock loose or at least crash pulse for it to restart again.

@peterofferman
Amazing what results you can achieve by following the how-to isn't it?

### Post by Ms_Angel_D on 2010-05-24
I don't know if this will help anyone but I wanted to share how I have gotten both LOTRO & DDO working on my Kubuntu 10.04 install.


I used the regular download manager from turbine in Virtualbox and downloaded both LOTRO & DDO.

I copied 
```
C:\Program Files\Turbine\The Lord of the Rings Online 
```

and 

```
C:\Program Files\Turbine\Dungeons and Dragons Online - Eberron Unlimited
```

Both to 
```
/home/angel/.wine/drive_c/Program Files/Turbine/
```
Note: I had to create the Turbine folder myself.

using [winetricks]("http://wiki.winehq.org/winetricks") I installed:
[LIST]
[*]win2k
[*]gecko    
[*]vcrun2005    
[*]vcrun2005sp1
[/LIST]

I then downloaded and installed [pylotro]("http://www.lotrolinux.com/download.php") in wine

Once I ran pylotro I had to go into the settings and point it to my LOTRO and DDO installs. While I wasn't able to patch DDO I can play it just fine. With LOTRO I was able to patch and everything runs just fine.

Note You'll have 2 folders(1 if you only play LOTRO) in your /home directory

Dungeons and Dragons Online 

and 

The Lord of the Rings Online

These would generally be the folders that would appear in your Documents folder in windows.This folder will normally hold your UserPreferences.ini file along with your lotro.keymap file.

For reference [here's]("http://ubuntu.pastebin.com/Ad6PHCCv") a copy my UserPreferences.ini file

I haven't had any sound issues and my game seems to run fine. Of course I don't use pulseaudio being on kubuntu instead of Ubuntu. One thing I did notice is that settings aren't saved to the ini file so if you say adjust your sound one session then log and come back later your need to readjust them. I worked around this but just editing the volume settings in the actual UserPreferences.ini file instead of using the in game options.

I hope this helps somebody.

Angel

### Post by wylfing on 2010-06-15
A question about pylotro. Under options, in the Wine Program field, what kinds of alternatives can be put here? I am wondering:
[LIST]
[*]Can additional arguments be inserted, specifically padsp or pasuspend. For example, 'padsp wine' instead of just 'wine'.
[*]If not (as I suspect), then can this functionality be allowed, so that people have a way of modifying the actual wine launch?
[/LIST]

This would allow some variation in how sound is handled, so that either LotRO can be 'tricked' into using OSS or so that pulseaudio can be suspended gracefully for LotRO.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-06-15
> **wylfing said:**
> A question about pylotro. Under options, in the Wine Program field, what kinds of alternatives can be put here? I am wondering:
It lets you specify the full path to the wine command, if for example it isn't normally picked up.

> **wylfing said:**
> 
[LIST]
[*]Can additional arguments be inserted, specifically padsp or pasuspend. For example, 'padsp wine' instead of just 'wine'.
[*]If not (as I suspect), then can this functionality be allowed, so that people have a way of modifying the actual wine launch?
[/LIST]
No and yes but not by me :)

You can create a simple script file containing
```
padsp wine $@
```
and put that filename into the command box, it should work (haven't tried it). Under Lucid, for me anyway, wine and pulseaudio seem to get along just fine.

### Post by Ms_Angel_D on 2010-08-16
> **Hogmeister said:**
> should i install lotro from dvd's or should i somehow find a way to get the trial download going? i'm confused why i can't use my dvd's to be completely honest but if for whatever reason it results in my problems then so be it i'll find someone to get it for me.

You can try installing it in virtualbox with your cd's (or if you have a windows partition that's even better) and starting it and logging in so that it will download any patches or changes from the server. 

Then copy the install folder from your windows partition or the virtulbox install.
```
C:\Program Files\Turbine\The Lord of the rings online\
```

with my most recent install a few days ago I did not need to patch using pylotro. I just ran the game and it worked fine.


For the record I'm not sure what will happen once the game goes to launch in virtualbox as the 3D drivers may cause problems.

good luck!

### Post by articanos on 2010-08-23
Ok, great news for all Intel graphics users, there is a workaround for the game error 129 hardware texture compression was not detected. After days of banging my head against a wall I found a solution! Go into your administration => software sources => check multiverse. Now go back into administration => synaptic package manager, once there type in Driconf. Check it and allow it to install. Next open a terminal and type Driconf, a nice gui will appear. Click on the tab labeled image quality, ensure "Enable S3TC texture compression even if software support is not available" is clicked to yes. Now close the program and enjoy all the goodness that is Lord of the Rings Online whether intel wants you to or not!:guitar:

---

### Post by schtufbox on 2010-08-26
Okay, I fixed it.  For anyone else using a non Ubuntu distro (or maybe even a *buntu one with a messed up pulse install?)  and trying to get LOTRO to work and finding that pylotro exits without properly loading the game or even creating configs I will post my fix here.
It may be elsewhere in this thread but I am not going back through all these pages to check sorry!

In my case the issue was actually Pulseaudio.  I am guessing PCLinuxOS has a different version to Ubuntu. On a whim I temporarilly halted Pulseaudio and LOTRO magically sprung to life!  That would explain why it worked before as PCLinuxOS doesn't have pulseaudio enabled by default :D

Anyway to get it working with pulse I followed some old instructions on the web. In a nutshell run:
```
padsp winecfg
```
Then in the audio tab sellect the OSS driver only.
Then run pylotro like this:
```
padsp pylotro
```

Lotro should then work fine.  Well fine except for no music in the starting titles but the ingame sound works fine :)
If you are not using the native pylotro I would assume the command would be:
```
padsp wine pylotro.exe
```

Or you can install the pulseaudio esound compatibility package and set wine to use esound, that works too, and the cutscene sound works as well, though my mic didn't work for the ingame voice.

Hope this helps someone :)

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-08-27
You should be able to do all that without the padsp part, the OSS sound option should be there, I think all padsp does is create an extra layer of conversion for the sound so that it eventually does end up going through pulse (not 100% certain on that though).

Go back far enough in this thread (haven't got a clue how far back you need to go) and I did post that some crashes were being caused by pulse and wine falling out. Luckily pulse in Ubuntu seems to happily co-exist with wine due to changes on both sides, other distros and older Ubuntus are lagging it seems.

### Post by ajackson on 2010-09-09
Until F2P goes live in the EU zone I can't do any checking but msvcr71.dll is part of the Visual C++ 2003 runtime. You should have Visual C++ 2005 runtime installed already but not the 2003 one (the file is also supplied by .NET 1.1).

Try using winetricks to install vcrun2003. That might work.

Otherwise look in the backup directory for patchclient.dll, rename it something else (lotropatch.dll) copy that to the game directory. Fire up PyLotRO and in the options page change the patchclient.dll to point to the dll you just copied. That should force it to use the old version which may still patch the game.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-09-10
> **thom_raindog said:**
> Looking at how pylotro SEEMS to work (to an illiterate in coding) it would appear possible to "just" change the gamecode from LOTROEU to (probably?) LOTROUS and put in the right servers (no idea what IP that would be).

What that do the trick? Could someone with a US install send me their files from the .LotROLinux folder to lotro [at] raindog dot de?
You could just about do it by getting a copy of the US version of TurbineLauncher.exe.config **but** when you patched it might just corrupt your entire setup (ie not download some stuff but download others and leave the thing a mess that won't work either way).

The safe way of doing it is downloading the US client and installing that as the test version, PyLotRO lets you have two installs one is called test because I was lacking imagination when I named it :) That way you can keep both until you are happy with your fresh start over in the US (you can't transfer your existing characters).

### Post by Reardon on 2010-09-10
> **Hogmeister said:**

### Post by thom_raindog on 2010-09-14
Alright, where's the embarrassed smiley? Ah, here: :oops:

Reading, that's what you do when you have a problem, right? Well, at last I managed to get it after reading the latest appdb entry. 
Case someone stumbles over this, what you need is:
```

./winetricks vcrun2003
***./winetricks d3dx9*

```

Works flawlessly right now, so one more time: Thanks ajackson and all the folks who made LotrO possible on linux. You ROCK!

---

### Post by xBiocorex on 2010-09-14

EDIT: Problem solved. Received a PM from schtufbox that suggested I set my Wine sound to esound, and that did it. Of all the silly things. ><

### Post by schtufbox on 2010-09-15
> **xBiocorex said:**
> I'm having the ***FINISHED*** crash as well, but the above suggestion didn't fix it.

I'm running PyLOTRO 0.1.13 on Wine 1.3.2, Ubuntu 10.4 Lucid Lynx, I've got C++ 2008, d3dx9 (and the other two versions), vcrun2003, vcrun2008 and d3dx10 using Winetricks.

EDIT: Problem solved. Received a PM from schtufbox that suggested I set my Wine sound to esound, and that did it. Of all the silly things. ><
Was the same issue I had, I am guessing maybe a regression in Wine 1.3.2? I'm no expert though just guessing as having sound set to ALSA worked fine for me on my Ubuntu 10.04 with wine 1.2

Had to use that on PCLinuxOS until they updated ALSA, and as the poster above was having exactly the same issues I had, I thought I would suggest the fix to him and see if it worked. It did, though I suspect the ingame voice won't work, I know mine didn't with using oss or esound-compat.

### Post by shuamu on 2010-09-18
> **ajackson said:**
> At the risk of being called offensive, try reading backwards in this thread. That question has been answered several times (last post on page 232 for example).

You need to use winetricks to install vcrun2003


My apologies. This thread is far too long, and several attempts at searching for my specific problem didn't really come up with anything. The post you were referring to on page 232 reports an entirely different error message, so I didn't make the connection. Sorry about the trouble, but from what I've been able to tell there have been numerous repeats of the same questions because this thread is 236 pages long.

It would be much better if the pylotro threads were split into different issues (one for patching issues, one for graphics issues, one for install issues), so that people would have an easier time find others with the same problem.

On that note, your vcrun2003 fixed the problem. The install wiki ([http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X](http://lorebook.lotro.com/wiki/LOTRO_under_Linux_and_Mac_OS/X)) is outdated, then, since it specifies vcrun2005. 

Sorry for the trouble, and thank you for the help.

### Post by ajackson on 2010-09-19
> **Sierpinski said:**
> I haven't been answered yet, does anyone have any ideas about what is wrong?

It might not help but try adding d3dx9_42.dll to your game directory.

There are still some odd crashes where the causes haven't been found yet and they only seem to affect some (and often with little commonality which makes diagnosis harder).

Edit: Just a thought, do you have compiz enabled (the fancy eye candy window effects)? If so try disabling that see if it makes a difference.

Another thing to try is use winecfg to enable a virtual desktop the same size as your display, that can sometimes help.

---

### Post by SNy on 2010-09-20
Hey, so I had a look at the new F2P client and at starting it with my launcher script.
First thing was that when logging in it told me I had no valid subscription (which was true, I was using one of the new free acounts). So the status of said account is "Expired", the script however only offered "Active" ones. OK, changed, checked, works now.
Second, with a wine setup working for the previous version, I would get a crash right at startup without any diagnostic messages whatsoever. winetricks d3dx9 fixed that, rest of my wine setup was kept unchanged.
Third. I can actually start both game instances (paid EU account and F2P US account) on my box just fine, even without any real performance hit.

Fourth. Yeah, have fun. :)
SNy

---

### Post by isotope171 on 2010-09-23
Ok, it has taken me a while to get running, but with the help of this thread I've done it at last. Thank you for pylotro and all your help and support Alan.

I'm on Ubuntu 10.04
Ran the new f2p pando installer in an xp virtual machine. Copied those files over to Ubi, ran the installer with wine. No problems. I attempted to patch with pylotro, and returned the same problem as stated earlier. Installed vcrun2003 via winetricks. Patched fine. Started the game, but got the texture compression error. Used driconf to sort that out. Tried to start the game and got an audio connection error.Opened up the wine configuration gui, and unchecked all audio drivers. No intro sound, but in game sound works fine.
Runs like a dream so far.:popcorn:

### Post by BatPenguin on 2010-10-11
> **TempleNav said:**
> I dunno if that is supposed to be there or not... Also my specs are Radeon 3870 512 mb card, Windsor AMD 3.0 ghz processor with 2 Gb of ram.

You have an ATI card, that could be the problem right there. They can be a big pain in Linux. I admit that I haven't been following the ATI drivers at all lately, but it used to be a miracle even to get the game to start with those. I used to have a laptop with an ATI card (well, still do...my daughter uses it for playing Flash Dora game now =), and it wasn't easy at all getting it to work. So it could simply be an issue with the Radeon's drivers. Have you made sure you have the latest ones installed? Are desktop effects off etc, I'd try everything like that, maybe set the memory to lower than what your card has as a test, disable various graphics in the game, try that sort of things to see if it would help. Maybe googling for your display card and its performance in other Wine games might give you some hints on whether it's the cause of the problems.

Unfortunately Nvidia is (or at least used to be, unless something dramatic has happened) much much better supported in Linux.

I know this doesn't help much but just wanted to mention the ATI card being a quite likely cause of your problems. Good luck.

### Post by wylfing on 2010-10-13
Got some beautiful results presently with the latest wine and the just-released nVidia 260.19.12 drivers on a Geforce 250 GTS. 

Antialiasing works! My regedit setup is under \\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\Direct3D I have these set:
[LIST]
[*]Multisampling enabled
[*]DirectDrawRenderer opengl
[*]VideoMemorySize 512
[/LIST]

Adjust VideoMemorySize to match your rig. I tried a lot of different settings in nvidia-settings for FSAA and Anisotropic Filtering, but they don't seem to have any affect. Upon entering the game, I was able to select 2X or 4X antialiasing. It may be required to restart the game to see it take effect. The problem I kept having with FSAA was that I would get a black screen (except for the floating names and UI). Sometimes this only happened when I looked in certain directions. The problem seems to be an issue with Glow Mapping. I switched Glow Mapping off and the black screen issue went away. Post-processing and high-quality lighting can be left on.

Almost everything else is cranked up as high as it can go and I'm still getting 45+ FPS. The only thing that continues to give me grief is anisotropic texture filtering. If I set textures to Anisotropic I get blockiness (no matter what the filter level), so I have to leave textures at Very High instead.

Overall it looks really incredible. Now if only the wine guys can get DX10 working :P

---

> **slopshid said:**
> So I've gone through this forum as well as the Lorebook installation guide and some other resources, and I've gotten everything installed and patched. However, when I try to login it's still hanging on "Checking account details", using both the standalone and the native client.  If I run the client through the command line, this is the error that occurs:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "PylotRO\build\pyi.win32\pylotro\outPYZ1.pyz/PyLotROLauncher.MainWindow", line 289, in btnLoginClicked
  File "PylotRO\build\pyi.win32\pylotro\outPYZ1.pyz/PyLotROLauncher.MainWindow", line 308, in AuthAccount
AttributeError: AuthenticateUser instance has no attribute 'authSuccess

wine 1.3.3
pylotro 0.1.13
ubuntu 10.10
That's interesting, that might help explain the problem some people have been reporting when a new F2P account has been created.

Can you do me a favour if you are getting that error can you post a copy of GLSAuthServer.config. If you are using the native version it should be in ~/.LotROLinux, if you are using the standalone version I think it lives in the .wine directory (or whatever you have called the WINEPREFIX) in the directory drive_c/users/<your username>/Application Data/PyLotRO (if you have it set as WinXP, if it is Win2000 I think there is a profile directory under the windows directory).

The problem is the games proper launcher would tell you to link the game account to a user account but until I find out what it does and to a greater extent how it does it there isn't much I can do.

**PyLotRO 0.1.14**

Just a couple of tweaks:
- News feed now uses correct CSS file (should display quicker)
- New error [E14], if you see this you need to login to your user account at Turbine/Codemasters and associate your game account with your user account

DEBs are being built, source files up, standalone EXE being built but won't be available till late morning US time (need Codeweavers staff to swap the pointer for me).

### Post by Ocye on 2010-11-06
> I'm trying to run LOTRO, but I get no in-game sound.
For me padsp wrapper helped. And there are a lot options in winecfg about sound...

### Post by wylfing on 2010-11-06
My final solution was to switch to a wine build that includes actual support for Pulseaudio ([linky]("http://www.webupd8.org/2010/05/install-wine-with-built-in-pulseaudio.html")). I used the c-korn PPA.

You'll have to uninstall wine, remove the official wine PPA, and then re-install from c-korn. After that, run winecfg and pick Pulseaudio from the Audio tab. All sound works perfectly for me now, including in-game voice chat.

---

### Post by SNy on 2010-11-20
> **Ocye said:**
> Some parameters seems to be changed with f2p. 

lotrolaucher fails with:

```
Connecting to world login queue for realm [DE-RP] Belegaer...
http://lotroeugls.com/login/login_queue.php%0Ahttp://lotroeugls.com/GLS.AuthServer/LoginQueue.aspx:
2010-11-05 12:34:56 FEHLER 400: Bad Request.
```

Pylotro says *** Finished *** but game is not started.

Any suggestion what to do?

PS: With pylotro and updated wine/dx9 game starts well.

Yeah, the guys over at Codemasters changed over to Turbines new login components and simply commented the old URI out (in the XML file the launcher downloads). The script does not parse XML but rather just slices it up and uses the pieces it finds, hence ending up with both parts old and new, delimited by a newline ("%0A").

I refined the script, also put (finally) the queue looping in that Steelsnake sent in way back and did some other minor cleaning up.
Will upload it shortly, needed to get my box working again first which did not like me disconnecting it from the power over my holidays at all and failed to boot. NIC/MoBo and SSD seem fubared and I can only boot it right now with keyboard/network and monitor cables detached. What a mess.

SNy

---

### Post by SNy on 2010-11-24
I just put up the new version of the CLI launcher, now ready for F2P, to my site (see the sig).

The error guys above this posting are getting is that the launchers expected active subscriptions, as without, you were not able to play before F2P. The check for the subscription status "Active" will obviously not work for free accounts.

### Post by SNy on 2010-11-26
> **Fuzzybair said:**
> I was Looking into SNy CLI launcher in the GLSAuth() function i pulled the wget call and ran it with out the -q option and received the following output (...)

That is indeed something I could have mentioned. Had this set to -nv earlier, which is some diagnostics but since I don't need them, had this set to -q and did keep it in the uploaded script.

So, to anyone having problems, do try it with wget set to verbose mode (-v) or not-quite-so-verbose mode (-nv) instead of quiet mode (-q) so you can see what is happening. 

Anyway, regarding the US lotro access, I just tried it here with the 1.0rc1 version of the script and my F2P account and it worked like a charm. Did you try this when there was some kind of maintenance or somesuch?

SNy

### Post by Fuzzybair on 2010-11-27
I found the solution. i did a packet trace to compare on windows. the windows client truncates the password at 16 characters. an update to the CLI and PyLotro to reflect this behavior would be nice.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2010-11-28
> **Fuzzybair said:**
> I found the solution. i did a packet trace to compare on windows. the windows client truncates the password at 16 characters. an update to the CLI and PyLotro to reflect this behavior would be nice.

Unless Turbine state that the password can't be more than 16 characters long that is abysmally poor programming, not to mention making a mockery out of strong password strength. Well it is Turbine I guess.

---

### Post by SNy on 2010-11-28
> **Fuzzybair said:**
> I found the solution. i did a packet trace to compare on windows. the windows client truncates the password at 16 characters. an update to the CLI and PyLotro to reflect this behavior would be nice.

Wow, now that you write this, I remember a discussion in the German section of Codemasters' LOTRO forum that went a bit into this direction: [link to page 30 of the thread (German!)]("http://community.codemasters.com/forum/allgemeine-diskussionen-zum-spiel-544/445474-traurig-aber-wahr-geklaute-accountdaten-und-die-folgen-30.html")

What they said (amongst other stuff, this was OT to the thread) is that the forum permits logins with different passwords, turns out only a number of 8 characters are treated as relevant, the rest get truncated.
Now, that is Codemasters and all, but you never know. I agree with ajackson that this kind of password handling is very poor but I'm not sure we can do something to a) get a definitive answer on that out of Turbine b) let alone get them to change it.

As a workaround for now, you could simply use ${pass:0:16} in the script on line 204, which just uses 16 characters beginning with the first.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by SNy on 2010-11-29
> **mbrundage said:**
> Got the problem fixed by copying the clientpatch.dll file from the backup directory to the maine LotRO directory.  Patching fine now.

Ah, this might be caused by the new patchclient.dll, which needs the newer C runtime libraries. If you run my CLI launcher and tell it to patch, it will most likely output unsatisfied linker errors.

Try installing the appropriate libraries with winetricks (the name escapes me right now, so you need to look yourself, sorry) because sooner or later the patchclient will be overwritten again with the new version.

ibd, I don't know what you mean with 'blank' window, exactly. If the launcher doesn't return you and it seems your box is doing something, it could be that the game actually runs, minimized into the lower left corner. Try using the /explorer setting found commented in the current CLI script. It's what I use, otherwise I cannot alt-tab without the game minimizing itself. You can usually just double-click it and it will come up, but it is annoying.

SNy

---
