### Post by SNy on 2010-12-08
> **ibd said:**
> Btw: If I connect with the CLI launcher the last output is: 
Connecting to world login queue for realm "..."...
Ready. Now starting the client...

And then the window disappears immediately :-/

ibd, as you can see in previous postings (ie #2345 by thom_raindog and #2361 by myself), most people that had problems with the game not starting at all without diagnostic messages needed to install d3dx9 via winetricks.

Since you said you did that (I assume you do start the game with the same wine prefix you installed d3dx9 into), there was another posting regarding sound configuration that also helped resolve a silent crash (#2348).

Since you say your crash/problem is different due to missing "finished" (I don't use pylotro so I can't comment on what that usually means), you might want to try ways that might output something.

I would therefore suggest you try the CLI launcher in a terminal you open up yourself (so it stays open after executing the script) and re-enable the wine debug output (comment the line that exports WINEDEBUG). All of the settings you can and probably should tweak are in the SETUP section at the very top of the script and a comment or two explain what they are supposed to be doing.

HTH,
SNy

### Post by ajackson on 2010-12-13
> **Needhelpforgaming said:**
> Wine 1.2.1
You are better off using the latest version of Wine, see the sticky at the top of this forum. Games are directx heavy, each release of wine improves how directx performs.

> **Needhelpforgaming said:**
> So I copied the already installed game file from an external drive that had been used as a backup around early October.
The trouble with a copy install is that some of the needed stuff is not installed. Using winetricks (in the repositories) install Visual C++ Run-times 2005 and 2008, you might need directx modern as well.

> **Needhelpforgaming said:**
> There are no "instructions" to follow when I select the Patch option. 
What exactly do you see when you click patch, if it just goes straight to finished then that indicates that patching failed completely, PyLotRO uses the games own patcher, which in turn needs the visual C++ run-times mentioned earlier.

Edit: There are no instructions to follow for patching because there doesn't need to be, the trouble comes when people install in an odd way or Turbine break something with an update.

### Post by ajackson on 2010-12-14
Quoting directly from [** Before asking for help with Wine << PLEASE READ BEFORE POSTING **]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=885111")

> Use the proprietary drivers for your video card - many graphics issues in Wine are due to missing features in the free drivers for various video cards. Try enabling the proprietary drivers for your video card, if available, using System->Administration->Additional Drivers.

> Things to include when asking for help:

   4. Hardware specs/drivers - Name your video card and say whether you're using the proprietary or stock drivers. lspci on a terminal can help identify the chipset if needed (it'll show up as VGA compatible controller, display adapter, or something similar).

The stickies are there for a reason.

I fired up pylotro and patched the game (took about an hour and a half), then tried to log in.  I'm getting these errors:

err:module:import_dll Library d3dx9_42.dll (which is needed by L"C:\\Program Files\\Turbine\\The Lord of the Rings Online\\lotroclient.exe") not found

err:module:LdrInitializeThunk Main exe initialization for L"C:\\Program Files\\Turbine\\The Lord of the Rings Online\\lotroclient.exe" failed, status c0000135


I looked back through this thread, and saw that someone with the same issues resolved them first by installing vcrun2005 and vcrun2005sp1.  I installed vcrun2005 via winetricks with no problems, but wasn't given a vcrun2005sp1.  However, the description for vcrun2005 states "MS Visual C++ 2005 sp1 libraries....", so maybe sp1 is included in vcrun2005 now?
Also, the directx dll issue...  I copied d3dx9_42.dll over from another game folder into the lotro game folder.

### Post by ajackson on 2011-03-26
> **TruFaith said:**
> Ok can you give me the best way to wipe my .wine directory? Sorry still new in this area.

Open up a terminal window, that should place you in your home directory.

You can view hidden directories using
```
ls -a
```
the -a is for view all, one of those should be .wine

To completely remove that use
```
 rm -rf .wine
```
The -rf mean apply recursively (ie get rid of sub-directories) and the f part of it forces a "yes" response if for some reason you were going to be asked an "are you sure?" or "delete xxx?" style question.

To view hidden folders in Nautilus (the default file browser in Ubuntu) press Ctrl-H to toggle hidden directories, again you should see .wine and you can delete it that way if you don't fancy using a console window.

### Post by wylfing on 2011-04-05
I've been looking at that page from time to time, but it doesn't provide much insight into what works right now and what doesn't.

LotRO tests at least three things: whether you're running Vista or higher, whether you've got DX10 or DX11 installed, and whether your video card is capable of DX10 or DX11. If you simply set wine to Vista, LotRO launches normally but immediately falls back to DX9 mode and reports there's no DX10/DX11 support present. So wine itself doesn't meet the requirements (yet).

I tried installing the Russian DX10-for-XP hack, but that produces only screen garbage. My next experiment will be to set a clean wine bottle to Vista and "upgrade" it with the DX11 distributable. I know installing DirectX like that isn't supposed to work, but it does have some non-zero effect, and I want to see what it is.

Relatedly, in case anyone is wondering, I also recently ran the game under Windows 7 with DX10 and everything cranked up super-high. (My video card doesn't do DX11.) I was kind of underwhelmed. I mean, it's better, but not very much. Wine with DX9 does a damn good job running this game.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2011-05-05
The strange grid screen is because there are now 10 (I think) loading screens but for reasons known only to your patcher it didn't bother to get them during a patch. It's not game breaking but they are raw/languagecode/logo directory and have names lotro_generic_teleport_screen_nn.jpg when nn is between 01 and 10, if you have both an en and en_gb create them in both (copy the lotro_ad_pregame*.jpg file and see if a patch gets them updated).

The patche seems to require VCRun2003,2005 & 2008 depending on how you installed, wine version etc for 2003 you just need MSVCR71.dll, stick it in the game directory and it should work.

### Post by ajackson on 2011-06-02
**EU migration to NA**

Finally managed to get this done (their servers are taking a hammering). These are the steps I followed:

Go to [https://transfer.lotro.com/](https://transfer.lotro.com/) and follow the instructions, it is pretty simple but the site may display the maintenance if demand gets too high.

That gets your account on the queue to be moved, to sort out your client:

Start PyLotRO
Start the patcher
When patching has finished close PyLotRO
Go to your LoTRO directory (ie where the game client is)
From the TEMP folder move anything in the APPLYFAST folder to the game directory (in my case it was TurbineInvoker.exe and TurbineLauncher.exe.config)

When you now start PyLotRO you should see the big list of servers but might get an error about the news feed. I do have a fix for that but I'm waiting to see if it is just a temporary glitch and I'm also holding it back in case something else needs a tweak, it shouldn't do as everyone is now in effect at the same state as the US client was pre-merging.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2011-06-02
Well I have found one snag.

If you previous had the EN GB version (should have client_local_EN_GB.dat in game folder) this needs replacing with the standard English one (client_local_English.dat).

Now Turbine's launcher does this and then patches it but I can't see how it managed to do it. The file is pretty big 0.5GB so sticking somewhere like fileserve would be tricky.

---

### Post by ajackson on 2011-06-02
OK pretty easy solution to the EN GB problem, it's a kludge and ugly but who cares it works :)

- Before starting PyLotRO open your game directory in a file browser (command line will do).
- Create a file called client_local_English.dat (case sensitive but can be zero length file)
- Start PyLotRO
- Ensure language is set to English and not English (UK)
- Delete the client_local_English.dat you have created
- Start the patcher, it gets a 3MB file and then patches it up to it's full size so it will take a while
- Exit PyLotRO
- Remove/rename client_local_EN_GB.dat

---

### Post by ajackson on 2011-06-04
In the game directory there should be a temp directory and under that an applylast (not fast, that was a typo :)). Failing that there may be a Patch directory. The key file to find is TurbineLauncher.exe.config it should be in one of those directories.

Patching errors (the 0x8192003) is caused by one (or more of):
- no VCRun2008 installed
- files with datx in the game directory
- files with zero length in the forward directory

The datx files cause Turbine's launcher to choke as well but deleting them or moving/renaming them gets it working again.

Once you pull the TurbineLauncher.exe.config into the game directory you need to restart PyLotRO as it reads that on start-up and then re-patch to get the last parts down.

All I can say is that I had to copy the files out of applylast, restart pylotro and re-patch to get LotRO working again.

---
